text.NRSV: Adam, Seth, Enosh;
text.Rotherham: Adam, Seth, Enosh:

text.NRSV: Kenan, Mahalalel, Jared;
text.Rotherham: Kenan, Mahalalel, Jared:

text.NRSV: Enoch, Methuselah, Lamech;
text.Rotherham: Enoch, Methuselah, Lamech:

text.NRSV: Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text.Rotherham: Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

text.NRSV: The sons of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Japheth, Gomer, and Magog, and Madai, and Javan, and Tubal, and Meshech and Tiras.

text.NRSV: The sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Diphath, and Togarmah.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Gomer, Ashkenaz, and Diphath, and Togarmah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Rodanim.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Javan, Elishah, and Tarshish,—Kittim, and Rodanim.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ham: Cush, Egypt, Put, and Canaan.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Ham, Cush, and Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.

text.NRSV: The sons of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabta, Raamah, and Sabteca. The sons of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Cush, Seba, and Havilah, and Sabta, and Raama, and Sabteca. And, the sons of Raamah, Sheba, and Dedan.

text.NRSV: Cush became the father of Nimrod; he was the first to be a mighty one on the earth.
text.Rotherham: And, Cush, begat Nimrod,—he, began to be a mighty one in the earth.

text.NRSV: Egypt became the father of Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naphtuhim,
text.Rotherham: And, Mizraim, begat Ludim, and Anamim, and Lehabim, and Naphtuhim,

text.NRSV: Pathrusim, Casluhim (from whom the Philistines come), and Caphtorim.
text.Rotherham: and Pathrusim, and Casluhim—whence went forth the Philistines, and Caphtorim.

text.NRSV: Canaan became the father of Sidon his firstborn, and Heth,
text.Rotherham: And, Canaan, begat Zidon his firstborn, and Heth;

text.NRSV: and the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,
text.Rotherham: and the Jebusite, and the Amorite, and the Girgashite;

text.NRSV: the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,
text.Rotherham: and the Hivite, and the Arkite, and the Sinite;

text.NRSV: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites.
text.Rotherham: and the Arvadite, and the Zemarite, and the Hamathite.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, Aram, Uz, Hul, Gether, and Meshech.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Shem, Elam, and Asshur, and Arpachshad, and Lud, and Aram,—and Uz, and Hul, and Gethur, and Meshech.

text.NRSV: Arpachshad became the father of Shelah; and Shelah became the father of Eber.
text.Rotherham: And, Arpachshad, begat Shelah,—and, Shelah, begat Eber.

text.NRSV: To Eber were born two sons: the name of the one was Peleg (for in his days the earth was divided), and the name of his brother Joktan.
text.Rotherham: And, unto Eber, were born two sons,—the name of the one, was Peleg, for, in his days, was the earth divided, and, the name of his brother, was Joktan.

text.NRSV: Joktan became the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
text.Rotherham: And, Joktan, begat Almodad, and Sheleph,—and Hazarmaveth, and Jerah;

text.NRSV: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
text.Rotherham: and Hadoram, and Uzal, and Diklah;

text.NRSV: Ebal, Abimael, Sheba,
text.Rotherham: and Ebal, and Abimael, and Sheba;

text.NRSV: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab; all these were the sons of Joktan.
text.Rotherham: and Ophir, and Havilah, and Jobab. All these, were the sons of Joktan.

text.NRSV: Shem, Arpachshad, Shelah;
text.Rotherham: Shem, Arpachshad, Shelah;

text.NRSV: Eber, Peleg, Reu;
text.Rotherham: Eber, Peleg, Reu;

text.NRSV: Serug, Nahor, Terah;
text.Rotherham: Serug, Nahor, Terah;

text.NRSV: Abram, that is, Abraham.
text.Rotherham: Abram—the same, is Abraham.

text.NRSV: The sons of Abraham: Isaac and Ishmael.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Abraham, Isaac and Ishmael.

text.NRSV: These are their genealogies: the firstborn of Ishmael, Nebaioth; and Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
text.Rotherham: These, are their generations,—the firstborn of Ishmael, Nebaioth, then Kedar, and Adbeel, and Mibsam;

text.NRSV: Mishma, Dumah, Massa, Hadad, Tema,
text.Rotherham: Mishma, and Dumah, Massa, Hadad, and Tema;

text.NRSV: Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah. These are the sons of Ishmael.
text.Rotherham: Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah. These, are the sons of Ishmael.

text.NRSV: The sons of Keturah, Abraham’s concubine: she bore Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah. The sons of Jokshan: Sheba and Dedan.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Keturah, the concubine of Abraham, she bare Zimran, and Jokshan, and Medan, and Midian, and Ishbak, and Shuah,—and, the sons of Jokshan, Sheba, and Dedan;

text.NRSV: The sons of Midian: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the descendants of Keturah.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Midian, Ephah, and Epher, and Hanoch, and Abida, and Eldaah. All these, were the sons of Keturah.

text.NRSV: Abraham became the father of Isaac. The sons of Isaac: Esau and Israel.
text.Rotherham: So then Abraham begat Isaac,—the sons of Isaac, were Esau and Israel.

text.NRSV: The sons of Esau: Eliphaz, Reuel, Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Esau, Eliphaz, Reuel, and Jeush, and Jalam, and Korah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Eliphaz: Teman, Omar, Zephi, Gatam, Kenaz, Timna, and Amalek.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Eliphaz, Teman, and Omar, Zephi, and Gatam, Kenaz, and Timna, and Amalek.

text.NRSV: The sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Reuel, Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Seir: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Seir, Lotan, and Shobal, and Zibeon, and Anah,—and Dishon, and Ezer, and Dishan.

text.NRSV: The sons of Lotan: Hori and Homam; and Lotan’s sister was Timna.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Lotan, Hori, and Homam,—and, Lotan’s sister, was Timna.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shobal: Alian, Manahath, Ebal, Shephi, and Onam. The sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Shobal, Alian, and Manahath, and Ebal, Shephi and Onam. And, the sons of Zibeon, Aiah and Anah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Anah: Dishon. The sons of Dishon: Hamran, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Anah, Dishon,—and, the sons of Dishon, Hamran, and Eshban, and Ithran, and Cheran.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Jaakan. The sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Ezer, Bilhan, and Zaavan, Jaakan. The sons of Dishan, Uz and Aran.

text.NRSV: These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before any king reigned over the Israelites: Bela son of Beor, the name of whose city was Dinhabah.
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the kings, who reigned in the land of Edom, before there reigned a king of the sons of Israel,—Bela the son of Beor, and, the name of his city, was Dinhabah.

text.NRSV: When Bela died, Jobab son of Zerah of Bozrah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Bela died,—and there reigned in his stead, Jobab son of Zerah, of Bozrah.

text.NRSV: When Jobab died, Husham of the land of the Temanites succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Jobab died,—and there reigned in his stead, Husham, of the land of the Temanites.

text.NRSV: When Husham died, Hadad son of Bedad, who defeated Midian in the country of Moab, succeeded him; the name of his city was Avith.
text.Rotherham: And Husham died,—and there reigned in his stead, Hadad the son of Bedad, who smote Midian in the field of Moab, and, the name of his city, was Avith.

text.NRSV: When Hadad died, Samlah of Masrekah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Hadad died,—and there reigned in his stead, Samlah of Masrekah.

text.NRSV: When Samlah died, Shaul of Rehoboth on the Euphrates succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Samlah died,—and there reigned in his stead, Shaul, of Rehoboth by the River.

text.NRSV: When Shaul died, Baal-hanan son of Achbor succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Shaul died,—and there reigned in his stead, Baal-hanan, the son of Achbor.

text.NRSV: When Baal-hanan died, Hadad succeeded him; the name of his city was Pai, and his wife’s name Mehetabel daughter of Matred, daughter of Mezahab.
text.Rotherham: And Baal-hanan died,—and there reigned in his stead, Hadad, and, the name of his city, was Pai,—and, the name of his wife, was Mehetabel, daughter of Matred, daughter of Mezahab.

text.NRSV: And Hadad died. The clans of Edom were: clans Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
text.Rotherham: And Hadad died. And there were chiefs of Edom: chief Timna, chief Aliah, chief Jetheth;

text.NRSV: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
text.Rotherham: chief Oholibamah, chief Elah, chief Pinon;

text.NRSV: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
text.Rotherham: chief Kenaz, chief Teman, chief Mibzar;

text.NRSV: Magdiel, and Iram; these are the clans of Edom.
text.Rotherham: chief Magdiel, chief Iram. These, were the chiefs of Edom.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Israel: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun,
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Israel,—Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun;

text.NRSV: Dan, Joseph, Benjamin, Naphtali, Gad, and Asher.
text.Rotherham: Dan, Joseph, and Benjamin, Naphtali, Gad, and Asher,

text.NRSV: The sons of Judah: Er, Onan, and Shelah; these three Bath-shua the Canaanite bore to him. Now Er, Judah’s firstborn, was wicked in the sight of the Lord, and he put him to death.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Judah, Er, and Onan, and Shelah, the three born to him of the daughter of Shua the Canaanitess,—but Er the firstborn of Judah became wicked in the eyes of Yahweh, and he slew him.

text.NRSV: His daughter-in-law Tamar also bore him Perez and Zerah. Judah had five sons in all.
text.Rotherham: And, Tamar his daughter-in-law, bare him, Perez and Zerah. All the sons of Judah, were five.

text.NRSV: The sons of Perez: Hezron and Hamul.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Perez, Hezron and Hamul.

text.NRSV: The sons of Zerah: Zimri, Ethan, Heman, Calcol, and Dara, five in all.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Zerah, Zimri and Ethan, and Heman and Calcol and Dara, all of them, five.

text.NRSV: The sons of Carmi: Achar, the troubler of Israel, who transgressed in the matter of the devoted thing;
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Carmi, Achar, the troubler of Israel, who transgressed in a thing devoted.

text.NRSV: and Ethan’s son was Azariah.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Ethan, Azariah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Hezron, who were born to him: Jerahmeel, Ram, and Chelubai.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Hezron, who were born to him, Jerahmeel, and Ram, and Calubai.

text.NRSV: Ram became the father of Amminadab, and Amminadab became the father of Nahshon, prince of the sons of Judah.
text.Rotherham: And, Ram, begat Amminadab,—and, Amminadab, begat Nahshon, leader of the sons of Judah.

text.NRSV: Nahshon became the father of Salma, Salma became the father of Boaz,
text.Rotherham: And, Nahshon, begat Salma, and, Salma, begat Boaz;

text.NRSV: Boaz became the father of Obed, Obed became the father of Jesse.
text.Rotherham: and, Boaz, begat Obed, and, Obed, begat Jesse;

text.NRSV: Jesse became the father of Eliab his firstborn, Abinadab the second, Shimea the third,
text.Rotherham: and, Jesse, begat—his firstborn, Eliab,—and Abinadab, the second, and Shimea, the third;

text.NRSV: Nethanel the fourth, Raddai the fifth,
text.Rotherham: Nethanel, the fourth, Raddai, the fifth;

text.NRSV: Ozem the sixth, David the seventh;
text.Rotherham: Ozem, the sixth, David, the seventh.

text.NRSV: and their sisters were Zeruiah and Abigail. The sons of Zeruiah: Abishai, Joab, and Asahel, three.
text.Rotherham: And, their sisters, were Zeruiah and Abigail,—and, the sons of Zeruiah, Abishai and Joab and Asahel, three,

text.NRSV: Abigail bore Amasa, and the father of Amasa was Jether the Ishmaelite.
text.Rotherham: And, Abigail, bare Amasa,—and, the father of Amasa, was Jether the Ishmaelite.

text.NRSV: Caleb son of Hezron had children by his wife Azubah, and by Jerioth; these were her sons: Jesher, Shobab, and Ardon.
text.Rotherham: And, Caleb son of Hezron, begat children of Azubah his wife, and of Jerioth,—and, these, were her sons, Jesher and Shobab and Ardon.

text.NRSV: When Azubah died, Caleb married Ephrath, who bore him Hur.
text.Rotherham: And Azubah died,—and Caleb took unto him Ephrath, and she bare to him Hur.

text.NRSV: Hur became the father of Uri, and Uri became the father of Bezalel.
text.Rotherham: And, Hur, begat Uri, and, Uri, begat Bezalel.

text.NRSV: Afterward Hezron went in to the daughter of Machir father of Gilead, whom he married when he was sixty years old; and she bore him Segub.
text.Rotherham: And, afterward, Hezron went in unto the daughter of Machir, father of Gilead, and he took her when he was sixty years old,—and she bare him Segub.

text.NRSV: And Segub became the father of Jair, who had twenty-three towns in the land of Gilead.
text.Rotherham: And, Segub, begat Jair,—who came to have twenty-three cities, in the land of Gilead;

text.NRSV: But Geshur and Aram took from them Havvoth-jair, Kenath and its villages, sixty towns. All these were descendants of Machir, father of Gilead.
text.Rotherham: but Geshur and Aram took the towns of Jair from them, with Kenath and the villages thereof, sixty cities. All these, were the sons of Machir, father of Gilead.

text.NRSV: After the death of Hezron, in Caleb-ephrathah, Abijah wife of Hezron bore him Ashhur, father of Tekoa.
text.Rotherham: And, after the death of Hezron, Caleb entered Ephrathah,—and, the wife of Hezron, was Abiah, who bare him Ashur, father of Tekoa.

text.NRSV: The sons of Jerahmeel, the firstborn of Hezron: Ram his firstborn, Bunah, Oren, Ozem, and Ahijah.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Jerahmeel firstborn of Hezron were, the firstborn, Ram,—and Bunah, and Oren, and Ozem [of] Ahijah.

text.NRSV: Jerahmeel also had another wife, whose name was Atarah; she was the mother of Onam.
text.Rotherham: And Jerahmeel had another wife, whose name, was Atarah,—the same, was the mother of Onam.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ram, the firstborn of Jerahmeel: Maaz, Jamin, and Eker.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Ram, the firstborn of Jerameel, were,—Maaz and Jamin, and Eker.

text.NRSV: The sons of Onam: Shammai and Jada. The sons of Shammai: Nadab and Abishur.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Onam were, Shammai and Jada,—and, the sons of Shammai, Nadab and Abishur.

text.NRSV: The name of Abishur’s wife was Abihail, and she bore him Ahban and Molid.
text.Rotherham: And, the name of the wife of Abishur, was Abihail,—and she bare him Ahban, and Molid.

text.NRSV: The sons of Nadab: Seled and Appaim; and Seled died childless.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Nadab, Seled and Appaim,—but Seled died without sons.

text.NRSV: The son of Appaim: Ishi. The son of Ishi: Sheshan. The son of Sheshan: Ahlai.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Appaim, Ishi, and, the sons of Ishi, Sheshan, and, the sons of Sheshan, Ahlai.

text.NRSV: The sons of Jada, Shammai’s brother: Jether and Jonathan; and Jether died childless.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Jada, brother of Shammai, Jether, and Jonathan,—but Jether died without sons.

text.NRSV: The sons of Jonathan: Peleth and Zaza. These were the descendants of Jerahmeel.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Jonathan, Peleth and Zaza. These, were the sons of Jerahmeel.

text.NRSV: Now Sheshan had no sons, only daughters; but Sheshan had an Egyptian slave, whose name was Jarha.
text.Rotherham: Now Sheshan had, no sons, but, daughters,—and, Sheshan, had a servant, an Egyptian, whose name was Jarha;

text.NRSV: So Sheshan gave his daughter in marriage to his slave Jarha; and she bore him Attai.
text.Rotherham: so Sheshan gave his daughter to Jarha his servant, to wife,—and she bare him Attai.

text.NRSV: Attai became the father of Nathan, and Nathan became the father of Zabad.
text.Rotherham: And, Attai, begat Nathan, and, Nathan, begat Zabad;

text.NRSV: Zabad became the father of Ephlal, and Ephlal became the father of Obed.
text.Rotherham: and, Zabad, begat Ephlal, and, Ephlal, begat Obed;

text.NRSV: Obed became the father of Jehu, and Jehu became the father of Azariah.
text.Rotherham: and, Obed, begat Jehu, and, Jehu, begat Azariah;

text.NRSV: Azariah became the father of Helez, and Helez became the father of Eleasah.
text.Rotherham: and, Azariah, begat Helez, and, Helez, begat Eleasah;

text.NRSV: Eleasah became the father of Sismai, and Sismai became the father of Shallum.
text.Rotherham: and, Eleasah, begat Sismai, and, Sismai, begat Shallum;

text.NRSV: Shallum became the father of Jekamiah, and Jekamiah became the father of Elishama.
text.Rotherham: and, Shallum, begat Jekamiah, and, Jekamiah, begat Elishama.

text.NRSV: The sons of Caleb brother of Jerahmeel: Mesha his firstborn, who was the father of Ziph. The sons of Mareshah father of Hebron.
text.Rotherham: Now, the sons of Caleb, brother of Jerahmeel, were, Mesha his firstborn, the same, was the father of Ziph,—and the sons of Mareshah the father of Hebron.

text.NRSV: The sons of Hebron: Korah, Tappuah, Rekem, and Shema.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Hebron, Korah and Tappuah, and Rekem and Shema.

text.NRSV: Shema became the father of Raham, father of Jorkeam; and Rekem became the father of Shammai.
text.Rotherham: And, Shema, begat Raham, the father of Jorkeam,—and, Rekem, begat Shammai.

text.NRSV: The son of Shammai: Maon; and Maon was the father of Beth-zur.
text.Rotherham: And, the son of Shammai, was Maon,—and, Maon, was the father of Beth-zur.

text.NRSV: Ephah also, Caleb’s concubine, bore Haran, Moza, and Gazez; and Haran became the father of Gazez.
text.Rotherham: And, Ephah, the concubine of Caleb, bare Haran and Moza, and Gazez,—and, Haran, begat Gazez.

text.NRSV: The sons of Jahdai: Regem, Jotham, Geshan, Pelet, Ephah, and Shaaph.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Jahdai, Regem and Jotham and Geshan and Pelet, and Ephah and Shaaph.

text.NRSV: Maacah, Caleb’s concubine, bore Sheber and Tirhanah.
text.Rotherham: A concubine of Caleb, Maacah, bare Sheber, and Tirhanah;

text.NRSV: She also bore Shaaph father of Madmannah, Sheva father of Machbenah and father of Gibea; and the daughter of Caleb was Achsah.
text.Rotherham: she also bare Shaaph, father of Madmannah, Sheva, father of Machbena, and father of Gibea,—and, the daughter of Caleb, was Achsah.

text.NRSV: These were the descendants of Caleb. The sons of Hur the firstborn of Ephrathah: Shobal father of Kiriath-jearim,
text.Rotherham: These, were the sons of Caleb, sons of Hur, firstborn of Ephrathah,—Shobal, the father of Kiriath-jearim;

text.NRSV: Salma father of Bethlehem, and Hareph father of Beth-gader.
text.Rotherham: Salma, the father of Bethlehem, Hareph, the father of Beth-gader.

text.NRSV: Shobal father of Kiriath-jearim had other descendants: Haroeh, half of the Menuhoth.
text.Rotherham: And Shobal, the father of Kiriath-jearim had sons,—Haroeh, half of the Menuhoth.

text.NRSV: And the families of Kiriath-jearim: the Ithrites, the Puthites, the Shumathites, and the Mishraites; from these came the Zorathites and the Eshtaolites.
text.Rotherham: And, the families of Kiriath-jearim, were the Ithrites, and the Puthites, and the Shumathites, and the Mishraites,—from these, came the Zorathites, and the Eshtaolites.

text.NRSV: The sons of Salma: Bethlehem, the Netophathites, Atroth-beth-joab, and half of the Manahathites, the Zorites.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Salma, were Bethlehem, and the Netophathites, Atrothbeth-joab,—and half of the Manahathites, the Zorites;

text.NRSV: The families also of the scribes that lived at Jabez: the Tirathites, the Shimeathites, and the Sucathites. These are the Kenites who came from Hammath, father of the house of Rechab.
text.Rotherham: and the families of scribes who dwelt at Jabez, the Tirathites, the Shimeathites, the Sucathites. The same, are the Kenites who came in from Hammath, father of the house of Rechab.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of David who were born to him in Hebron: the firstborn Amnon, by Ahinoam the Jezreelite; the second Daniel, by Abigail the Carmelite;
text.Rotherham: Now, these, were the sons of David, who were born to him in Hebron,—the firstborn, Amnon, by Ahinoam the Jezreelitess, the second, Daniel, by Abigail the Carmelitess;

text.NRSV: the third Absalom son of Maacah, daughter of King Talmai of Geshur; the fourth Adonijah son of Haggith;
text.Rotherham: the third, Absalom, son of Maacah, daughter of Talmai, king of Geshur,—the fourth, Adonijah, son of Haggith;

text.NRSV: the fifth Shephatiah, by Abital; the sixth Ithream, by his wife Eglah;
text.Rotherham: the fifth, Shephatiah, by Abital,—the sixth, Ithream, by Eglah his wife:

text.NRSV: six were born to him in Hebron, where he reigned for seven years and six months. And he reigned thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: six born to him in Hebron, and he reigned there, seven years and six months. And, thirty and three years, reigned he, in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: These were born to him in Jerusalem: Shimea, Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon, four by Bath-shua, daughter of Ammiel;
text.Rotherham: And, these, were born to him in Jerusalem, Shimea, and Shobab, and Nathan, and Solomon—four, by Bathshua, daughter of Ammiel;

text.NRSV: then Ibhar, Elishama, Eliphelet,
text.Rotherham: and Ibhar, and Elishama, and Eliphelet,

text.NRSV: Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia,
text.Rotherham: and Nogah, and Nepheg, and Japhia,

text.NRSV: Elishama, Eliada, and Eliphelet, nine.
text.Rotherham: and Elishama, and Eliada, and Eliphelet—nine.

text.NRSV: All these were David’s sons, besides the sons of the concubines; and Tamar was their sister.
text.Rotherham: All, sons of David,—besides sons of concubines, and, Tamar, their sister.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Solomon: Rehoboam, Abijah his son, Asa his son, Jehoshaphat his son,
text.Rotherham: And, the son of Solomon, was Rehoboam,—Abijah his son, Asa his son, Jehoshaphat his son;

text.NRSV: Joram his son, Ahaziah his son, Joash his son,
text.Rotherham: Joram his son, Ahaziah his son, Joash his son;

text.NRSV: Amaziah his son, Azariah his son, Jotham his son,
text.Rotherham: Amaziah his son, Azariah his son, Jotham his son;

text.NRSV: Ahaz his son, Hezekiah his son, Manasseh his son,
text.Rotherham: Ahaz his son, Hezekiah his son, Manasseh his son;

text.NRSV: Amon his son, Josiah his son.
text.Rotherham: Amon his son, Josiah his son.

text.NRSV: The sons of Josiah: Johanan the firstborn, the second Jehoiakim, the third Zedekiah, the fourth Shallum.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Josiah, the firstborn, Johanan, the second, Jehoiakim,—the third, Zedekiah, the fourth, Shallum.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Jehoiakim: Jeconiah his son, Zedekiah his son;
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Jehoiakim,—Jeconiah his son, Zedekiah his son.

text.NRSV: and the sons of Jeconiah, the captive: Shealtiel his son,
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Jeconiah the captive, Shealtiel his son;

text.NRSV: Malchiram, Pedaiah, Shenazzar, Jekamiah, Hoshama, and Nedabiah;
text.Rotherham: and Malchiram, and Pedaiah, and Shenazzar,—Jekamiah, Hoshama, and Nedabiah.

text.NRSV: the sons of Pedaiah: Zerubbabel and Shimei; and the sons of Zerubbabel: Meshullam and Hananiah, and Shelomith was their sister;
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Pedaiah, Zerubbabel, and Shimei,—and, the sons of Zerubbabel, Meshullam, and Hananiah, and, Shelomith, was their sister;

text.NRSV: and Hashubah, Ohel, Berechiah, Hasadiah, and Jushab-hesed, five.
text.Rotherham: and Hashubah, and Ohel, and Berechiah, and Hasadiah, Jushab-hesed—five.

text.NRSV: The sons of Hananiah: Pelatiah and Jeshaiah, his son Rephaiah, his son Arnan, his son Obadiah, his son Shecaniah.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Hananiah, Pelatiah, and Jeshaiah,—the sons of Rephaiah, the sons of Arnan, the sons of Obadiah, the sons of Shecaniah;

text.NRSV: The son of Shecaniah: Shemaiah. And the sons of Shemaiah: Hattush, Igal, Bariah, Neariah, and Shaphat, six.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Shecaniah, Shemaiah,—and, the sons of Shemaiah, Hattush, and Igal, and Bariah, and Neariah, and Shaphat—six;

text.NRSV: The sons of Neariah: Elioenai, Hizkiah, and Azrikam, three.
text.Rotherham: and, the son of Neariah, Elioenai, and Hizkiah, and Azrikam—three;

text.NRSV: The sons of Elioenai: Hodaviah, Eliashib, Pelaiah, Akkub, Johanan, Delaiah, and Anani, seven.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Elioenai, Hodaviah, and Eliashib, and Pelaiah, and Akkub, and Johanan, and Delaiah, and Anani—seven.

text.NRSV: The sons of Judah: Perez, Hezron, Carmi, Hur, and Shobal.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Judah, Perez, Hezron, and Carmi, and Hur, and Shobal.

text.NRSV: Reaiah son of Shobal became the father of Jahath, and Jahath became the father of Ahumai and Lahad. These were the families of the Zorathites.
text.Rotherham: And, Reaiah son of Shobal, begat Jahath, and, Jahath, begat Ahumai, and Lahad. These, are the families of the Zorathites.

text.NRSV: These were the sons of Etam: Jezreel, Ishma, and Idbash; and the name of their sister was Hazzelelponi,
text.Rotherham: And, these, were the sons of Etam, Jezreel and Ishma, and Idbash,—and, the name of their sister, was Hazzelelponi;

text.NRSV: and Penuel was the father of Gedor, and Ezer the father of Hushah. These were the sons of Hur, the firstborn of Ephrathah, the father of Bethlehem.
text.Rotherham: and Penuel the father of Gedor, and Ezer, the father of Hushah. These, are the sons of Hur, the firstborn of Ephrathah, the father of Bethlehem.

text.NRSV: Ashhur father of Tekoa had two wives, Helah and Naarah;
text.Rotherham: And, Ashhur the father of Tekoa, had two wives,—Helah, and Naarah.

text.NRSV: Naarah bore him Ahuzzam, Hepher, Temeni, and Haahashtari. These were the sons of Naarah.
text.Rotherham: And Naarah bare him Ahuzzam and Hepher, and Temeni, and Haahashtari. These, were the sons of Naarah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Helah: Zereth, Izhar, and Ethnan.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Helah, were Zereth, Izhar, and Ethnan.

text.NRSV: Koz became the father of Anub, Zobebah, and the families of Aharhel son of Harum.
text.Rotherham: And, Koz, begat Anub, and Zobebah,—and the families of Aharhel, the son of Harum.

text.NRSV: Jabez was honored more than his brothers; and his mother named him Jabez, saying, “Because I bore him in pain.”
text.Rotherham: Now it came to pass that Jabez was more honourable than his brethren,—but, his mother, had called his name Jabez, [="he causes pain"] saying, Because I bare him with pain.

text.NRSV: Jabez called on the God of Israel, saying, “Oh that you would bless me and enlarge my border, and that your hand might be with me, and that you would keep me from hurt and harm!” And God granted what he asked.
text.Rotherham: So then Jabez called on the God of Israel, saying, Oh that thou wouldst, indeed bless, me, and enlarge my boundary, and that thy hand might be with me,—and that thou wouldst work [to deliver me] from evil, that it be not my pain. And God brought about that which he asked.

text.NRSV: Chelub the brother of Shuhah became the father of Mehir, who was the father of Eshton.
text.Rotherham: And, Chelub the brother of Shuhah, begat Mehir, the same, was the father of Eshton.

text.NRSV: Eshton became the father of Beth-rapha, Paseah, and Tehinnah the father of Ir-nahash. These are the men of Recah.
text.Rotherham: And, Eshton, begat Beth-rapha, and Paseah, and Tehinnah, the father of Ir-nahash. These, are the men of Recah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kenaz: Othniel and Seraiah; and the sons of Othniel: Hathath and Meonothai.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Kenaz, were Othniel, and Seraiah,—and, the sons of Othniel, Hathath.

text.NRSV: Meonothai became the father of Ophrah; and Seraiah became the father of Joab father of Ge-harashim, so-called because they were artisans.
text.Rotherham: And, Meonothai, begat Ophrah,—and, Seraiah, begat Joab, the father of Ge-harashim, for they were, craftsmen.

text.NRSV: The sons of Caleb son of Jephunneh: Iru, Elah, and Naam; and the son of Elah: Kenaz.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Caleb son of Jephunneh, were Iru, Elah, and Naam,—and the sons of Elah and Kenaz.

text.NRSV: The sons of Jehallelel: Ziph, Ziphah, Tiria, and Asarel.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Jehallelel, Ziph and Ziphah, Tiria, and Asarel.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ezrah: Jether, Mered, Epher, and Jalon. These are the sons of Bithiah, daughter of Pharaoh, whom Mered married; and she conceived and bore Miriam, Shammai, and Ishbah father of Eshtemoa.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Ezrah, Jether and Mered, and Epher and Jalon. And, these, are the sons of Bithia, daughter of Pharaoh, whom Mered took,—and she conceived and bare Miriam and Shammai, and Ishbah, the father of Eshtemoa.

text.NRSV: And his Judean wife bore Jered father of Gedor, Heber father of Soco, and Jekuthiel father of Zanoah.
text.Rotherham: And, his wife, the Jewess, bare Jered the father of Gedor, and Heber the father of Soco, and Jekuthiel, the father of Zanoah.

text.NRSV: The sons of the wife of Hodiah, the sister of Naham, were the fathers of Keilah the Garmite and Eshtemoa the Maacathite.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of the wife of Hodiah, the sister of Naham, were the father of Keilah, the Garmite,—and Eshtemoa, the Maacathite.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shimon: Amnon, Rinnah, Ben-hanan, and Tilon. The sons of Ishi: Zoheth and Ben-zoheth.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Shimon, Amnon, and Rinnah, Ben-hanan and Tilon. And, the sons of Ishi, Zoheth, and Ben-zoheth.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shelah son of Judah: Er father of Lecah, Laadah father of Mareshah, and the families of the guild of linen workers at Beth-ashbea;
text.Rotherham: the sons of Shelah, the son of Judah, Er, the father of Lecah, and Laadah, the father of Mareshah,—and the families of the house of them that wrought fine linen, of the house of Ashbea;

text.NRSV: and Jokim, and the men of Cozeba, and Joash, and Saraph, who married into Moab and returned to Lehem (now the records are ancient).
text.Rotherham: and Jokim, and the men of Cozeba, and Joash, and Saraph who ruled for Moab, and Jashubi-lehem,—but, the records, are ancient.

text.NRSV: These were the potters and inhabitants of Netaim and Gederah; they lived there in the king’s service.
text.Rotherham: They, were the potters and the inhabitants of Netaim, and Gederah,—with the king in his work, dwelt they there.

text.NRSV: The sons of Simeon: Nemuel, Jamin, Jarib, Zerah, Shaul;
text.Rotherham: The sons of Simeon, Nemuel, and Jamin, Jarib, Zerah, Shaul:

text.NRSV: Shallum was his son, Mibsam his son, Mishma his son.
text.Rotherham: Shallum his son, Mibsam his son, Mishma his son.

text.NRSV: The sons of Mishma: Hammuel his son, Zaccur his son, Shimei his son.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Mishma, Hammuel his son, Zaccur his son, Shimei his son.

text.NRSV: Shimei had sixteen sons and six daughters; but his brothers did not have many children, nor did all their family multiply like the Judeans.
text.Rotherham: And, Shimei, had sixteen sons, and six daughters, but, his brethren, had not many children,—nor did, any of their family, multiply so much as the sons of Judah.

text.NRSV: They lived in Beer-sheba, Moladah, Hazar-shual,
text.Rotherham: And they dwelt in Beer-sheba, and Moladah, and Hazar-shual;

text.NRSV: Bilhah, Ezem, Tolad,
text.Rotherham: and in Bilhah, and in Ezem, and in Tolad;

text.NRSV: Bethuel, Hormah, Ziklag,
text.Rotherham: and in Bethuel, and in Hormah, and in Ziklag;

text.NRSV: Beth-marcaboth, Hazar-susim, Beth-biri, and Shaaraim. These were their towns until David became king.
text.Rotherham: and in Beth-marcaboth, and in Hazar-susim, and in Beth-biri, and in Shaaraim. These, were their cities unto the reign of David.

text.NRSV: And their villages were Etam, Ain, Rimmon, Tochen, and Ashan, five towns,
text.Rotherham: And, their villages, were Etam, and Ain, Rimmon, and Tochen, and Ashan,—five cities;

text.NRSV: along with all their villages that were around these towns as far as Baal. These were their settlements. And they kept a genealogical record.
text.Rotherham: and all their villages that were round about these cities, as far as Baal. These, were their habitations, and they had their own genealogical register.

text.NRSV: Meshobab, Jamlech, Joshah son of Amaziah,
text.Rotherham: And Meshobab, and Jamlech, and Joshah, the son of Amaziah;

text.NRSV: Joel, Jehu son of Joshibiah son of Seraiah son of Asiel,
text.Rotherham: and Joel,—and Jehu, the son of Joshibiah, the son of Seraiah, the son of Asiel:

text.NRSV: Elioenai, Jaakobah, Jeshohaiah, Asaiah, Adiel, Jesimiel, Benaiah,
text.Rotherham: and Elioenai, and Jaakobah, and Jeshohaiah, and Asaiah, and Adiel, and Jesimiel, and Benaiah:

text.NRSV: Ziza son of Shiphi son of Allon son of Jedaiah son of Shimri son of Shemaiah—
text.Rotherham: and Ziza, son of Shiphi, son of Allon, son of Jedaiah, son of Shimri, son of Shemaiah.

text.NRSV: these mentioned by name were princes in their families, and their ancestral houses increased greatly.
text.Rotherham: These, introduced by their names, were leading men in their families. And, their ancestral house, brake forth exceedingly;

text.NRSV: They journeyed to the entrance of Gedor, to the east side of the valley, to seek pasture for their flocks,
text.Rotherham: so they went to the entering in of Gerar, unto the east of the valley,—to seek pasture for their flocks;

text.NRSV: where they found rich, good pasture, and the land was very broad, quiet, and peaceful; for the former inhabitants there belonged to Ham.
text.Rotherham: and they found pasture, fat and good, and, the land, was broad on both hands, and quiet, and secure,—for, of Ham, were the dwellers there aforetime.

text.NRSV: These, registered by name, came in the days of King Hezekiah of Judah, and attacked their tents and the Meunites who were found there, and exterminated them to this day, and settled in their place, because there was pasture there for their flocks.
text.Rotherham: But these written by name came in the days of Hezekiah king of Judah, and smote their tents, and the Meunim who were found there, and devoted them, until this day, and dwelt in their stead,—for there was pasture for their flocks, there.

text.NRSV: And some of them, five hundred men of the Simeonites, went to Mount Seir, having as their leaders Pelatiah, Neariah, Rephaiah, and Uzziel, sons of Ishi;
text.Rotherham: And, some of them, of the sons of Simeon, went to Mount Seir, five hundred men,—with Pelatiah, and Neariah, and Rephaiah, and Uzziel, sons of Ishi, at their head;

text.NRSV: they destroyed the remnant of the Amalekites that had escaped, and they have lived there to this day.
text.Rotherham: and they smote the remainder that had escaped, of the Amalekites,—and dwelt there—[as they have] unto this day.

text.NRSV: The sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel. (He was the firstborn, but because he defiled his father’s bed his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph son of Israel, so that he is not enrolled in the genealogy according to the birthright;
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel, for, he, was the firstborn, but because he defiled the couch of his father, his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph son of Israel,—and he is not to be enrolled in the place of firstborn,

text.NRSV: though Judah became prominent among his brothers and a ruler came from him, yet the birthright belonged to Joseph.)
text.Rotherham: for, Judah, prevailed over his brethren, so that, even the prince, is from him,—although, the birthright, pertaineth to Joseph;

text.NRSV: The sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel, Hanoch and Pallu, Hezron and Carmi.

text.NRSV: The sons of Joel: Shemaiah his son, Gog his son, Shimei his son,
text.Rotherham: The sons of Joel, Shemaiah his son, Gog his son, Shimei his son;

text.NRSV: Micah his son, Reaiah his son, Baal his son,
text.Rotherham: Micah his son, Reaiah his son, Baal his son;

text.NRSV: Beerah his son, whom King Tilgath-pilneser of Assyria carried away into exile; he was a pioneer of the Reubenites.
text.Rotherham: Beerah his son, whom Tilgath-pilneser king of Assyria carried away captive,—he, was a leader to the Reubenites;

text.NRSV: And his kindred by their families, when the genealogy of their generations was reckoned: the chief, Jeiel, and Zechariah,
text.Rotherham: and his brethren, by his families, in the genealogical registering, by their generations,—the chief Jeiel, and Zechariah;

text.NRSV: and Bela son of Azaz, son of Shema, son of Joel, who lived in Aroer, as far as Nebo and Baal-meon.
text.Rotherham: and Bela, the son of Azaz, the son of Shema, the son of Joel,—he, dwelt in Aroer, even as far as Nebo, and Baal-meon;

text.NRSV: He also lived to the east as far as the beginning of the desert this side of the Euphrates, because their cattle had multiplied in the land of Gilead.
text.Rotherham: and, eastward, dwelt he as far as the entering in of the desert, from the river Euphrates,—because, their cattle, were multiplied in the land of Gilead;

text.NRSV: And in the days of Saul they made war on the Hagrites, who fell by their hand; and they lived in their tents throughout all the region east of Gilead.
text.Rotherham: and, in the days of Saul, they made war with the Hagrites, who fell by their hand,—so they dwelt in their tents, over all the face of the land east of Gilead.

text.NRSV: The sons of Gad lived beside them in the land of Bashan as far as Salecah:
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Gad, over against them, dwelt, in the land of Bashan, as far as Salecah:

text.NRSV: Joel the chief, Shapham the second, Janai, and Shaphat in Bashan.
text.Rotherham: Joel the chief, and Shapham, the second,—and Janai and Shaphat, in Bashan;

text.NRSV: And their kindred according to their ancestral houses: Michael, Meshullam, Sheba, Jorai, Jacan, Zia, and Eber, seven.
text.Rotherham: and their brethren of their ancestral house, Michael and Meshullam and Sheba and Jorai and Jacan and Zia and Eber, seven.

text.NRSV: These were the sons of Abihail son of Huri, son of Jaroah, son of Gilead, son of Michael, son of Jeshishai, son of Jahdo, son of Buz;
text.Rotherham: These, were the sons of Abihail, son of Huri, son of Jaroah, son of Gilead, son of Michael, son of Jehishai, son of Jahdo, son of Buz,

text.NRSV: Ahi son of Abdiel, son of Guni, was chief in their ancestral houses;
text.Rotherham: Ahi son of Abdiel, son of Guni, chief of their ancestral house;

text.NRSV: and they lived in Gilead, in Bashan and its towns, and in all the pasture lands of Sharon to their limits.
text.Rotherham: and they dwelt in Gilead—in Bashan, and in her villages,—and in all the pasture-lands of Sharon, up to their outgoings.

text.NRSV: All of these were enrolled by genealogies in the days of King Jotham of Judah, and in the days of King Jeroboam of Israel.
text.Rotherham: All of them, were genealogically registered in the days of Jotham king of Judah,—and in the days of Jeroboam king of Israel.

text.NRSV: The Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh had valiant warriors, who carried shield and sword, and drew the bow, expert in war, forty-four thousand seven hundred sixty, ready for service.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Reuben and Gad, and half tribe of Manasseh, of the sons of valour, men bearing shield and sword and bending the bow, and instructed in war, were forty-four thousand and seven hundred and sixty, ready to go forth in the host.

text.NRSV: They made war on the Hagrites, Jetur, Naphish, and Nodab;
text.Rotherham: So they made war with the Hagrites,—and Jetur and Naphish, and Nodab;

text.NRSV: and when they received help against them, the Hagrites and all who were with them were delivered into their hands, for they cried to God in the battle, and he granted their entreaty because they trusted in him.
text.Rotherham: and were helped against them, and the Hagrites and all that were with them were delivered into their hand,—for, unto God, made they outcry, in the war, and he suffered himself to be entreated by them, because they put their trust in him;

text.NRSV: They captured their livestock: fifty thousand of their camels, two hundred fifty thousand sheep, two thousand donkeys, and one hundred thousand captives.
text.Rotherham: and they captured their cattle—their camels, fifty thousand, and, flocks, two hundred and fifty thousand, and, asses, two thousand,—and, persons of men, a hundred thousand;

text.NRSV: Many were slain, because the war was of God. And they lived in their territory until the exile.
text.Rotherham: for, many slain, fell, for, of God, was the war,—and they dwelt in their stead, until the captivity.

text.NRSV: The members of the half-tribe of Manasseh lived in the land; they were very numerous from Bashan to Baal-hermon, Senir, and Mount Hermon.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of the half tribe of Manasseh, dwelt in the land,—from Bashan, as far as to Baal-hermon and Senir and Mount Hermon, they, were multiplied.

text.NRSV: These were the heads of their ancestral houses: Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, and Jahdiel, mighty warriors, famous men, heads of their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: And, these, were the heads of their ancestral house,—Epher and Ishi and Eliel and Azriel and Jeremiah and Hodaviah and Jahdiel, men who were heroes of valour, men of renown, chiefs to their ancestral house.

text.NRSV: But they were unfaithful to the God of their ancestors, and prostituted themselves to the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God had destroyed before them.
text.Rotherham: But they dealt unfaithfully with the God of their fathers,—and went unchastely after the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God destroyed from before them;

text.NRSV: So the God of Israel stirred up the spirit of King Pul of Assyria, the spirit of King Tilgath-pilneser of Assyria, and he carried them away, namely, the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh, and brought them to Halah, Habor, Hara, and the river Gozan, to this day.
text.Rotherham: so the God of Israel stirred up the spirit of Pul king of Assyria, and the spirit of Tilgath-pilneser king of Assyria, and he took them away captive, even the Reubenites and the Gadites and the half tribe of Manasseh,—and brought them in unto Halah, and Habor, and Hara, and to the river of Gozan, unto this day.

text.NRSV: The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Levi, Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Kohath, Amram, Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel.

text.NRSV: The children of Amram: Aaron, Moses, and Miriam. The sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: And, the children of Amram, Aaron, and Moses, and Miriam,—and, the sons of Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

text.NRSV: Eleazar became the father of Phinehas, Phinehas became the father of Abishua,
text.Rotherham: Eleazar, begat Phinehas, Phinehas, begat Abishua;

text.NRSV: Abishua became the father of Bukki, Bukki became the father of Uzzi,
text.Rotherham: and, Abishua, begat Bukki, and, Bukki, begat Uzzi;

text.NRSV: Uzzi became the father of Zerahiah, Zerahiah became the father of Meraioth,
text.Rotherham: and, Uzzi, begat Zerahiah, and, Zerahiah, begat Meraioth;

text.NRSV: Meraioth became the father of Amariah, Amariah became the father of Ahitub,
text.Rotherham: Meraioth, begat Amariah, and, Amariah, begat Ahitub;

text.NRSV: Ahitub became the father of Zadok, Zadok became the father of Ahimaaz,
text.Rotherham: and, Ahitub, begat Zadok, and, Zadok, begat Ahimaaz;

text.NRSV: Ahimaaz became the father of Azariah, Azariah became the father of Johanan,
text.Rotherham: and, Ahimaaz, begat Azariah, and, Azariah, begat Johanan,

text.NRSV: and Johanan became the father of Azariah (it was he who served as priest in the house that Solomon built in Jerusalem).
text.Rotherham: and, Johanan, begat Azariah,—he, it was who ministered as priest, in the house which Solomon built in Jerusalem;

text.NRSV: Azariah became the father of Amariah, Amariah became the father of Ahitub,
text.Rotherham: and Azariah begat Amariah,—and, Amariah, begat Ahitub;

text.NRSV: Ahitub became the father of Zadok, Zadok became the father of Shallum,
text.Rotherham: and, Ahitub, begat Zadok, and, Zadok, begat Shallum;

text.NRSV: Shallum became the father of Hilkiah, Hilkiah became the father of Azariah,
text.Rotherham: and, Shallum, begat Hilkiah, and Hilkiah, begat Azariah;

text.NRSV: Azariah became the father of Seraiah, Seraiah became the father of Jehozadak;
text.Rotherham: and, Azariah, begat Seraiah, and, Seraiah, begat Jehozadak;

text.NRSV: and Jehozadak went into exile when the Lord sent Judah and Jerusalem into exile by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar.
text.Rotherham: and, Jehozadak, departed, when Yahweh carried away Judah and Jerusalem into captivity,—by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar.

text.NRSV: The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Levi, Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the sons of Gershom: Libni and Shimei.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the names of the sons of Gershom, Libni and Shimei.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Kohath, were Amram, and Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Merari, Mahli and Mushi. And, these, are the families of Levi by their fathers:

text.NRSV: Of Gershom: Libni his son, Jahath his son, Zimmah his son,
text.Rotherham: To Gershom, pertained Libni his son, Jahath his son, Zimmah his son,

text.NRSV: Joah his son, Iddo his son, Zerah his son, Jeatherai his son.
text.Rotherham: Joah his son, Iddo his son, Zerah his son, Jeatherai his son.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kohath: Amminadab his son, Korah his son, Assir his son,
text.Rotherham: The sons of Kohath, Amminadab his son, Korah his son, Assir his son;

text.NRSV: Elkanah his son, Ebiasaph his son, Assir his son,
text.Rotherham: Elkanah his son, and Ebiasaph his son, and Assir his son;

text.NRSV: Tahath his son, Uriel his son, Uzziah his son, and Shaul his son.
text.Rotherham: Tahath his son, Uriel his son, Uzziah his son, and Shaul his son.

text.NRSV: The sons of Elkanah: Amasai and Ahimoth,
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Elkanah, Amasai, and Ahimoth.

text.NRSV: Elkanah his son, Zophai his son, Nahath his son,
text.Rotherham: As for Elkanah, the sons of Elkanah, Zophai his son, and Nahath his son;

text.NRSV: Eliab his son, Jeroham his son, Elkanah his son.
text.Rotherham: Eliab his son, Jeroham his son, Elkanah his son.

text.NRSV: The sons of Samuel: Joel his firstborn, the second Abijah.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Samuel, the firstborn, [Joel], and, the second, Abiah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: Mahli, Libni his son, Shimei his son, Uzzah his son,
text.Rotherham: the sons of Merari, Mahli,—Libni his son, Shimei his son, Uzzah his son;

text.NRSV: Shimea his son, Haggiah his son, and Asaiah his son.
text.Rotherham: Shimea his son, Haggiah his son, Asaiah his son.

text.NRSV: These are the men whom David put in charge of the service of song in the house of the Lord, after the ark rested there.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are they whom David caused to stand, as the servants of song, in the house of Yahweh, when he had given rest unto the ark;

text.NRSV: They ministered with song before the tabernacle of the tent of meeting, until Solomon had built the house of the Lord in Jerusalem; and they performed their service in due order.
text.Rotherham: so they became attendants before the habitation of the tent of meeting, with song, until Solomon built the house of Yahweh, in Jerusalem,—and they took their stand according to their prescribed manner over their work.

text.NRSV: These are the men who served; and their sons: Of the Kohathites: Heman, the singer, son of Joel, son of Samuel,
text.Rotherham: Yea, these, are they who stood, with their sons. Of the sons of the Kohathites, Heman the singer, son of Joel, son of Samuel,

text.NRSV: son of Elkanah, son of Jeroham, son of Eliel, son of Toah,
text.Rotherham: son of Elkanah, son of Jeroham, son of Eliel, son of Toah,—

text.NRSV: son of Zuph, son of Elkanah, son of Mahath, son of Amasai,
text.Rotherham: son of Zuph, son of Elkanah, son of Mahath, son of Amasai:—

text.NRSV: son of Elkanah, son of Joel, son of Azariah, son of Zephaniah,
text.Rotherham: son of Elkanah, son of Joel, son of Azariah, son of Zephaniah,—

text.NRSV: son of Tahath, son of Assir, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah,
text.Rotherham: son of Tahath, son of Assir, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah;—

text.NRSV: son of Izhar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, son of Israel;
text.Rotherham: son of Izhar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, son of Israel.

text.NRSV: and his brother Asaph, who stood on his right, namely, Asaph son of Berechiah, son of Shimea,
text.Rotherham: And, his brother Asaph who stood on his right hand, Asaph son of Berechiah, son of Shimea,—

text.NRSV: son of Michael, son of Baaseiah, son of Malchijah,
text.Rotherham: son of Michael, son of Baaseiah, son of Malchijah,—

text.NRSV: son of Ethni, son of Zerah, son of Adaiah,
text.Rotherham: son of Ethni, son of Zerah, son of Adaiah;—

text.NRSV: son of Ethan, son of Zimmah, son of Shimei,
text.Rotherham: son of Ethan, son of Zimmah, son of Shimei,—

text.NRSV: son of Jahath, son of Gershom, son of Levi.
text.Rotherham: son of Jahath, son of Gershom, son of Levi.

text.NRSV: On their left were their kindred the sons of Merari: Ethan son of Kishi, son of Abdi, son of Malluch,
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Merari, their brethren, on the left hand, Ethan son of Kishi, son of Abdi, son of Malluch;—

text.NRSV: son of Hashabiah, son of Amaziah, son of Hilkiah,
text.Rotherham: son of Hashabiah, son of Amaziah, son of Hilkiah,—

text.NRSV: son of Amzi, son of Bani, son of Shemer,
text.Rotherham: son of Amzi, son of Bani, son of Shemer;—

text.NRSV: son of Mahli, son of Mushi, son of Merari, son of Levi;
text.Rotherham: son of Mahli, son of Mushi, son of Merari, son of Levi.

text.NRSV: and their kindred the Levites were appointed for all the service of the tabernacle of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, their brethren, the Levites, were given, for all the work of the habitation, of the house of God.

text.NRSV: But Aaron and his sons made offerings on the altar of burnt offering and on the altar of incense, performing all the work of the most holy place, to make atonement for Israel, according to all that Moses the servant of God had commanded.
text.Rotherham: But, Aaron and his sons, were to make perfume upon the altar of ascending-sacrifice, and upon the altar of incense, according to all the service of the holy of holies,—even for putting a propitiatory-covering over Israel, according go all that, Moses, the servant of God, commanded.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Aaron: Eleazar his son, Phinehas his son, Abishua his son,
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the sons of Aaron,—Eleazar his son, Phinehas his son, Abishua his son,—

text.NRSV: Bukki his son, Uzzi his son, Zerahiah his son,
text.Rotherham: Bukki his son, Uzzi his son, Zerahiah his son,—

text.NRSV: Meraioth his son, Amariah his son, Ahitub his son,
text.Rotherham: Meraioth his son, Amariah his son, Ahitub his son,—

text.NRSV: Zadok his son, Ahimaaz his son.
text.Rotherham: Zadok his son, Ahimaaz his son.

text.NRSV: These are their dwelling places according to their settlements within their borders: to the sons of Aaron of the families of Kohathites—for the lot fell to them first—
text.Rotherham: And, these, are their dwelling-places, according to their encampments, in their boundary,—pertaining to the sons of Aaron, of the families of the Kohathites, for, theirs, was the lot:

text.NRSV: to them they gave Hebron in the land of Judah and its surrounding pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: so they gave them Hebron, in the land of Judah, and the pasture lands thereof, round about it;

text.NRSV: but the fields of the city and its villages they gave to Caleb son of Jephunneh.
text.Rotherham: but, the field-land of the city, and the villages thereof, gave they to Caleb son of Jephunneh.

text.NRSV: To the sons of Aaron they gave the cities of refuge: Hebron, Libnah with its pasture lands, Jattir, Eshtemoa with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: And, to the sons of Aaron, gave they, the cities of refuge—Hebron, and Libnah, with her pasture lands,—and Jattir and Eshtemoa, with her pasture lands;

text.NRSV: Hilen with its pasture lands, Debir with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: and Hilen with her pasture lands, Debir, with her pasture lands;

text.NRSV: Ashan with its pasture lands, and Beth-shemesh with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Ashan, with her pasture lands, and Bethshemesh, with her pasture lands;

text.NRSV: From the tribe of Benjamin, Geba with its pasture lands, Alemeth with its pasture lands, and Anathoth with its pasture lands. All their towns throughout their families were thirteen.
text.Rotherham: and, out of the tribe of Benjamin, Geba, with her pasture lands, and Allemeth, with her pasture lands,—and Anathoth, with her pasture lands;—all their cities, were thirteen cities, throughout their families.

text.NRSV: To the rest of the Kohathites were given by lot out of the family of the tribe, out of the half-tribe, the half of Manasseh, ten towns.
text.Rotherham: And, unto the rest of the sons of Kohath—of the families of the tribe of Ephraim and of the tribe of Dan, and of the half-tribe of Manasseh, by lot ten cities.

text.NRSV: To the Gershomites according to their families were given thirteen towns out of the tribes of Issachar, Asher, Naphtali, and Manasseh in Bashan.
text.Rotherham: And, to the sons of Gershom, by their families—out of the tribe of Issachar, and out of the tribe of Asher, and of the tribe of Naphtali, and out of the tribe of Manasseh in Bashan, thirteen cities.

text.NRSV: To the Merarites according to their families were given by lot twelve towns out of the tribes of Reuben, Gad, and Zebulun.
text.Rotherham: And, unto sons of Merari, by their families—out of the tribe of Reuben, and out of the tribe of Gad, and out of the tribe of Zebulun, by lot twelve cities.

text.NRSV: So the people of Israel gave the Levites the towns with their pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: So then the sons of Israel gave, to the Levites,—the cities and the pasture lands thereof;

text.NRSV: They also gave them by lot out of the tribes of Judah, Simeon, and Benjamin these towns that are mentioned by name.
text.Rotherham: yea they gave, by lot, out of the tribe of the sons of Judah, and out of the tribe of the sons of Simeon, and out of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, these cities which are mentioned by name.

text.NRSV: And some of the families of the sons of Kohath had towns of their territory out of the tribe of Ephraim.
text.Rotherham: But, as for some of the families of the sons of Kohath, they had the cities of their lot, out of the tribe of Ephraim;

text.NRSV: They were given the cities of refuge: Shechem with its pasture lands in the hill country of Ephraim, Gezer with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: and they gave unto them the cities of refuge—Shechem and her pasture lands, in the hill country of Ephraim,—and Gezer, with her pasture lands;

text.NRSV: Jokmeam with its pasture lands, Beth-horon with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: and Jokmeam, with her pasture lands, and Beth-heron, with her pasture lands;

text.NRSV: Aijalon with its pasture lands, Gath-rimmon with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Aijalon, with her pasture lands, and Gath-rimmon, with her pasture lands;

text.NRSV: And out of the half-tribe of Manasseh, Aner with its pasture lands, and Bileam with its pasture lands, for the rest of the families of the Kohathites.
text.Rotherham: and, out of the half-tribe of Manasseh, Aner, with her pasture lands, and Bileam, with her pasture lands,—for the rest of the family of the sons of Kohath.

text.NRSV: To the Gershomites: out of the half-tribe of Manasseh: Golan in Bashan with its pasture lands and Ashtaroth with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: Unto the sons of Gershom—out of the family of the half tribe of Manasseh, Golan in Bashan, with her pasture lands,—and Ashtaroth, with her pasture lands.

text.NRSV: Out of the tribe of Issachar: Kedesh with its pasture lands, Daberath with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: And, out of the tribe of Issachar, Kedesh, with her pasture lands,—Daberath, with her pasture lands;

text.NRSV: Ramoth with its pasture lands, and Anem with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Ramoth, with her pasture lands, and Anem, with her pasture lands.

text.NRSV: Out of the tribe of Asher: Mashal with its pasture lands, Abdon with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: And, out of the tribe of Asher, Mashal, with her pasture lands,—and Abdon, with her pasture lands;

text.NRSV: Hukok with its pasture lands, and Rehob with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Hukok, with her pasture lands,—and Rehob, with her pasture lands.

text.NRSV: Out of the tribe of Naphtali: Kedesh in Galilee with its pasture lands, Hammon with its pasture lands, and Kiriathaim with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: And, out of the tribe of Naphtali, Kedesh in Galilee, with her pasture lands, and Hammon, with her pasture lands,—and Kiryathaim, with her pasture lands.

text.NRSV: To the rest of the Merarites: out of the tribe of Zebulun: Rimmono with its pasture lands, Tabor with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: Unto the rest of the sons of Merari—out of the tribe of Zebulun, Rimmono, with her pasture lands,—Tabor, with her pasture lands.

text.NRSV: Across the Jordan from Jericho, on the east side of the Jordan, out of the tribe of Reuben: Bezer in the steppe with its pasture lands, Jahzah with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: And, beyond the Jordan at Jericho, on the east of the Jordan—out of the tribe of Reuben, Bezer in the wilderness, with her pasture lands,—and Jahzah, with her pasture lands;

text.NRSV: Kedemoth with its pasture lands, and Mephaath with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Kedemoth, with her pasture lands, and Mephaath, with her pasture lands.

text.NRSV: And out of the tribe of Gad: Ramoth in Gilead with its pasture lands, Mahanaim with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: And, out of the tribe of Gad, Ramoth in Gilead, with her pasture lands,—and Mahanaim, with her pasture lands;

text.NRSV: Heshbon with its pasture lands, and Jazer with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Heshbon, with her pasture lands, and Jazer, with her pasture lands.

text.NRSV: The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron, four.
text.Rotherham: And, to the sons of Issachar, belonged, Tola and Push, Jashib and Shimron, four.

text.NRSV: The sons of Tola: Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Ibsam, and Shemuel, heads of their ancestral houses, namely of Tola, mighty warriors of their generations, their number in the days of David being twenty-two thousand six hundred.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Tola, were Uzzi and Rephaiah and Jeriel and Jahmai and Ibsam and Shemuel, chiefs of their ancestral house, pertaining to Tola, heroes of valour, in their generations,—their number, in the days of David, twenty-two thousand and six hundred.

text.NRSV: The son of Uzzi: Izrahiah. And the sons of Izrahiah: Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah, five, all of them chiefs;
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Uzzi, Izrahiah,—and, the sons of Izrahiah, Michael and Obadiah and Joel, Isshiah—five, chiefs, all of them;

text.NRSV: and along with them, by their generations, according to their ancestral houses, were units of the fighting force, thirty-six thousand, for they had many wives and sons.
text.Rotherham: and, with them by their generations, pertaining to their ancestral house, were bands of a host for war, thirty-six thousand,—for they had many wives and sons;

text.NRSV: Their kindred belonging to all the families of Issachar were in all eighty-seven thousand mighty warriors, enrolled by genealogy.
text.Rotherham: and, their brethren, of all the families of Issachar, heroes of great valour, were eighty-seven thousand, when they had, all, registered themselves.

text.NRSV: The sons of Benjamin: Bela, Becher, and Jediael, three.
text.Rotherham: Benjamin, Bela and Becher and Jediael, three.

text.NRSV: The sons of Bela: Ezbon, Uzzi, Uzziel, Jerimoth, and Iri, five, heads of ancestral houses, mighty warriors; and their enrollment by genealogy was twenty-two thousand thirty-four.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Bela, Ezbon and Uzzi and Uzziel and Jerimoth and Iri, five, chiefs of the ancestral house, heroes of great valour,—who, when they had registered themselves, were twenty-two thousand and thirty-four,

text.NRSV: The sons of Becher: Zemirah, Joash, Eliezer, Elioenai, Omri, Jeremoth, Abijah, Anathoth, and Alemeth. All these were the sons of Becher.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Becher, Zemirah and Joash and Eliezer and Elioenai and Omri, and Jeremoth and Abijah, and Anathoth, and Alemeth. All these, were sons of Becher.

text.NRSV: Their enrollment by genealogy, according to their generations, as heads of their ancestral houses, mighty warriors, was twenty thousand two hundred.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had registered themselves by their generations, the chiefs of their ancestral house, the heroes of valour, were twenty thousand and two hundred.

text.NRSV: The son of Jediael: Bilhan. And the sons of Bilhan: Jeush, Benjamin, Ehud, Chenaanah, Zethan, Tarshish, and Ahishahar.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Jediael, Bilhan,—and, the sons of Bilhan, Jeish and Benjamin and Ehud and Chenaanah, and Zethan, and Tarshish, and Ahishahah.

text.NRSV: All these were the sons of Jediael, according to the heads of their ancestral houses, mighty warriors, seventeen thousand two hundred, ready for service in war.
text.Rotherham: All these—sons of Jediael, by ancestral chiefs, heroes of great valour, were seventeen thousand and two hundred, ready to go forth as a host to war:—

text.NRSV: And Shuppim and Huppim were the sons of Ir, Hushim the son of Aher.
text.Rotherham: Shuppim also and Huppim, sons of Ir, Hushim sons of Aher.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Naphtali: Jahziel, Guni, Jezer, and Shallum, the descendants of Bilhah.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Naphtali, Jahziel and Guni, and Jezer and Shallum, sons of Bilhah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Manasseh: Asriel, whom his Aramean concubine bore; she bore Machir the father of Gilead.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Manasseh, Asriel, whom, [his wife] bare,—his concubine, the Syrian, bare Machir the father of Gilead;

text.NRSV: And Machir took a wife for Huppim and for Shuppim. The name of his sister was Maacah. And the name of the second was Zelophehad; and Zelophehad had daughters.
text.Rotherham: and, Machir, took a wife pertaining to Huppim and Shuppim, and, the name of his sister, was Maacah, and, the name of the second, Zelophehad,—and, Zelophehad, had, daughters.

text.NRSV: Maacah the wife of Machir bore a son, and she named him Peresh; the name of his brother was Sheresh; and his sons were Ulam and Rakem.
text.Rotherham: So then Maacah, wife of Machir, bare a son, and called his name Peresh, and, the name of his brother, was Sheresh,—and, his sons, were Ulam and Rakem;

text.NRSV: The son of Ulam: Bedan. These were the sons of Gilead son of Machir, son of Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Ulam, Bedan. These, were the sons of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh.

text.NRSV: And his sister Hammolecheth bore Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.
text.Rotherham: And, his sister, Hammolecheth, bare Ishhod, and Abiezer, and Mahlah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shemida were Ahian, Shechem, Likhi, and Aniam.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Shemida, were Ahian and Shechem, and Likhi, and Aniam.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ephraim: Shuthelah, and Bered his son, Tahath his son, Eleadah his son, Tahath his son,
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Ephraim, Shuthelah,—and Bered his son, and Tahath his son, and Eleadah his son, and Tahath his son;

text.NRSV: Zabad his son, Shuthelah his son, and Ezer and Elead. Now the people of Gath, who were born in the land, killed them, because they came down to raid their livestock.
text.Rotherham: and Zabad his son and Shuthelah his son, and Ezer and Elead,—but the men of Gath who had been born in the land slew them, because they had come down to take away their cattle.

text.NRSV: And their father Ephraim mourned many days, and his kindred came to comfort him.
text.Rotherham: And Ephraim their father mourned many days,—and his brethren came to comfort him.

text.NRSV: Ephraim went in to his wife, and she conceived and bore a son; and he named him Beriah, because disaster had befallen his house.
text.Rotherham: And he went in unto his wife, and she conceived, and bare a son,—and he called his name, Beriah, because, in misfortune, was she in his house.

text.NRSV: His daughter was Sheerah, who built both Lower and Upper Beth-horon, and Uzzen-sheerah.
text.Rotherham: And, his daughter, was Sheerah, who built Beth-horon the nether and the upper,—and Uzzen-sheerah.

text.NRSV: Rephah was his son, Resheph his son, Telah his son, Tahan his son,
text.Rotherham: And Rephah his son, and Resheph, and Telah his son, and Tahan his son,—

text.NRSV: Ladan his son, Ammihud his son, Elishama his son,
text.Rotherham: Ladan his son, Ammihud his son, Elishama his son,—

text.NRSV: Nun his son, Joshua his son.
text.Rotherham: Non his son, Joshua his son.

text.NRSV: Their possessions and settlements were Bethel and its towns, and to the east Naaran, and to the west Gezer and its towns, Shechem and its towns, as far as Ayyah and its towns;
text.Rotherham: And, their possessions, and their dwellings, were Bethel, and the villages thereof; and, eastward, Naaran, and, westward, Gezer, and the villages thereof, and Shechem, and the villages thereof,—as far as Aiyah, and the villages thereof;—

text.NRSV: also along the borders of the Manassites, Beth-shean and its towns, Taanach and its towns, Megiddo and its towns, Dor and its towns. In these lived the sons of Joseph son of Israel.
text.Rotherham: and, on the sides of the sons of Manasseh, Beth-shean and the villages thereof, Taanach and the villages thereof, Megiddo and the villages thereof, Dor and the villages thereof. In these, dwelt the sons of Joseph, son of Israel.

text.NRSV: The sons of Asher: Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and their sister Serah.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Asher, Imnah, and Ishvah, and Ishvi and Beriah,—and Serah their sister.

text.NRSV: The sons of Beriah: Heber and Malchiel, who was the father of Birzaith.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Beriah, Heber, and Malchiel,—the same, was the father of Birzaith.

text.NRSV: Heber became the father of Japhlet, Shomer, Hotham, and their sister Shua.
text.Rotherham: And, Heber, begat Japhlet, and Shomer, and Hotham,—and, Shua, their sister.

text.NRSV: The sons of Japhlet: Pasach, Bimhal, and Ashvath. These are the sons of Japhlet.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Japhlet, Pasach, and Bimhal, and Ashvath. These, were the sons of Japhlet.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shemer: Ahi, Rohgah, Hubbah, and Aram.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Shemer, Ahi, and Rohgah, Jahbah, and Aram.

text.NRSV: The sons of Helem his brother: Zophah, Imna, Shelesh, and Amal.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Helem, his brother, Zophah, and Imna, and Shelesh and Amal.

text.NRSV: The sons of Zophah: Suah, Harnepher, Shual, Beri, Imrah,
text.Rotherham: The sons of Zophah, Suah and Harnepher, and Shual and Beri, and Imrah,—

text.NRSV: Bezer, Hod, Shamma, Shilshah, Ithran, and Beera.
text.Rotherham: Bezer and Hod, and Shamma and Shilshah, and Ithran and Beera.

text.NRSV: The sons of Jether: Jephunneh, Pispa, and Ara.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Jether, Jephunneh, and Pispa, and Ara.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ulla: Arah, Hanniel, and Rizia.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Ulla, Arah, and Hanniel, and Rizia.

text.NRSV: All these were the sons of Asher, heads of ancestral houses, select mighty warriors, chief of the princes. Their number enrolled by genealogy, for service in war, was twenty-six thousand men.
text.Rotherham: All these, were sons of Asher, chiefs of the ancestral house, choice men, heroes of great valour, chiefs of the leaders,—and, when they registered themselves, in host, for war, the number of the men, was twenty-six thousand.

text.NRSV: Benjamin became the father of Bela his firstborn, Ashbel the second, Aharah the third,
text.Rotherham: And, Benjamin, begat Bela his firstborn,—Ashbel, the second, and Aharah, the third,—

text.NRSV: Nohah the fourth, and Rapha the fifth.
text.Rotherham: Nohah, the fourth, and Rapha, the fifth.

text.NRSV: And Bela had sons: Addar, Gera, Abihud,
text.Rotherham: And Bela had sons—Addar, and Gera, and Abihud,—

text.NRSV: Abishua, Naaman, Ahoah,
text.Rotherham: and Abishua, and Naaman, and Ahoah,—

text.NRSV: Gera, Shephuphan, and Huram.
text.Rotherham: and Gera, and Shephuphan, and Huram.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Ehud (they were heads of ancestral houses of the inhabitants of Geba, and they were carried into exile to Manahath):
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the sons of Ehud,—these, are the ancestral chiefs to the inhabitants of Geba, but they were carried away captive, unto Manahath;

text.NRSV: Naaman, Ahijah, and Gera, that is, Heglam, who became the father of Uzza and Ahihud.
text.Rotherham: and, as for Naaman and Ahijah and Gera, the same, carried them captive,—and he begat Uzza and Ahihud.

text.NRSV: And Shaharaim had sons in the country of Moab after he had sent away his wives Hushim and Baara.
text.Rotherham: And, Shaharaim, begat children in the field of Moab, after he had sent away Hushim and Baara his wives.

text.NRSV: He had sons by his wife Hodesh: Jobab, Zibia, Mesha, Malcam,
text.Rotherham: Then begat he, of Hodesh his wife,—Jobab, and Zibia, and Mesha, and Malcam;

text.NRSV: Jeuz, Sachia, and Mirmah. These were his sons, heads of ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: and Jeuz, and Sachia, and Mirmah,—these, were his sons, ancestral chiefs.

text.NRSV: He also had sons by Hushim: Abitub and Elpaal.
text.Rotherham: And, of Hushim, he begat Abitub, and Elpaal.

text.NRSV: The sons of Elpaal: Eber, Misham, and Shemed, who built Ono and Lod with its towns,
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Elpaal, Eber, and Misham, and Shemed,—he, built Ono, and Lod, and the villages thereof.

text.NRSV: and Beriah and Shema (they were heads of ancestral houses of the inhabitants of Aijalon, who put to flight the inhabitants of Gath);
text.Rotherham: And, as for Beriah and Shema, they, were ancestral chiefs to the inhabitants of Aijalon,—they, put to flight the inhabitants of Gath;

text.NRSV: and Ahio, Shashak, and Jeremoth.
text.Rotherham: and Ahio, Shashak, and Jeremoth,—

text.NRSV: Zebadiah, Arad, Eder,
text.Rotherham: and Zebadiah, and Arad, and Eder,—

text.NRSV: Michael, Ishpah, and Joha were sons of Beriah.
text.Rotherham: and Michael, and Ishpah, and Joha, sons of Beriah,—

text.NRSV: Zebadiah, Meshullam, Hizki, Heber,
text.Rotherham: and Zebadiah, and Meshullam, and Hizki, and Heber,—

text.NRSV: Ishmerai, Izliah, and Jobab were sons of Elpaal.
text.Rotherham: and Ishmerai, and Izliah, and Jobab, sons of Elpaal,—

text.NRSV: Jakim, Zichri, Zabdi,
text.Rotherham: and Jakim, and Zichri, and Zabdi—

text.NRSV: Elienai, Zillethai, Eliel,
text.Rotherham: and Elienai, and Zillethai, and Eliel,—

text.NRSV: Adaiah, Beraiah, and Shimrath were sons of Shimei.
text.Rotherham: and Adaiah, and Beraiah, and Shimrath, sons of Shimei,—

text.NRSV: Ishpan, Eber, Eliel,
text.Rotherham: and Ishpan, and Eber, and Eliel,—

text.NRSV: Abdon, Zichri, Hanan,
text.Rotherham: and Abdon, and Zichri, and Hanan,—

text.NRSV: Hananiah, Elam, Anthothijah,
text.Rotherham: and Hananiah, and Elam, and Anthothijah,—

text.NRSV: Iphdeiah, and Penuel were sons of Shashak.
text.Rotherham: and Iphdeiah, and Penuel, sons of Shashak,—

text.NRSV: Shamsherai, Shehariah, Athaliah,
text.Rotherham: and Shamsherai, and Shehariah, and Athaliah,—

text.NRSV: Jaareshiah, Elijah, and Zichri were sons of Jeroham.
text.Rotherham: and Jaareshiah, and Elijah, and Zichri, sons of Jeroham:

text.NRSV: These were heads of ancestral houses, according to their generations, chiefs. These lived in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: these, were ancestral chiefs to their generations, chief men,—these, dwelt in Jerusalem;

text.NRSV: Jeiel the father of Gibeon lived in Gibeon, and the name of his wife was Maacah.
text.Rotherham: And, in Gibeon, dwelt the father of Gibeon [Jeiel], the name of whose wife, was Maacah,—

text.NRSV: His firstborn son: Abdon, then Zur, Kish, Baal, Nadab,
text.Rotherham: and his firstborn son, Abdon, and Zur and Kish, and Baal and Ner and Nadab,—

text.NRSV: Gedor, Ahio, Zecher,
text.Rotherham: and Gedor, and Ahio, and Zecher.

text.NRSV: and Mikloth, who became the father of Shimeah. Now these also lived opposite their kindred in Jerusalem, with their kindred.
text.Rotherham: And, Mikloth, begat Shimeah,—moreover also, they, over against their brethren, did dwell in Jerusalem, with their own brethren.

text.NRSV: Ner became the father of Kish, Kish became the father of Saul, Saul became the father of Jonathan, Malchi-shua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal;
text.Rotherham: So then, Ner, begat Kish, and, Kish, begat Saul,—and, Saul, begat Jonathan, and Malchishua, and Abinadab, and Eshbaal.

text.NRSV: and the son of Jonathan was Merib-baal; and Merib-baal became the father of Micah.
text.Rotherham: And, the son of Jonathan, was Merib-baal,—and, Merib-baal, begat Micah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Micah: Pithon, Melech, Tarea, and Ahaz.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Micah, were Pithon, and Melech, and Tarea, and Ahaz.

text.NRSV: Ahaz became the father of Jehoaddah; and Jehoaddah became the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri; Zimri became the father of Moza.
text.Rotherham: And, Ahaz, begat Jehoaddah, and, Jehoaddah, begat Alemeth, and Azmaveth, and Zimri,—and, Zimri, begat Moza;

text.NRSV: Moza became the father of Binea; Raphah was his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son.
text.Rotherham: and, Moza, begat Binea,—Raphah was his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son.

text.NRSV: Azel had six sons, and these are their names: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. All these were the sons of Azel.
text.Rotherham: And, Azel, had six sons, and, these, were their names—Azrikam, Bocheru, and Ishmael, and Sheariah, and Obediah, and Hanan and Asah,—all these, were sons of Azel.

text.NRSV: The sons of Eshek his brother: Ulam his firstborn, Jeush the second, and Eliphelet the third.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Eshek his brother, were Ulam his firstborn, Jeush the second, and Eliphelet the third.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ulam were mighty warriors, strong archers, having many sons and grandsons, one hundred fifty. All these were Benjaminites.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Ulam became men that were heroes of valour, archers, with many sons, and sons’ sons, a hundred and fifty. All these, were of the sons of Benjamin.

text.NRSV: So all Israel was enrolled by genealogies; and these are written in the Book of the Kings of Israel. And Judah was taken into exile in Babylon because of their unfaithfulness.
text.Rotherham: And, all Israel, registered themselves, and lo! they are written, in the Book of the Kings of Israel, and, Judah, was carried away captive to Babylon, for their faithlessness.

text.NRSV: Now the first to live again in their possessions in their towns were Israelites, priests, Levites, and temple servants.
text.Rotherham: Now, the first inhabitants, who were in their possessions, in their cities, were Israel, the priests, the Levites, and the Nethinim.

text.NRSV: And some of the people of Judah, Benjamin, Ephraim, and Manasseh lived in Jerusalem:
text.Rotherham: And, in Jerusalem, there dwelt, of the sons of Judah, and of the sons of Benjamin,—and of the sons of Ephraim, and Manasseh:

text.NRSV: Uthai son of Ammihud, son of Omri, son of Imri, son of Bani, from the sons of Perez son of Judah.
text.Rotherham: Uthai son of Ammihud, son of Omri, son of Imri, son of Bani, of the sons of Perez, son of Judah.

text.NRSV: And of the Shilonites: Asaiah the firstborn, and his sons.
text.Rotherham: And, of the Shilonites, Asaiah the firstborn, and his sons.

text.NRSV: Of the sons of Zerah: Jeuel and their kindred, six hundred ninety.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Zerah, Jeuel,—and their brethren, six hundred and ninety.

text.NRSV: Of the Benjaminites: Sallu son of Meshullam, son of Hodaviah, son of Hassenuah,
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Benjamin, Sallu, son of Meshullam, son of Hodaviah, son of Hassenuah;

text.NRSV: Ibneiah son of Jeroham, Elah son of Uzzi, son of Michri, and Meshullam son of Shephatiah, son of Reuel, son of Ibnijah;
text.Rotherham: and Ibneiah, son of Jeroham, and Elah, son of Uzzi, son of Michri,—and Meshullam, son of Shephatiah, son of Reuel, son of Ibnijah;

text.NRSV: and their kindred according to their generations, nine hundred fifty-six. All these were heads of families according to their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: and their brethren, by their generations, nine hundred and fifty-six,—all these men, were ancestral chiefs, to their ancestral house.

text.NRSV: Of the priests: Jedaiah, Jehoiarib, Jachin,
text.Rotherham: And, of the priests, Jedaiah, and Jehoiarib, and Jachin,—

text.NRSV: and Azariah son of Hilkiah, son of Meshullam, son of Zadok, son of Meraioth, son of Ahitub, the chief officer of the house of God;
text.Rotherham: and Azariah son of Hilkiah, son of Meshullam, son of Zadok, son of Meraioth, son of Ahitub, chief ruler of the house of God;

text.NRSV: and Adaiah son of Jeroham, son of Pashhur, son of Malchijah, and Maasai son of Adiel, son of Jahzerah, son of Meshullam, son of Meshillemith, son of Immer;
text.Rotherham: and Adaiah, son of Jeroham, son of Pashhur, son of Malchijah,—and Maasai son of Adiel, son of Jahzerah, son of Meshullam, son of Meshillemith, son of Immer;

text.NRSV: besides their kindred, heads of their ancestral houses, one thousand seven hundred sixty, qualified for the work of the service of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: and their brethren, chief men of their ancestral house, a thousand and seven hundred and sixty,—able men, for the business of the service of the house of God.

text.NRSV: Of the Levites: Shemaiah son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, of the sons of Merari;
text.Rotherham: And, of the Levites, Shemaiah, son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, of the sons of Merari;

text.NRSV: and Bakbakkar, Heresh, Galal, and Mattaniah son of Mica, son of Zichri, son of Asaph;
text.Rotherham: and Bakbakkar, Heresh, and Galal,—and Mattaniah, son of Mica, son of Zichri, son of Asaph;

text.NRSV: and Obadiah son of Shemaiah, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun, and Berechiah son of Asa, son of Elkanah, who lived in the villages of the Netophathites.
text.Rotherham: and Obadiah, son of Shemaiah, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun,—and Berechiah son of Asa, son of Elkanah, who dwelt in the villages of the Netophathites.

text.NRSV: The gatekeepers were: Shallum, Akkub, Talmon, Ahiman, and their kindred (Shallum was the chief),
