text.Rotherham: And, the keepers of the gates, were Shallum, and Akkub, and Talmon, and Ahiman,—and their brethren—Shallum the chief;

text.NRSV: stationed until now at the King’s Gate on the east side. These were the gatekeepers of the camp of the Levites.
text.Rotherham: and, hitherto, they were in the gate of the king, eastward,—the same, were the keepers of the gate, for the camps of the sons of Levi.

text.NRSV: Shallum son of Kore, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah, and his kindred of his ancestral house, the Korahites, were in charge of the work of the service, guardians of the thresholds of the tent, as their ancestors had been in charge of the camp of the Lord, guardians of the entrance.
text.Rotherham: And, Shallum, son of Kore, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah and his brethren of his ancestral house—the Korahites, were over the business of the service, watchers at the vestibule of the tent,—and, their fathers, had been over the camp of Yahweh, watchers at the entrance.

text.NRSV: And Phinehas son of Eleazar was chief over them in former times; the Lord was with him.
text.Rotherham: And, Phinehas son of Eleazar, was, chief ruler, over them aforetime, Yahweh, being with him.

text.NRSV: Zechariah son of Meshelemiah was gatekeeper at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: Zechariah son of Meshelemiah, was door-keeper at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

text.NRSV: All these, who were chosen as gatekeepers at the thresholds, were two hundred twelve. They were enrolled by genealogies in their villages. These David and the seer Samuel established in their office of trust.
text.Rotherham: All those who were chosen for door-keepers in the vestibule, were two hundred and twelve,—the same, in their villages, had registered themselves, the same, did David and Samuel the seer establish in their trust.

text.NRSV: So they and their sons were in charge of the gates of the house of the Lord, that is, the house of the tent, as guards.
text.Rotherham: So, they and their sons, were over the gates of the house of Yahweh, of the house of the tent, by watches.

text.NRSV: The gatekeepers were on the four sides, east, west, north, and south;
text.Rotherham: Towards the four winds, were the keepers of the gates,—eastward, westward, northward, and southward.

text.NRSV: and their kindred who were in their villages were obliged to come in every seven days, from time to time, to be with these;
text.Rotherham: And, their brethren in their villages, had to come in, every seven days, from time to time, along with these.

text.NRSV: for the four chief gatekeepers, who were Levites, were in charge of the chambers and the treasures of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: For, in trust, were four mighty men of the keepers of the gates, the same, were Levites,—and they were over the chambers, and over the treasuries of the house of God.

text.NRSV: And they would spend the night near the house of God; for on them lay the duty of watching, and they had charge of opening it every morning.
text.Rotherham: And, round about the house of God, used they to lodge,—for, upon them, was the charge, and they were over the setting open, morning by morning.

text.NRSV: Some of them had charge of the utensils of service, for they were required to count them when they were brought in and taken out.
text.Rotherham: And, some from among them, were over the utensils of the service,—for, by number, used they to bring them in, and, by number, used they to take them forth.

text.NRSV: Others of them were appointed over the furniture, and over all the holy utensils, also over the choice flour, the wine, the oil, the incense, and the spices.
text.Rotherham: And, some from among them, were appointed over the utensils, yea over all the vessels of the holy place,—and over the fine meal, and the wine, and the oil, and the frankincense, and the spices.

text.NRSV: Others, of the sons of the priests, prepared the mixing of the spices,
text.Rotherham: And, some from among the sons of the priests, were compounders of perfumes, with the spices.

text.NRSV: and Mattithiah, one of the Levites, the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite, was in charge of making the flat cakes.
text.Rotherham: And, Mattithiah, from among the Levites,—the same, was the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite—was in trust over the making of the flat cakes.

text.NRSV: Also some of their kindred of the Kohathites had charge of the rows of bread, to prepare them for every sabbath.
text.Rotherham: And, some from among the Kohathites, of their brethren, were over the Bread that was set in Array,—to place it sabbath by sabbath.

text.NRSV: Now these are the singers, the heads of ancestral houses of the Levites, living in the chambers of the temple free from other service, for they were on duty day and night.
text.Rotherham: These, also were the singers, ancestral chiefs of the Levites, in the chambers, free,—for, by day and by night, was there [a charge] upon them, in the business.

text.NRSV: These were heads of ancestral houses of the Levites, according to their generations, chiefs; these lived in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: These, were the ancestral chiefs, of the Levites, by their generations, chief men,—these, dwelt in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: In Gibeon lived the father of Gibeon, Jeiel, and the name of his wife was Maacah.
text.Rotherham: And, in Gibeon, dwelt the father of Gibeon, Jeiel,—the name of whose wife, was Maacah:

text.NRSV: His firstborn son was Abdon, then Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,
text.Rotherham: and, his firstborn son, Abdon,—and Zur, and Kish, and Baal and Ner, and Nadab;

text.NRSV: Gedor, Ahio, Zechariah, and Mikloth;
text.Rotherham: and Gedor, and Ahio, and Zechariah, and Mikloh.

text.NRSV: and Mikloth became the father of Shimeam; and these also lived opposite their kindred in Jerusalem, with their kindred.
text.Rotherham: And, Mikloth, begat Shimeam—and, they also, over against their brethren, did dwell in Jerusalem, along with their brethren.

text.NRSV: Ner became the father of Kish, Kish became the father of Saul, Saul became the father of Jonathan, Malchi-shua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal;
text.Rotherham: And, Ner, begat Kith, and, Kish, begat Saul,—and, Saul, begat Jonathan, and Malchishua, and Abinadab, and Eshbaal;

text.NRSV: and the son of Jonathan was Merib-baal; and Merib-baal became the father of Micah.
text.Rotherham: and, the son of Jonathan, was Merib-baal,—and, Merib-baal, begat Micah;

text.NRSV: The sons of Micah: Pithon, Melech, Tahrea, and Ahaz;
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Micah, were Pithon, and Melech, and Tahrea [and Ahaz] ;

text.NRSV: and Ahaz became the father of Jarah, and Jarah became the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri; and Zimri became the father of Moza.
text.Rotherham: and, Ahaz, begat Jarah, and, Jarah begat Alemeth, and Azmaveth, and Zimri,—and Zimri, begat Moza;

text.NRSV: Moza became the father of Binea; and Rephaiah was his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son.
text.Rotherham: and, Moza, begat Binea,—and Raphaiah his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son;

text.NRSV: Azel had six sons, and these are their names: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan; these were the sons of Azel.
text.Rotherham: and, Azel, had six sons, and these are their names—Azrikam, [his firstborn], and Ishmael, and Sheariah and Obadiah, and Hanan, [and Asah] . These, were the sons of Azel.

text.NRSV: Now the Philistines fought against Israel; and the men of Israel fled before the Philistines, and fell slain on Mount Gilboa.
text.Rotherham: Now, the Philistines, fought against Israel,—and the men of Israel fled from before the Philistines, and the wounded fell in Mount Gilboa.

text.NRSV: The Philistines overtook Saul and his sons; and the Philistines killed Jonathan and Abinadab and Malchi-shua, sons of Saul.
text.Rotherham: And the Philistines followed hard after Saul, and after his sons,—and the Philistines smote Jonathan, and Abinadab, and Malchishua, sons of Saul.

text.NRSV: The battle pressed hard upon Saul; and the archers found him, and he was wounded by the archers.
text.Rotherham: And the battle, went sore, against Saul, and the archers discovered him with the bow,—and he was terrified because of the archers.

text.NRSV: Then Saul said to his armor-bearer, “Draw your sword, and thrust me through with it, so that these uncircumcised may not come and make sport of me.” But his armor-bearer was unwilling, for he was terrified. So Saul took his own sword and fell on it.
text.Rotherham: Therefore said Saul unto his armour-bearer, Draw out thy sword and thrust me through therewith, lest these uncircumcised come and abuse me. But his armour-bearer would not, for he feared greatly,—so then Saul took the sword, and fell upon it.

text.NRSV: When his armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he also fell on his sword and died.
text.Rotherham: And, when his armour-bearer saw that Saul was dead, then, he also, fell upon the sword, and died.

text.NRSV: Thus Saul died; he and his three sons and all his house died together.
text.Rotherham: So Saul died, and his three sons, and, all his house, together, died.

text.NRSV: When all the men of Israel who were in the valley saw that the army had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead, they abandoned their towns and fled; and the Philistines came and occupied them.
text.Rotherham: And, when all the men of Israel that were in the vale saw, that they had fled, and that Saul and his sons were dead, then forsook they their cities, and fled, and the Philistines came and dwelt in them.

text.NRSV: The next day when the Philistines came to strip the dead, they found Saul and his sons fallen on Mount Gilboa.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the morrow, when the Philistines came to strip the slain,—that they found Saul, and his sons, lying prostrate in Mount Gilboa.

text.NRSV: They stripped him and took his head and his armor, and sent messengers throughout the land of the Philistines to carry the good news to their idols and to the people.
text.Rotherham: So they stript him, and took his head, and his armour,—and sent throughout the land of the Philistines round about, to publish it to their idols, and to the people.

text.NRSV: They put his armor in the temple of their gods, and fastened his head in the temple of Dagon.
text.Rotherham: And they put his armour, in the house of their gods,—but, his skull, fastened they up, in the house of Dagon.

text.NRSV: But when all Jabesh-gilead heard everything that the Philistines had done to Saul,
text.Rotherham: And, when all Jabesh-Gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul,

text.NRSV: all the valiant warriors got up and took the body of Saul and the bodies of his sons, and brought them to Jabesh. Then they buried their bones under the oak in Jabesh, and fasted seven days.
text.Rotherham: then rose up all the men of valour, and took away the corpse of Saul, and the corpses of his sons, and brought them to Jabesh,—and buried their bodies under the terebinth, in Jabesh, and fasted seven days.

text.NRSV: So Saul died for his unfaithfulness; he was unfaithful to the Lord in that he did not keep the command of the Lord; moreover, he had consulted a medium, seeking guidance,
text.Rotherham: Thus died Saul, for his unfaithfulness wherewith he dealt unfaithfully against Yahweh, over the word of Yahweh, which he kept not,—and, also, in asking by necromancy when he desired to enquire;

text.NRSV: and did not seek guidance from the Lord. Therefore the Lord put him to death and turned the kingdom over to David son of Jesse.
text.Rotherham: and enquired not of Yahweh, wherefore he slew him,—and turned round the kingdom unto David, son of Jesse.

text.NRSV: Then all Israel gathered together to David at Hebron and said, “See, we are your bone and flesh.
text.Rotherham: Then did all Israel gather themselves together to David, unto Hebron, saying,—Lo! thy bone and thy flesh, are we.

text.NRSV: For some time now, even while Saul was king, it was you who commanded the army of Israel. The Lord your God said to you: It is you who shall be shepherd of my people Israel, you who shall be ruler over my people Israel.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, in time past even when Saul was king, thou, wast he that led out and brought in Israel,—and Yahweh thy God said unto thee, Thou, shalt shepherd my people Israel, Yea, thou, shalt be chief ruler, over my people Israel.

text.NRSV: So all the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron, and David made a covenant with them at Hebron before the Lord. And they anointed David king over Israel, according to the word of the Lord by Samuel.
text.Rotherham: Thus came all the elders of Israel unto the king, to Hebron, and David solemnised to them a covenant in Hebron, before Yahweh,—and they anointed David to be king over Israel, according to the word of Yahweh, through Samuel.

text.NRSV: David and all Israel went to Jerusalem, that is Jebus, where the Jebusites were, the inhabitants of the land.
text.Rotherham: Then went David and all Israel to Jerusalem, the same, was Jebus,—where were the Jebusites, the inhabitants of the land.

text.NRSV: The inhabitants of Jebus said to David, “You will not come in here.” Nevertheless David took the stronghold of Zion, now the city of David.
text.Rotherham: And the inhabitants of Jebus said unto David, Thou shalt not come in hither,—But David captured the citadel of Zion, the same, is the city of David.

text.NRSV: David had said, “Whoever strikes the Jebusites first shall be chief and commander.” And Joab son of Zeruiah went up first, so he became chief.
text.Rotherham: And David said, Whosoever smiteth the Jebusites, first, shall become a chief, and a ruler,—So then Joab son of Zeruiah, went up first, and became a chief.

text.NRSV: David resided in the stronghold; therefore it was called the city of David.
text.Rotherham: And David dwelt in the citadel,—for this cause, did they call it, The City of David.

text.NRSV: He built the city all around, from the Millo in complete circuit; and Joab restored the rest of the city.
text.Rotherham: And he built the city round about, from Millo even as far as the circuit,—but, Joab, suffered the remainder of the city to live.

text.NRSV: And David became greater and greater, for the Lord of hosts was with him.
text.Rotherham: Thus did David wax greater and yet greater,—and, Yahweh of Hosts, was with him.

text.NRSV: Now these are the chiefs of David’s warriors, who gave him strong support in his kingdom, together with all Israel, to make him king, according to the word of the Lord concerning Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the chiefs of the heroes, that pertained to David, who held strongly with him in his kingdom, with all Israel, to make him king,—according to the word of Yahweh, concerning Israel.

text.NRSV: This is the list of David’s warriors: Jashobeam son of a Hachmonite was chief of the Three; he wielded his spear against three hundred whom he killed at one time.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the number of the heroes who pertained to David,—Jashobeam son of a Hachmonite, chief of the thirty, the same, brandished his spear over three hundred—slain at one time.

text.NRSV: And next to him among the three warriors was Eleazar son of Dodo, the Ahohite.
text.Rotherham: And, after him, was Eleazar son of Dodai, the Ahohite,—he, was among the three heroes;

text.NRSV: He was with David at Pas-dammim when the Philistines were gathered there for battle. There was a plot of ground full of barley. Now the people had fled from the Philistines,
text.Rotherham: he, was with David in Pas-dammim, where, the Philistines, were gathered together to battle, and there was a field-plot full of barley,—and, the people, fled, from before the Philistines;

text.NRSV: but he and David stood their ground in the middle of the plot, defended it, and killed the Philistines; and the Lord saved them by a great victory.
text.Rotherham: so they took their stand in the midst of the plot and rescued it, and smote the Philistines,—and Yahweh wrought a great victory.

text.NRSV: Three of the thirty chiefs went down to the rock to David at the cave of Adullam, while the army of the Philistines was encamped in the valley of Rephaim.
text.Rotherham: And three of the thirty chiefs went down upon the rock, unto David, into the cave of Adullam,—a host of Philistines, being encamped in the vale of Rephaim.

text.NRSV: David was then in the stronghold; and the garrison of the Philistines was then at Bethlehem.
text.Rotherham: Now, David, then was in the stronghold,—and, a garrison of Philistines, then was in Bethlehem.

text.NRSV: David said longingly, “O that someone would give me water to drink from the well of Bethlehem that is by the gate!”
text.Rotherham: And David longed, and said—Who will give me to drink of the water, out of the well of Bethlehem, that is within the gate?

text.NRSV: Then the Three broke through the camp of the Philistines, and drew water from the well of Bethlehem that was by the gate, and they brought it to David. But David would not drink of it; he poured it out to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: And the three brake through the host of the Philistines, and drew water out of the well of Bethlehem, that was within the gate, and bare it, and brought it in unto David,—yet would not David drink it, but poured it out unto Yahweh;

text.NRSV: and said, “My God forbid that I should do this. Can I drink the blood of these men? For at the risk of their lives they brought it.” Therefore he would not drink it. The three warriors did these things.
text.Rotherham: and said—Far be it from me, of my God, that I should do this! The blood of these men, shall I drink, with their lives? For, with their lives, have they brought it! So he would not drink it. These things, did the three heroes.

text.NRSV: Now Abishai, the brother of Joab, was chief of the Thirty. With his spear he fought against three hundred and killed them, and won a name beside the Three.
text.Rotherham: And, Abishai brother of Joab—he, was the chief of three, he, having brandished his spear against three hundred—who were slain,—and so, he, had a name among three.

text.NRSV: He was the most renowned of the Thirty, and became their commander; but he did not attain to the Three.
text.Rotherham: Of the three in the second rank, was he honourable, therefore became he their captain,—though, unto the [first] three, did he not attain.

text.NRSV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a valiant man of Kabzeel, a doer of great deeds; he struck down two sons of Ariel of Moab. He also went down and killed a lion in a pit on a day when snow had fallen.
text.Rotherham: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, son of a man of valour, hero of many a deed, from Kabziel—he, smote the two [sons] of Ariel of Moab, he, also went down and smote a lion in the midst of a pit, in time of snow;

text.NRSV: And he killed an Egyptian, a man of great stature, five cubits tall. The Egyptian had in his hand a spear like a weaver’s beam; but Benaiah went against him with a staff, snatched the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and killed him with his own spear.
text.Rotherham: he, also smote an Egyptian, a man of [great] stature, five cubits, and, in the hand of the Egyptian, was a spear like a weaver’s beam, so he went down unto him with a staff,—and wrested the spear out of the hand of the Egyptian, and slew him with his spear.

text.NRSV: Such were the things Benaiah son of Jehoiada did, and he won a name beside the three warriors.
text.Rotherham: These things, did Benaiah son of Jehoiada, so, he, had a name among three heroes.

text.NRSV: He was renowned among the Thirty, but he did not attain to the Three. And David put him in charge of his bodyguard.
text.Rotherham: Among the thirty, lo! honourable, was he, but, unto the [first] three, did he not attain,—howbeit David set him over his council.

text.NRSV: The warriors of the armies were Asahel brother of Joab, Elhanan son of Dodo of Bethlehem,
text.Rotherham: Now, the heroes of the forces, were,—Asahel, brother of Joab, Elhanan, son of Dodo, of Bethlehem;

text.NRSV: Shammoth of Harod, Helez the Pelonite,
text.Rotherham: Shammoth, the Harorite, Helez the Paltite;

text.NRSV: Ira son of Ikkesh of Tekoa, Abiezer of Anathoth,
text.Rotherham: Ira, son of Ikkesh, the Tekoite, Abiezer, the Anathothite;

text.NRSV: Sibbecai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,
text.Rotherham: Sibbecai, the Hushathite, Ilai, the Ahohite;

text.NRSV: Maharai of Netophah, Heled son of Baanah of Netophah,
text.Rotherham: Maharai, the Netophathite, Heled, son of Baanah, the Netophathite;

text.NRSV: Ithai son of Ribai of Gibeah of the Benjaminites, Benaiah of Pirathon,
text.Rotherham: Ithai, son of Ribai, of Gibeah, of the sons of Benjamin,—Benaiah, the Pirathonite;

text.NRSV: Hurai of the wadis of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite,
text.Rotherham: Hurai, of the torrents of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite;

text.NRSV: Azmaveth of Bahurim, Eliahba of Shaalbon,
text.Rotherham: Azmaveth, the Baharumite, Eliahba, the Shaalbonite;

text.NRSV: Hashem the Gizonite, Jonathan son of Shagee the Hararite,
text.Rotherham: the sons of Hashem, the Gizonite, Jonathan, son of Shageh, the Hararite;

text.NRSV: Ahiam son of Sachar the Hararite, Eliphal son of Ur,
text.Rotherham: Ahiam, son of Sacar, the Hararite, Eliphal, son of Ur;

text.NRSV: Hepher the Mecherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite,
text.Rotherham: Hepher, the Mecherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite;

text.NRSV: Hezro of Carmel, Naarai son of Ezbai,
text.Rotherham: Hezro, the Carmelite, Naarai, son of Ezbai;

text.NRSV: Joel the brother of Nathan, Mibhar son of Hagri,
text.Rotherham: Joel, brother of Nathan, Mibhar son of Hagri;

text.NRSV: Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai of Beeroth, the armor-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah,
text.Rotherham: Zelek, the Ammonite,—Naharai, the Berothite, the armour-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah;

text.NRSV: Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,
text.Rotherham: Ira, the Ithrite, Gareb, the Ithrite;

text.NRSV: Uriah the Hittite, Zabad son of Ahlai,
text.Rotherham: Uriah, the Hittite, Zabad son of Ahlai;

text.NRSV: Adina son of Shiza the Reubenite, a leader of the Reubenites, and thirty with him,
text.Rotherham: Adina, son of Shiza, the Reubenite, a chief of the Reubenites and over the thirty;

text.NRSV: Hanan son of Maacah, and Joshaphat the Mithnite,
text.Rotherham: Hanan, son of Maacah, and Joshaphat, the Mithnite;

text.NRSV: Uzzia the Ashterathite, Shama and Jeiel sons of Hotham the Aroerite,
text.Rotherham: Uzzia, the Ashterathite,—Shama, and Jeiel, sons of Hotham, the Aroerite;

text.NRSV: Jediael son of Shimri, and his brother Joha the Tizite,
text.Rotherham: Jediael, son of Shimri, and Joha, his brother, the Tizite;

text.NRSV: Eliel the Mahavite, and Jeribai and Joshaviah sons of Elnaam, and Ithmah the Moabite,
text.Rotherham: Eliel, the Mahavite, and Jeribai and Joshaviah, sons of Elnaam,—and Ithmah the Moabite;

text.NRSV: Eliel, and Obed, and Jaasiel the Mezobaite.
text.Rotherham: Eliel, and Obed, and Jaasiel, of Zobah.

text.NRSV: The following are those who came to David at Ziklag, while he could not move about freely because of Saul son of Kish; they were among the warriors who helped him in war.
text.Rotherham: Now these, are they who came unto David, to Ziklag, he yet being shut up, because of Saul son of Kish,—and, they, were among the heroes, helpers in the war;

text.NRSV: They were archers who could shoot arrows and sling stones with either the right hand or the left; they were Benjaminites, Saul’s kindred.
text.Rotherham: armed with the bow, using right hand or left with stones, and with arrows in the bow,—of the brethren of Saul, out of Benjamin:

text.NRSV: The chief was Ahiezer, then Joash, both sons of Shemaah of Gibeah; also Jeziel and Pelet sons of Azmaveth; Beracah, Jehu of Anathoth,
text.Rotherham: the chief, Ahiezer, and Joash, sons of Shemaah the Gibeathite, and Jeziel and Pelet, sons of Azmaveth,—and Beracah, and Jehu the Anathothite;

text.NRSV: Ishmaiah of Gibeon, a warrior among the Thirty and a leader over the Thirty; Jeremiah, Jahaziel, Johanan, Jozabad of Gederah,
text.Rotherham: and Ishmaiah, the Gibeonite, a hero among the thirty, and over the thirty,—and Jeremiah and Jahaziel, and Johanan, and Jozabad, the Gederathite;

text.NRSV: Eluzai, Jerimoth, Bealiah, Shemariah, Shephatiah the Haruphite;
text.Rotherham: Eluzai and Jerimoth and Bealiah and Shemariah, and Shephatiah, the Haruphite;

text.NRSV: Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, and Jashobeam, the Korahites;
text.Rotherham: Elkanah and Isshiah, and Azarel, and Joezer and Jashobeam, the Korahites;

text.NRSV: and Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jeroham of Gedor.
text.Rotherham: and Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jeroham, of Gedor.

text.NRSV: From the Gadites there went over to David at the stronghold in the wilderness mighty and experienced warriors, expert with shield and spear, whose faces were like the faces of lions, and who were swift as gazelles on the mountains:
text.Rotherham: And, of the Gadites, there separated themselves unto David, to the stronghold towards the desert, heroes of valour, men of war, for battle, men that could handle shield and spear,—and, faces of lions, were their faces, and, like gazelles upon the mountains, were they, for swiftness:

text.NRSV: Ezer the chief, Obadiah second, Eliab third,
text.Rotherham: Ezer, the chief, Obadiah, the second,—Eliab, the third;

text.NRSV: Mishmannah fourth, Jeremiah fifth,
text.Rotherham: Mashmannah, the fourth, Jeremiah, the fifth;

text.NRSV: Attai sixth, Eliel seventh,
text.Rotherham: Attai, the sixth, Eliel the seventh;

text.NRSV: Johanan eighth, Elzabad ninth,
text.Rotherham: Johanan, the eighth, Elzabad, the ninth;

text.NRSV: Jeremiah tenth, Machbannai eleventh.
text.Rotherham: Jeremiah, the tenth, Machbannai, the eleventh.

text.NRSV: These Gadites were officers of the army, the least equal to a hundred and the greatest to a thousand.
text.Rotherham: These, were of the sons of Gad, chiefs of the host,—one to a hundred, the least, and, the greatest, to a thousand.

text.NRSV: These are the men who crossed the Jordan in the first month, when it was overflowing all its banks, and put to flight all those in the valleys, to the east and to the west.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who passed over the Jordan, in the first month, when, it, was full, over all its banks,—and they put to flight all them of the vales, to the east and to the west.

text.NRSV: Some Benjaminites and Judeans came to the stronghold to David.
text.Rotherham: And there came, of the sons of Benjamin, and Judah, as far as the stronghold, unto David.

text.NRSV: David went out to meet them and said to them, “If you have come to me in friendship to help me, then my heart will be knit to you; but if you have come to betray me to my adversaries, though my hands have done no wrong, then may the God of our ancestors see and give judgment.”
text.Rotherham: And David went forth to meet them, and responded, and said to them, If, peaceably, ye are come unto me, to help me, I shall have towards you, a heart for unity,—but, if to betray me to mine adversaries, there being no violence in my hands, may the God of our fathers see and rebuke!

text.NRSV: Then the spirit came upon Amasai, chief of the Thirty, and he said, “We are yours, O David; and with you, O son of Jesse! Peace, peace to you, and peace to the one who helps you! For your God is the one who helps you.” Then David received them, and made them officers of his troops.
text.Rotherham: Then, the spirit, clothed Amasai, chief of the thirty, Thine, O David, Yea, with, thee, O son of Jesse! Prosperity, prosperity to thee, And prosperity to thy helpers, For thy God, hath helped thee: So David accepted them, and set them among the chiefs of the band.

text.NRSV: Some Manassites deserted to David when he came with the Philistines for the battle against Saul. (Yet he did not help them, for the lords of the Philistines took counsel and sent him away, saying, “He will desert to his master Saul at the cost of our heads.”)
text.Rotherham: And, of Manasseh, there fell away unto David, when he came with the Philistines against Saul to battle, but they helped them not,—for the lords of the Philistines advisedly sent him away, saying, At the price of our heads, he might fall away to his master, Saul.

text.NRSV: As he went to Ziklag these Manassites deserted to him: Adnah, Jozabad, Jediael, Michael, Jozabad, Elihu, and Zillethai, chiefs of thousands in Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: When he went into Ziklag, there fell away unto him, of Manasseh, Adnah and Jozabad, and Jediael and Michael and Jozabad, and Elihu and Zillethai,—chiefs of the thousands that pertained to Manasseh;

text.NRSV: They helped David against the band of raiders, for they were all mighty warriors and were commanders in the army.
text.Rotherham: and, they, helped with David, against the [marauding] band, for, heroes of valour, were they all,—and they became captains in the host;

text.NRSV: Indeed from day to day people kept coming to David to help him, until there was a great army, like an army of God.
text.Rotherham: for, from day to day, there came unto David, to help him,—until it was a great camp, like a camp of God.

text.NRSV: These are the numbers of the divisions of the armed troops who came to David in Hebron to turn the kingdom of Saul over to him, according to the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the numbers of the chiefs armed for war, they came unto David, to Hebron,—to turn round the kingdom of Saul unto him, according to the bidding of Yahweh:

text.NRSV: Of the Judeans carrying shield and spear, six thousand eight hundred armed troops.
text.Rotherham: Sons of Judah, bearers of shield and spear, six thousand and eight hundred, armed for war.

text.NRSV: Of the Simeonites, mighty warriors, seven thousand one hundred.
text.Rotherham: Of sons of Simeon, heroes of valour for the war, seven thousand, and one hundred.

text.NRSV: Of the Levites, four thousand six hundred.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Levi, four thousand, and six hundred.

text.NRSV: Jehoiada, leader of the house of Aaron, and with him three thousand seven hundred.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoiada, was chief ruler for Aaron,—and, with him, three thousand and seven hundred;

text.NRSV: Zadok, a young warrior, and twenty-two commanders from his own ancestral house.
text.Rotherham: and Zadok, a young man, a hero of valour,—and the house of his father, captains twenty and two.

text.NRSV: Of the Benjaminites, the kindred of Saul, three thousand, of whom the majority had continued to keep their allegiance to the house of Saul.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Benjamin, brethren of Saul, three thousand,—and, hitherto, the greater part of them, had been keeping the charge of the house of Saul.

text.NRSV: Of the Ephraimites, twenty thousand eight hundred, mighty warriors, famous in their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Ephraim, twenty thousand, and eight hundred,—heroes of valour, men of renown, to their ancestral house.

text.NRSV: Of the half-tribe of Manasseh, eighteen thousand, who were expressly named to come and make David king.
text.Rotherham: And, of the half tribe of Manasseh, eighteen thousand, who were distinguished by name, coming in to make David king.

text.NRSV: Of Issachar, those who had understanding of the times, to know what Israel ought to do, two hundred chiefs, and all their kindred under their command.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Issachar, such as were of good understanding of the times, to know what Israel, should do, their chiefs, were two hundred, and, all their brethren, were at their bidding.

text.NRSV: Of Zebulun, fifty thousand seasoned troops, equipped for battle with all the weapons of war, to help David with singleness of purpose.
text.Rotherham: Of Zebulun—such as were ready to go forth in host, expert for battle with all weapons of war, fifty thousand,—and for setting in array, not of two minds!

text.NRSV: Of Naphtali, a thousand commanders, with whom there were thirty-seven thousand armed with shield and spear.
text.Rotherham: And, of Naphtali, a thousand captains,—and, with them, with shield and spear, thirty-seven thousand.

text.NRSV: Of the Danites, twenty-eight thousand six hundred equipped for battle.
text.Rotherham: And, of the Danite, expert for war, twenty-eight thousand, and six hundred.

text.NRSV: Of Asher, forty thousand seasoned troops ready for battle.
text.Rotherham: And, of Asher, such as were ready to go forth in host to set in array for battle, forty thousand.

text.NRSV: Of the Reubenites and Gadites and the half-tribe of Manasseh from beyond the Jordan, one hundred twenty thousand armed with all the weapons of war.
text.Rotherham: And, from over the Jordan, of the Reubenites and Gadites and half tribe of Manasseh, with all manner of weapons of war for battle, a hundred and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: All these, warriors arrayed in battle order, came to Hebron with full intent to make David king over all Israel; likewise all the rest of Israel were of a single mind to make David king.
text.Rotherham: All these, being men of war, expert in setting in battle-array, with a perfect heart, came to Hebron, to make David king over all Israel,—moreover also, all the rest of Israel, were of one heart to make David king.

text.NRSV: They were there with David for three days, eating and drinking, for their kindred had provided for them.
text.Rotherham: So they were there, with David, three days, eating and drinking,—for their brethren had made preparation for them.

text.NRSV: And also their neighbors, from as far as Issachar and Zebulun and Naphtali, were bringing food on donkeys, camels, mules, and oxen—abundant provisions of meal, cakes of figs, clusters of raisins, wine, oil, oxen, and sheep, for there was joy in Israel.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, they who were near of kin unto them, as far as Issachar and Zebulun and Naphtali, were bringing bread, on asses and on camels and on mules and on oxen, meal-food, cakes of figs and cakes of raisins, and wine, and oil, and oxen, and sheep—in abundance,—for there was joy Israel.

text.NRSV: David consulted with the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, with every leader.
text.Rotherham: And David consulted with the rulers of thousands and hundreds, even with every chief ruler.

text.NRSV: David said to the whole assembly of Israel, “If it seems good to you, and if it is the will of the Lord our God, let us send abroad to our kindred who remain in all the territories of Israel, and with them to the priests and Levites in the towns that have pasture lands, that they may come together to us.
text.Rotherham: And David said to all the convocation of Israel, If, unto you, it seemeth to be good, and from Yahweh our God, we will urgently send unto our brethren who remain in all the lands of Israel, and, with them, the priests and the Levites in their pasture-land cities,—that they may gather together unto us;

text.NRSV: Then let us bring again the ark of our God to us; for we did not seek it in the days of Saul.”
text.Rotherham: and let us bring round the ark of our God, unto us,—For they sought it not, in the days of Saul.

text.NRSV: The whole assembly agreed to do so, for the thing scarcely seemed right in the eyes of all the people.
text.Rotherham: Than said all the convocation, that it should be done thus,—for right was the thing, in the eyes of all the people.

text.NRSV: So David assembled all Israel from the Shihor of Egypt to Lebo-hamath, to bring the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim.
text.Rotherham: So David convoked all Israel, from Shihor of Egypt, even unto the entering in of Hamath,—to bring the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim.

text.NRSV: And David and all Israel went up to Baalah, that is, to Kiriath-jearim, which belongs to Judah, to bring up from there the ark of God, which is called by the name of the Lord who is enthroned on the cherubim.
text.Rotherham: And David went up and all Israel to Baalah, unto Kiriath-jearim, which pertaineth to Judah,—to bring up from thence, the ark of God—Yahweh who inhabiteth the cherubim, on which is called the Name.

text.NRSV: They carried the ark of God on a new cart, from the house of Abinadab, and Uzzah and Ahio were driving the cart.
text.Rotherham: And they carried the ark of God, upon a new waggon, out of the house of Aminadab,—and, Uzza and Ahio, were driving the waggon.

text.NRSV: David and all Israel were dancing before God with all their might, with song and lyres and harps and tambourines and cymbals and trumpets.
text.Rotherham: And, David and all Israel, were dancing for joy before God, with all boldness, and with songs and with lyres, and with harps and with timbrels, and with cymbals, and with trumpets.

text.NRSV: When they came to the threshing floor of Chidon, Uzzah put out his hand to hold the ark, for the oxen shook it.
text.Rotherham: And, when they came as far as the threshing-floor of Chidon, Uzza thrust forth his hand, to seize the ark, for the oxen were restive.

text.NRSV: The anger of the Lord was kindled against Uzzah; he struck him down because he put out his hand to the ark; and he died there before God.
text.Rotherham: Then was kindled the anger of Yahweh against Uzza, and he smote him, because he thrust forth his hand unto the ark,—and he died there, before God.

text.NRSV: David was angry because the Lord had burst out against Uzzah; so that place is called Perez-uzzah to this day.
text.Rotherham: And it was a sadness unto David, that Yahweh had broken in with a breach against Uzza,—and he called that place Perez-uzza, [as it is called] unto this day.

text.NRSV: David was afraid of God that day; he said, “How can I bring the ark of God home to me?”
text.Rotherham: And David was afraid of God, on that day, saying,—How can I bring unto me, the ark of God?

text.NRSV: So David did not take the ark into the city of David; he delivered it into the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
text.Rotherham: So David removed not the ark unto him, into the city of David,—but took it aside into the house of Obed-edom, the Gittite.

text.NRSV: The ark of God remained with the family of Obed-edom in his house three months; and the Lord blessed the house of Obed-edom and all that he had.
text.Rotherham: And the ark of God remained with the household of Obed-edom, in his house, three months,—and Yahweh blessed the household of Obed-edom, and all that he had.

text.NRSV: King Hiram of Tyre sent messengers to David, along with cedar logs, and masons and carpenters to build a house for him.
text.Rotherham: And Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers unto David, and timber of cedars, with masons and artificers, to build for him a house.

text.NRSV: David then perceived that the Lord had established him as king over Israel, and that his kingdom was highly exalted for the sake of his people Israel.
text.Rotherham: So David perceived, that Yahweh, had confirmed him, as king over Israel,—that his kingship was exalted, for the sake of his people Israel.

text.NRSV: David took more wives in Jerusalem, and David became the father of more sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: And David took more wives, in Jerusalem,—and David begat more sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the children who were born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the names of them who were born, whom he had in Jerusalem,—Shammua and Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon;

text.NRSV: Ibhar, Elishua, Elpelet,
text.Rotherham: and Ibhar and Elishua, and Elpelet;

text.NRSV: Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia,
text.Rotherham: and Nogah and Nepheg, and Japhia;

text.NRSV: Elishama, Beeliada, and Eliphelet.
text.Rotherham: and Elishama and Beeliada, and Eliphelet.

text.NRSV: When the Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over all Israel, all the Philistines went up in search of David; and David heard of it and went out against them.
text.Rotherham: And, when the Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over all Israel, all the Philistines came up to seek to secure David,—and David, hearing, went out against them.

text.NRSV: Now the Philistines had come and made a raid in the valley of Rephaim.
text.Rotherham: Now, the Philistines, had come and spread themselves out, in the vale of Rephaim.

text.NRSV: David inquired of God, “Shall I go up against the Philistines? Will you give them into my hand?” The Lord said to him, “Go up, and I will give them into your hand.”
text.Rotherham: Then David asked of God, saying, Shall I go up against the Philistines? and wilt thou deliver them into my hand? And Yahweh said to him, Go up, and I will deliver them into thy hand.

text.NRSV: So they went up to Baal-perazim, and David defeated them there. David said, “God has burst out against my enemies by my hand, like a bursting flood.” Therefore that place is called Baal-perazim.
text.Rotherham: So they came up in Baal-perazim, and David smote them there, and David said, God hath broken forth against mine enemies by my hand, like the breaking forth of waters,—For this cause, called they the name of that place, Baal-perazim.

text.NRSV: They abandoned their gods there, and at David’s command they were burned.
text.Rotherham: And they left their gods there,—and David gave the word, and they were burned up in fire.

text.NRSV: Once again the Philistines made a raid in the valley.
text.Rotherham: And the Philistines yet again spread themselves out in the vale.

text.NRSV: When David again inquired of God, God said to him, “You shall not go up after them; go around and come on them opposite the balsam trees.
text.Rotherham: So David, asked again, of God, and God said to him, Thou shalt not go up after them,—get thee round, away from them, so shalt thou come in upon them, over against the mulberry-trees;

text.NRSV: For when you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, then go out to battle; for God has gone out before you to strike down the army of the Philistines.”
text.Rotherham: and it shall be, when thou hearest a sound of marching in the tops of the mulberry-trees, then, shalt thou go forth into the battle,—for God will have gone forth before thee, to smite the host of the Philistines.

text.NRSV: David did as God had commanded him, and they struck down the Philistine army from Gibeon to Gezer.
text.Rotherham: So David did as God commanded him,—and they smote the host of the Philistines, from Gibeon even unto Gezer.

text.NRSV: The fame of David went out into all lands, and the Lord brought the fear of him on all nations.
text.Rotherham: And the name of David went forth, throughout all the lands,—and, Yahweh, put the dread of him upon all the nations.

text.NRSV: David built houses for himself in the city of David, and he prepared a place for the ark of God and pitched a tent for it.
text.Rotherham: And he made him houses, in the city of David,—and prepared a place for the ark of God, and pitched for it a tent.

text.NRSV: Then David said, “No one but the Levites may carry the ark of God, for the Lord chose them to carry the ark of the Lord and to minister to him forever.”
text.Rotherham: Then, said David, None must carry the ark of God, save the Levites,—for, of them, did Yahweh make choice, to bear the ark of Yahweh, and to attend it, unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: David assembled all Israel in Jerusalem to bring up the ark of the Lord to its place, which he had prepared for it.
text.Rotherham: So David convoked all Israel, unto Jerusalem,—to bring up the ark of Yahweh, unto the place thereof, which he had prepared for it.

text.NRSV: Then David gathered together the descendants of Aaron and the Levites:
text.Rotherham: And David gathered together the sons of Aaron, and the Levites:

text.NRSV: of the sons of Kohath, Uriel the chief, with one hundred twenty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Kohath, Uriel the chief, and his brethren, one hundred and twenty;

text.NRSV: of the sons of Merari, Asaiah the chief, with two hundred twenty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Merari, Asaiah the chief, and his brethren, two hundred and twenty;

text.NRSV: of the sons of Gershom, Joel the chief, with one hundred thirty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Gershom, Joel the chief, and his brethren, one hundred and thirty;

text.NRSV: of the sons of Elizaphan, Shemaiah the chief, with two hundred of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Elizaphan, Shemaiah the chief, and his brethren, two hundred;

text.NRSV: of the sons of Hebron, Eliel the chief, with eighty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Hebron, Eliel the chief, and his brethren, eighty;

text.NRSV: of the sons of Uzziel, Amminadab the chief, with one hundred twelve of his kindred.
text.Rotherham: of the son of Uzziel, Amminadab the chief, and his brethren, a hundred and twelve.

text.NRSV: David summoned the priests Zadok and Abiathar, and the Levites Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemaiah, Eliel, and Amminadab.
text.Rotherham: Then called David, for Zadok and for Abiathar, the priests,—and for the Levites, for Uriel, Asaiah and Joel, Shemaiah and Eliel, and Amminadab;

text.NRSV: He said to them, “You are the heads of the ancestral houses of the Levites; sanctify yourselves, you and your kindred, so that you may bring up the ark of the Lord, the God of Israel, to the place that I have prepared for it.
text.Rotherham: and said unto them, Ye, are the chiefs of the fathers of the Levites,—Hallow yourselves, ye and your brethren, so shall ye bring up the ark of Yahweh—God of Israel, into [the place] I have prepared for it.

text.NRSV: Because you did not carry it the first time, the Lord our God burst out against us, because we did not give it proper care.”
text.Rotherham: Because, at the first, ye, did not [bring it], Yahweh our God brake forth against us, because we sought him not in the appointed way.

text.NRSV: So the priests and the Levites sanctified themselves to bring up the ark of the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: So the priests and the Levites hallowed themselves,—to bring up the ark of Yahweh, God of Israel.

text.NRSV: And the Levites carried the ark of God on their shoulders with the poles, as Moses had commanded according to the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of the Levites bare the ark of God, just as Moses commanded, according to the word of Yahweh,—on their shoulder, with the staves upon them.

text.NRSV: David also commanded the chiefs of the Levites to appoint their kindred as the singers to play on musical instruments, on harps and lyres and cymbals, to raise loud sounds of joy.
text.Rotherham: And David spake to the chiefs of the Levites, to station their brethren the singers, with instruments of song, harps and lyres, and cymbals,—sounding aloud at the lifting up of the voice, for joy.

text.NRSV: So the Levites appointed Heman son of Joel; and of his kindred Asaph son of Berechiah; and of the sons of Merari, their kindred, Ethan son of Kushaiah;
text.Rotherham: So the Levites caused to stand, Heman son of Joel, and, of his brethren, Asaph, son of Berechiah,—and, of the sons of Merari their brethren, Ethan, son of Kushaiah;

text.NRSV: and with them their kindred of the second order, Zechariah, Jaaziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, and Mikneiah, and the gatekeepers Obed-edom and Jeiel.
text.Rotherham: and, with them, their brethren of the second degree,—Zechariah, Ben, and Jaaziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel and Unni, Eliab, and Benaiah, and Maaseiah, and Mattithiah, and Eliphelehu, and Mickneiah, and Obed-edom and Jeiel, the door-keepers.

text.NRSV: The singers Heman, Asaph, and Ethan were to sound bronze cymbals;
text.Rotherham: And, the singers, Heman, Asaph and Ethan, with cymbals of bronze, were to sound aloud;

text.NRSV: Zechariah, Aziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Maaseiah, and Benaiah were to play harps according to Alamoth;
text.Rotherham: and Zechariah, and Aziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Unni, and Eliab, and Maaseiah and Benaiah,—with harps, over the trebles;

text.NRSV: but Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-edom, Jeiel, and Azaziah were to lead with lyres according to the Sheminith.
text.Rotherham: and Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, and Mikneiah, and Obed-edom, and Jeiel and Azaziah,—with lyres over the bass, to take the lead.

text.NRSV: Chenaniah, leader of the Levites in music, was to direct the music, for he understood it.
text.Rotherham: And, Chenaniah the leader of the Levites, in the service, used to give instructions in the service, because skilful was he.

text.NRSV: Berechiah and Elkanah were to be gatekeepers for the ark.
text.Rotherham: And, Berechiah and Elkanah, were door-keepers to the ark.

text.NRSV: Shebaniah, Joshaphat, Nethanel, Amasai, Zechariah, Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, were to blow the trumpets before the ark of God. Obed-edom and Jehiah also were to be gatekeepers for the ark.
text.Rotherham: And, Shebaniah, and Joshaphat, and Nethanel, and Amasai, and Zechariah, and Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, were blowing with the trumpets, before the ark of God,—and, Obed-edom and Jehiah, were door-keepers, to the ark.

text.NRSV: So David and the elders of Israel and the commanders of thousands went to bring up the ark of the covenant of the Lord from the house of Obed-edom with rejoicing.
text.Rotherham: Thus, it was, David and the elders of Israel, and the rulers of thousands, who were going, to bring up the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, out of the house of Obed-edom, with joy.

text.NRSV: And because God helped the Levites who were carrying the ark of the covenant of the Lord, they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.
text.Rotherham: And so it was that, when God helped the Levites, who were bearing the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, they sacrificed seven bullocks and seven rams.

text.NRSV: David was clothed with a robe of fine linen, as also were all the Levites who were carrying the ark, and the singers, and Chenaniah the leader of the music of the singers; and David wore a linen ephod.
text.Rotherham: Now, David, was wrapped about with a robe of byssus, with all the Levites who were bearing the ark, and the singers, and Chenaniah the leader of the service rendered by the singers,—but, upon David, was an ephod of white linen.

text.NRSV: So all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the Lord with shouting, to the sound of the horn, trumpets, and cymbals, playing loudly on harps and lyres.
text.Rotherham: So, all Israel, were bringing up the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, with shouting, and with sound of the horn, and with trumpets, and with cymbals,—sounding aloud, with harps, and lyres.

text.NRSV: As the ark of the covenant of the Lord came to the city of David, Michal daughter of Saul looked out of the window, and saw King David leaping and dancing; and she despised him in her heart.
text.Rotherham: And so it was that, when the ark of the covenant of Yahweh came in as far as the city of David,—and Michal daughter of Saul looked forth through the window, and saw King David, dancing and playing, that she despised him in her heart.

text.NRSV: They brought the ark of God, and set it inside the tent that David had pitched for it; and they offered burnt offerings and offerings of well-being before God.
text.Rotherham: So they brought in the ark of God, and placed it in the midst of the tent, which David had pitched for it,—and they brought near ascending-sacrifices and peace-offerings, before God.

text.NRSV: When David had finished offering the burnt offerings and the offerings of well-being, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord;
text.Rotherham: And, when David had made an end of offering the ascending-sacrifices, and the peace-offerings, he blessed the people, in the name of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: and he distributed to every person in Israel—man and woman alike—to each a loaf of bread, a portion of meat, and a cake of raisins.
text.Rotherham: And he apportioned, to every one of Israel, both man and woman,—to every one, a loaf of bread, and a sweet drink, and a raisin cake.

text.NRSV: He appointed certain of the Levites as ministers before the ark of the Lord, to invoke, to thank, and to praise the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And he placed before the ark of Yahweh certain of the Levites, as attendants,—and to celebrate, and to give thanks and render praise, unto Yahweh, God of Israel;

text.NRSV: Asaph was the chief, and second to him Zechariah, Jeiel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel, with harps and lyres; Asaph was to sound the cymbals,
text.Rotherham: Asaph the chief, and, second to him, Zechariah,—Jeiel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Mattithiah, and Eliab, and Benaiah, and Obed-edom, and Jeiel, with instruments of harps, and with lyres, and Asaph with cymbals, sounding aloud:

text.NRSV: and the priests Benaiah and Jahaziel were to blow trumpets regularly before the ark of the covenant of God.
text.Rotherham: and Benaiah and Jahaziel, the priests,—with trumpets continually, before the ark of the covenant of God.

text.NRSV: Then on that day David first appointed the singing of praises to the Lord by Asaph and his kindred.
text.Rotherham: On that day, then, David gave [these songs] in chief, to give thanks unto Yahweh,—through Asaph, and his brethren:—

text.NRSV: O give thanks to the Lord, call on his name, make known his deeds among the peoples.
text.Rotherham: Give ye thanks to Yahweh, Call upon his name, Make known among the peoples, his doings;

text.NRSV: Sing to him, sing praises to him, tell of all his wonderful works.
text.Rotherham: Sing ye to him, Make music unto him, Speak ye of all his wonders.

text.NRSV: Glory in his holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the Lord rejoice.
text.Rotherham: Boast yourselves in his holy name, Joyful be the heart of them who are seeking Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Seek the Lord and his strength, seek his presence continually.
text.Rotherham: Search out Yahweh, and his power, Seek diligently his face, at all times.

text.NRSV: Remember the wonderful works he has done, his miracles, and the judgments he uttered,
text.Rotherham: Remember his wonders which he hath done, His splendid deeds, and the just decisions of his mouth.

text.NRSV: O offspring of his servant Israel, children of Jacob, his chosen ones.
text.Rotherham: O ye seed of Israel his servant, sons of Jacob his chosen ones:

text.NRSV: He is the Lord our God; his judgments are in all the earth.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh himself, is our God, through all the land, are his just decisions.

text.NRSV: Remember his covenant forever, the word that he commanded, for a thousand generations,
text.Rotherham: Remember yea to times age-abiding, his covenant, the word he hath commanded, to a thousand generations;

text.NRSV: the covenant that he made with Abraham, his sworn promise to Isaac,
text.Rotherham: Which he solemnised with Abraham, and his oath, to Isaac;

text.NRSV: which he confirmed to Jacob as a statute, to Israel as an everlasting covenant,
text.Rotherham: And confirmed it, unto Jacob, for a statute, unto Israel, as a covenant age-abiding:

text.NRSV: saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan as your portion for an inheritance.”
text.Rotherham: Saying, To thee, will I give the land of Canaan, as your inherited portion:

text.NRSV: When they were few in number, of little account, and strangers in the land,
text.Rotherham: While, as yet, ye were men who could be counted, a very few, and sojourners therein:

text.NRSV: wandering from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people,
text.Rotherham: And they wandered from nation to nation, and from one kingdom, to another people:

text.NRSV: he allowed no one to oppress them; he rebuked kings on their account,
text.Rotherham: He suffered no man to oppress them, And reproved—for their sakes—kings!

text.NRSV: saying, “Do not touch my anointed ones; do my prophets no harm.”
text.Rotherham: Ye may not touch mine Anointed ones, And, to my Prophets, may ye do no wrong.

text.NRSV: Sing to the Lord, all the earth. Tell of his salvation from day to day.
text.Rotherham: Sing to Yahweh, all the earth, Tell the tidings, from day to day, of his salvation:

text.NRSV: Declare his glory among the nations, his marvelous works among all the peoples.
text.Rotherham: Recount, among the nations, his glory, among all the peoples, his wonders.

text.NRSV: For great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised; he is to be revered above all gods.
text.Rotherham: For great is Yahweh, and worthy to be mightily praised, and to be revered is he above all gods;

text.NRSV: For all the gods of the peoples are idols, but the Lord made the heavens.
text.Rotherham: For, all the gods of the peoples, are things of nought, but, Yahweh, made, the heavens.

text.NRSV: Honor and majesty are before him; strength and joy are in his place.
text.Rotherham: Praise and majesty, are before him, Strength and joy, are in his dwelling-place.

text.NRSV: Ascribe to the Lord, O families of the peoples, ascribe to the Lord glory and strength.
text.Rotherham: Give to Yahweh, ye families of the peoples, Give to Yahweh, glory and strength:

text.NRSV: Ascribe to the Lord the glory due his name; bring an offering, and come before him. Worship the Lord in holy splendor;
text.Rotherham: Give to Yahweh, the glory of his name,—Bring ye a present, and enter before him, Bow down to Yahweh, in the adornment of holiness:

text.NRSV: tremble before him, all the earth. The world is firmly established; it shall never be moved.
text.Rotherham: Tremble at his presence, all the earth, But the world, shall be established, it shall not be shaken.

text.NRSV: Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice, and let them say among the nations, “The Lord is king!”
text.Rotherham: Let the heavens rejoice, and the earth exult, Let them say among the nations, Yahweh, hath become king!

text.NRSV: Let the sea roar, and all that fills it; let the field exult, and everything in it.
text.Rotherham: Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof, Let the field leap for joy, and all that is therein:

text.NRSV: Then shall the trees of the forest sing for joy before the Lord, for he comes to judge the earth.
text.Rotherham: Then, shall shout in triumph, the trees of the forest, before Yahweh, for he is coming, to judge the earth.

text.NRSV: O give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; for his steadfast love endures forever.
text.Rotherham: Give ye thanks unto Yahweh, For he is good, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.

text.NRSV: Say also: “Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather and rescue us from among the nations, that we may give thanks to your holy name, and glory in your praise.
text.Rotherham: And say ye, Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather us and deliver us, from among the nations,—that we may give thanks unto thy holy name, that we may triumph aloud in thy praise.

text.NRSV: Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting.” Then all the people said “Amen!” and praised the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Blessed be Yahweh, God of Israel, from one age even to another age. Then said all the people, Amen, And gave praise to Yahweh.

text.NRSV: David left Asaph and his kindred there before the ark of the covenant of the Lord to minister regularly before the ark as each day required,
text.Rotherham: So he left there, before the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, Asaph and his brethren,—to be in attendance before the ark continually, for the duty of a day on its day;

text.NRSV: and also Obed-edom and his sixty-eight kindred; while Obed-edom son of Jeduthun and Hosah were to be gatekeepers.
text.Rotherham: and Obed-edom with his brethren, sixty-eight,—and Obed-edom son of Jedithun and Hosah to be doorkeepers;

text.NRSV: And he left the priest Zadok and his kindred the priests before the tabernacle of the Lord in the high place that was at Gibeon,
text.Rotherham: and Zadok the priest, and his brethren the priests, before the habitation of Yahweh,—in the high place that was in Gibeon:

text.NRSV: to offer burnt offerings to the Lord on the altar of burnt offering regularly, morning and evening, according to all that is written in the law of the Lord that he commanded Israel.
text.Rotherham: to offer ascending-sacrifices unto Yahweh, upon the altar of ascending-sacrifice,—continually, morning and evening,—even according to all that is written in the law of Yahweh, which he commanded concerning Israel;

text.NRSV: With them were Heman and Jeduthun, and the rest of those chosen and expressly named to render thanks to the Lord, for his steadfast love endures forever.
text.Rotherham: and, with them, Heman and Jeduthun, and the rest of the chosen ones, who were distinguished by name,—to give thanks unto Yahweh, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness!

text.NRSV: Heman and Jeduthun had with them trumpets and cymbals for the music, and instruments for sacred song. The sons of Jeduthun were appointed to the gate.
text.Rotherham: And, with them, Heman and Jeduthun, [with] the trumpets, and the cymbals, for sounding aloud, and the instruments for the songs of God,—and the sons of Jeduthun, for the gate.

text.NRSV: Then all the people departed to their homes, and David went home to bless his household.
text.Rotherham: And all the people departed, every man to his house,—and David went round, to bless his own household.

text.NRSV: Now when David settled in his house, David said to the prophet Nathan, “I am living in a house of cedar, but the ark of the covenant of the Lord is under a tent.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when David had taken up his abode in his house, that David said unto Nathan the prophet, Lo! I, am dwelling in a house of cedars, but, the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, is under curtains.

text.NRSV: Nathan said to David, “Do all that you have in mind, for God is with you.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Nathan unto David, All that is in thy heart, do,—for, God, is with thee.

text.NRSV: But that same night the word of the Lord came to Nathan, saying:
text.Rotherham: But so it was that, in that night, the word of God came unto Nathan, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go and tell my servant David: Thus says the Lord: You shall not build me a house to live in.
text.Rotherham: Go and say unto David my servant, Thus, saith Yahweh,—Thou, shalt not build me a house, to dwell in;

text.NRSV: For I have not lived in a house since the day I brought out Israel to this day, but I have gone from tent to tent and from dwelling to dwelling.
text.Rotherham: seeing that I have not dwelt in a house, since the day that I brought up Israel, until this day,—but have been from tent to tent, and without a habitation.

text.NRSV: In all the places where I have moved about among all Israel, did I ever speak a word with any of the judges of Israel, whom I commanded to shepherd my people, saying, ‘Why have you not built me a house of cedar?’
text.Rotherham: Wheresoever I have wandered with all Israel, spake I ever, a word, with one of the judges of Israel, whom I charged to shepherd my people, saying,—Wherefore have ye not built me a house of cedars?

text.NRSV: Now therefore thus you shall say to my servant David: Thus says the Lord of hosts: I took you from the pasture, from following the sheep, to be ruler over my people Israel;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, thus, shalt thou say unto my servant David, Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, I myself, took thee from the pasture, from after the flock,—to become leader over my people Israel;

text.NRSV: and I have been with you wherever you went, and have cut off all your enemies from before you; and I will make for you a name, like the name of the great ones of the earth.
text.Rotherham: and was with thee whithersoever thou didst go, and have cut off all thine enemies, from before thee,—and will make thee a name, like the name of the great ones, who are in the earth;

text.NRSV: I will appoint a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, so that they may live in their own place, and be disturbed no more; and evildoers shall wear them down no more, as they did formerly,
text.Rotherham: and will appoint a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, and they shall inhabit their place, and be unsettled no more,—neither shall the sons of perversity again wear them out, as at the first;

text.NRSV: from the time that I appointed judges over my people Israel; and I will subdue all your enemies. Moreover I declare to you that the Lord will build you a house.
text.Rotherham: even from the days when I put judges in charge over my people Israel, and have subdued all thine enemies,—that I might make thee great, yea, a house, will Yahweh build for thee.

text.NRSV: When your days are fulfilled to go to be with your ancestors, I will raise up your offspring after you, one of your own sons, and I will establish his kingdom.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be that, when thy days are fulfilled, to sleep with thy fathers, then will I raise up thy seed after thee, who shall be of thy sons,—and I will establish his kingdom.

text.NRSV: He shall build a house for me, and I will establish his throne forever.
text.Rotherham: He, shall build me a house,—and I will establish his throne unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: I will be a father to him, and he shall be a son to me. I will not take my steadfast love from him, as I took it from him who was before you,
text.Rotherham: I, will become his father, and, he, shall become my son,—and, my lovingkindness, will I not cause to depart from him,—as I caused it to depart from him who was before thee;

text.NRSV: but I will confirm him in my house and in my kingdom forever, and his throne shall be established forever.”
text.Rotherham: but I will cause him to remain over my house and over my kingdom, unto times age-abiding,—and, his throne, shall be established, unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: In accordance with all these words and all this vision, Nathan spoke to David.
text.Rotherham: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so, spake Nathan unto David.

text.NRSV: Then King David went in and sat before the Lord, and said, “Who am I, O Lord God, and what is my house, that you have brought me thus far?
text.Rotherham: Then entered King David, and tarried before Yahweh,—and said, Who am, I, O Yahweh God, and what is my house, that thou hast brought me hitherto;

text.NRSV: And even this was a small thing in your sight, O God; you have also spoken of your servant’s house for a great while to come. You regard me as someone of high rank, O Lord God!
text.Rotherham: and hast made this seem little in thine eyes, O God, in that thou hast spoken concerning the house of thy servant, for a great while to come? Thus wilt thou provide for me, according to the rank of manhood, and hast exalted me, O Yahweh God!

text.NRSV: And what more can David say to you for honoring your servant? You know your servant.
text.Rotherham: What, yet further, can David say unto thee, [thus] to honour thy servant,—seeing that, thou thyself, knowest, thine own servant?

text.NRSV: For your servant’s sake, O Lord, and according to your own heart, you have done all these great deeds, making known all these great things.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, for the sake of thy servant, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all this great thing,—making known all the great things.

text.NRSV: There is no one like you, O Lord, and there is no God besides you, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, there is none like unto thee, yea there is no God besides thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.

text.NRSV: Who is like your people Israel, one nation on the earth whom God went to redeem to be his people, making for yourself a name for great and terrible things, in driving out nations before your people whom you redeemed from Egypt?
text.Rotherham: Who then is like thy people Israel, a nation alone in the earth,—whom God went to redeem, to be his own people, to make for thyself a name for great and fearful things, to drive out, nations, from before thy people, whom thou didst redeem out of Egypt;

text.NRSV: And you made your people Israel to be your people forever; and you, O Lord, became their God.
text.Rotherham: and didst appoint thy people Israel for thyself as a people, unto times age-abiding,—thou thyself, also, O Yahweh, becoming their God?

text.NRSV: “And now, O Lord, as for the word that you have spoken concerning your servant and concerning his house, let it be established forever, and do as you have promised.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Yahweh, the word which thou hast spoken concerning thy servant, and concerning his house, be it steadfast unto times age-abiding,—and do as thou hast spoken:

text.NRSV: Thus your name will be established and magnified forever in the saying, ‘The Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, is Israel’s God’; and the house of your servant David will be established before you.
text.Rotherham: yea let it be steadfast, and so let thy Name be magnified unto times age-abiding, saying, Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, is a God to Israel! seeing that, the house of David thy servant, is established before thee.

text.NRSV: For you, my God, have revealed to your servant that you will build a house for him; therefore your servant has found it in his heart to pray before you.
text.Rotherham: For, thou, O my God, hast unveiled the ear of thy servant, that thou wilt build for him a house,—for this cause, hath thy servant found [it in his heart] to pray before thee.

text.NRSV: And now, O Lord, you are God, and you have promised this good thing to your servant;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Yahweh, thou, art God,—therefore hast thou spoken concerning thy servant this goodness.

text.NRSV: now therefore may it please you to bless the house of your servant, that it may continue forever before you. For you, O Lord, have blessed and are blessed forever.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, let it please thee to bless the house of thy servant, that it may be unto times age-abiding before thee,—for, thou O Yahweh, hast blessed, and it is to be blessed unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: Some time afterward, David attacked the Philistines and subdued them; he took Gath and its villages from the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: And it canto to pass, after this, that David smote the Philistines, and subdued them,—and took Gath and her villages, out of the hand of the Philistines.

text.NRSV: He defeated Moab, and the Moabites became subject to David and brought tribute.
text.Rotherham: And he smote Moab,—and the Moabites became servants to David, bearers of a present.

text.NRSV: David also struck down King Hadadezer of Zobah, toward Hamath, as he went to set up a monument at the river Euphrates.
text.Rotherham: And David smote Hadarezer king of Zobah, towards Hamath,—as he went to establish his hold of the river Euphrates.

text.NRSV: David took from him one thousand chariots, seven thousand cavalry, and twenty thousand foot soldiers. David hamstrung all the chariot horses, but left one hundred of them.
text.Rotherham: And David captured from him a thousand chariots, and seven thousand horsemen, and twenty thousand footmen,—and David destroyed all the chariots, saving that he reserved from them a hundred chariots.

text.NRSV: When the Arameans of Damascus came to help King Hadadezer of Zobah, David killed twenty-two thousand Arameans.
text.Rotherham: And, when the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadarezer king of Zobah, David smote of the Syrians, twenty-two thousand men.

text.NRSV: Then David put garrisons in Aram of Damascus; and the Arameans became subject to David, and brought tribute. The Lord gave victory to David wherever he went.
text.Rotherham: And David put [garrisons] in Syria of Damascus, and so it was that the Syrians became, David’s, servants, bearers of a present,—so Yahweh gave victory to David, whithersoever he went.

text.NRSV: David took the gold shields that were carried by the attendants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And David took the shields of gold, that were upon the servants of Hadarezer,—and brought them to Jerusalem;

text.NRSV: From Tibhath and from Cun, cities of Hadadezer, David took a vast quantity of bronze; with it Solomon made the bronze sea and the pillars and the bronze vessels.
text.Rotherham: also, from Tibhath and from Cun, cities of Hadarezer, David took very much bronze,—therewith, did Solomon make the sea of bronze, and the pillars, and the vessels of bronze.

text.NRSV: When King Tou of Hamath heard that David had defeated the whole army of King Hadadezer of Zobah,
text.Rotherham: And, when Tou, king of Hamath, heard that David had smitten all the force of Hadarezer king of Zobah,

text.NRSV: he sent his son Hadoram to King David, to greet him and to congratulate him because he had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him. Now Hadadezer had often been at war with Tou. He sent all sorts of articles of gold, of silver, and of bronze;
text.Rotherham: he sent Hadoram his son unto King David, to ask after his welfare, and to bless him, because he had made war against Hadarezer and had smitten him, for, a man making wars against Tou, had been Hadarezer,—and [he had with him] all manner of vessels of gold and silver and bronze;

text.NRSV: these also King David dedicated to the Lord, together with the silver and gold that he had carried off from all the nations, from Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, and Amalek.
text.Rotherham: them also, did King David hallow unto Yahweh, with the silver and the gold, that he bare away from all the nations,—from Edom, and from Moab, and from the sons of Ammon, and from the Philistines, and from Amalek.

text.NRSV: Abishai son of Zeruiah killed eighteen thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, Abishai, son of Zeruiah, smote Edom, in the Valley of Salt,—eighteen thousand;

text.NRSV: He put garrisons in Edom; and all the Edomites became subject to David. And the Lord gave victory to David wherever he went.
text.Rotherham: and set, in Edom, garrisons, and all Edom became servants unto David,—and Yahweh gave David the victory, whithersoever he went.

text.NRSV: So David reigned over all Israel; and he administered justice and equity to all his people.
text.Rotherham: So David reigned over all Israel,—and he was executing justice and righteousness for all his people;

text.NRSV: Joab son of Zeruiah was over the army; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder;
text.Rotherham: and, Joab, son of Zeruiah, was over the army,—and, Jehoshaphat, son of Elihud, was recorder;

text.NRSV: Zadok son of Ahitub and Ahimelech son of Abiathar were priests; Shavsha was secretary;
text.Rotherham: and, Zadok, son of Ahitub, and Abimelech son of Abiathar, were priests,—and, Shavsha, was scribe;

text.NRSV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and David’s sons were the chief officials in the service of the king.
text.Rotherham: and, Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, was over the Cherethites, and the Pelethites,—and, the sons of David, were first, in attendance on the king.

text.NRSV: Some time afterward, King Nahash of the Ammonites died, and his son succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after this, that Nahash king of the sons of Ammon died,—and Hanun his son reigned in his stead.

text.NRSV: David said, “I will deal loyally with Hanun son of Nahash, for his father dealt loyally with me.” So David sent messengers to console him concerning his father. When David’s servants came to Hanun in the land of the Ammonites, to console him,
text.Rotherham: Then said David—I will show lovingkindness unto Hanun, son of Nahash,—for his father shewed unto me lovingkindness. So David sent messengers to console him concerning his father,—and the servants of David came into the land of the sons of Ammon unto Hanun, to console him.

text.NRSV: the officials of the Ammonites said to Hanun, “Do you imagine, because David has sent consolers to you, that he is honoring your father? Have not his servants come to you to search and to overthrow and to spy out the land?”
text.Rotherham: Then said the rulers of the sons of Ammon unto Hanun, Doth David honour thy father in thine eyes, that he hath sent to thee comforters? Is it not, for the sake of exploring and overthrowing, and spying out the land, that his servants have come to thee?

text.NRSV: So Hanun seized David’s servants, shaved them, cut off their garments in the middle at their hips, and sent them away;
text.Rotherham: So Hanun took the servants of David, and shaved them, and cut off their upper garments in the middle, as far as their parts of shame,—and let them go.

text.NRSV: and they departed. When David was told about the men, he sent messengers to them, for they were greatly ashamed. The king said, “Stay at Jericho until your beards have grown, and then return.”
text.Rotherham: And people went and told David, concerning the men, so he sent to meet them, because the men were greatly ashamed,—and the king said—Tarry at Jericho, until your beards be grown, and then return.

text.NRSV: When the Ammonites saw that they had made themselves odious to David, Hanun and the Ammonites sent a thousand talents of silver to hire chariots and cavalry from Mesopotamia, from Aram-maacah and from Zobah.
text.Rotherham: And, when the sons of Ammon saw, that they had made themselves odious with David, Hanun and the sons of Ammon sent a thousand talents of silver, to hire for themselves—out of Mesopotamia, and out of Aram-maacah, and out of Zobah,—chariots, and horsemen.

text.NRSV: They hired thirty-two thousand chariots and the king of Maacah with his army, who came and encamped before Medeba. And the Ammonites were mustered from their cities and came to battle.
text.Rotherham: So they hired for themselves thirty-two thousand chariots, and the king of Maacah, and his people, who came and pitched before Medeba,—and the sons of Ammon gathered themselves together out of their cities, and entered into the war.

text.NRSV: When David heard of it, he sent Joab and all the army of the warriors.
text.Rotherham: Now, when David heard, he sent Joab and all the army of heroes.

text.NRSV: The Ammonites came out and drew up in battle array at the entrance of the city, and the kings who had come were by themselves in the open country.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Ammon came out, and set in array for the battle, at the opening of the city,—whereas, the kings who had come, were by themselves, in the field.

text.NRSV: When Joab saw that the line of battle was set against him both in front and in the rear, he chose some of the picked men of Israel and arrayed them against the Arameans;
text.Rotherham: So, when Joab saw that the battle confronted him, before and behind,—he chose out of all the choice in Israel, and set in array to meet the Syrians;

text.NRSV: the rest of his troops he put in the charge of his brother Abishai, and they were arrayed against the Ammonites.
text.Rotherham: and, the rest of the people, delivered he into the hand of Abishai his brother,—and they set in array, to meet the sons of Ammon.

text.NRSV: He said, “If the Arameans are too strong for me, then you shall help me; but if the Ammonites are too strong for you, then I will help you.
text.Rotherham: And he said—If, too strong for me, be the Syrians, then shalt thou come to my help,—but, if, the sons of Ammon, be too strong for thee, then will I help thee.
