text.NRSV: Be brave, and let us fight bravely for our people and for the cities of our God; and may the Lord do what seems good to him.”
text.Rotherham: Be strong, and let us put forth our strength, for the sake of our people, and for the sake of the cities of our God,—and let, Yahweh, do, what is good in his own eyes.

text.NRSV: So Joab and the troops who were with him advanced toward the Arameans for battle; and they fled before him.
text.Rotherham: Then did Joab, and all the people who were with him, draw near before the Syrians unto the battle,—and they fled from before him.

text.NRSV: When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans fled, they likewise fled before Abishai, Joab’s brother, and entered the city. Then Joab came to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, when, the sons of Ammon, saw that the Syrians had fled, then fled, they also, from before Abishai his brother, and entered the city,—and Joab came to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: But when the Arameans saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they sent messengers and brought out the Arameans who were beyond the Euphrates, with Shophach the commander of the army of Hadadezer at their head.
text.Rotherham: And when the Syrians saw that they had been defeated before Israel, then sent they messengers, and brought out the Syrians who were Beyond the River,—with Shophach captain of the army of Hadarezer, before them.

text.NRSV: When David was informed, he gathered all Israel together, crossed the Jordan, came to them, and drew up his forces against them. When David set the battle in array against the Arameans, they fought with him.
text.Rotherham: And it was told David, and he gathered together all Israel, and crossed the Jordan, and came unto them, and set in array against them,—and, when David had set in array against the Syrians for battle, they fought with him.

text.NRSV: The Arameans fled before Israel; and David killed of the Arameans seven thousand chariot-drivers and forty thousand foot soldiers, and also killed Shophach the commander of their army.
text.Rotherham: And the Syrians fled from before Israel, and David slew of the Syrians [the men of] seven thousand chariots, and forty thousand footmen,—and, Shophach captain of the army, he put to death.

text.NRSV: When the servants of Hadadezer saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they made peace with David, and became subject to him. So the Arameans were not willing to help the Ammonites any more.
text.Rotherham: And, when the servants of Hadarezer saw that they were defeated before Israel, they made peace with David and served him,—neither would the Syrians help the sons of Ammon any more.

text.NRSV: In the spring of the year, the time when kings go out to battle, Joab led out the army, ravaged the country of the Ammonites, and came and besieged Rabbah. But David remained at Jerusalem. Joab struck Rabbah, and overthrew it.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at the time of the turn of the year, at the time of the going forth of kings, that Joab led forth the force of the army, and laid waste the land of the sons of Ammon, and came and besieged Rabbah, but, David, was tarrying in Jerusalem,—and Joab smote Rabbah, and overthrew it.

text.NRSV: David took the crown of Milcom from his head; he found that it weighed a talent of gold, and in it was a precious stone; and it was placed on David’s head. He also brought out the booty of the city, a very great amount.
text.Rotherham: And David took the crown of their king from off his head, and found it weighed a talent of gold, and, therein, were precious stones, and it was [set] on the head of David,—and, the spoil of the city, brought he forth, in great abundance;

text.NRSV: He brought out the people who were in it, and set them to work with saws and iron picks and axes. Thus David did to all the cities of the Ammonites. Then David and all the people returned to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: and, the people that were therein, brought he forth, and cut with the saw and with threshing sledges of iron and with axes, thus also, used David to do unto all the cities of the son of Ammon,—and David and all the people returned to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: After this, war broke out with the Philistines at Gezer; then Sibbecai the Hushathite killed Sippai, who was one of the descendants of the giants; and the Philistines were subdued.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after this, that there was yet again a battle in Gezer, with the Philistines,—then, Sibbecai the Hushathite smote Sippai of the children of the giants, and they were subdued.

text.NRSV: Again there was war with the Philistines; and Elhanan son of Jair killed Lahmi the brother of Goliath the Gittite, the shaft of whose spear was like a weaver’s beam.
text.Rotherham: And there again came to be a battle with the Philistines,—and Elhanan son of Jair smote Lahmi, brother of Goliath the Gittite, the staff of whose spear, was like a weaver’s beam.

text.NRSV: Again there was war at Gath, where there was a man of great stature, who had six fingers on each hand, and six toes on each foot, twenty-four in number; he also was descended from the giants.
text.Rotherham: And there again came to be war with Gath,—where was a man of great stature, whose fingers and toes, six on each hand and foot, were four and twenty, and, he also, had been born unto the giant.

text.NRSV: When he defied Israel, Jonathan son of Shimea, David’s brother, killed him.
text.Rotherham: But, when he reproached Israel, Jonathan, son of Shimea brother of David, smote him.

text.NRSV: These were descended from the giants in Gath; they fell by the hand of David and his servants.
text.Rotherham: These, were born unto the giant in Gath,—but they fell by the hand of David, and by the hand of his servants.

text.NRSV: Satan stood up against Israel, and incited David to count the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Then the accuser stood up, against Israel,—and moved David, to number Israel.

text.NRSV: So David said to Joab and the commanders of the army, “Go, number Israel, from Beer-sheba to Dan, and bring me a report, so that I may know their number.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Joab and unto the rulers of the people, Go, number Israel, from Beer-sheba even unto Dan,—and bring [the report] unto me, that I may know the number of them.

text.NRSV: But Joab said, “May the Lord increase his people a hundred times! Are they not, my lord the king, all of them my lord’s servants? Why then should my lord require this? Why should he bring guilt on Israel?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Joab, May Yahweh add unto his people, as many as they are, a hundred times, but are they not, my lord O king, all of them my lord’s, as servants? wherefore should my lord seek this? wherefore should it become guilt to Israel?

text.NRSV: But the king’s word prevailed against Joab. So Joab departed and went throughout all Israel, and came back to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: But, the word of the king, prevailed against Joab,—and Joab departed, and went to and fro throughout all Israel, and came to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Joab gave the total number of the people to David. In all Israel there were one million one hundred thousand men who drew the sword, and in Judah four hundred seventy thousand who drew the sword.
text.Rotherham: And Joab delivered the sum of the number of the people, unto David,—and all Israel was found to be a thousand thousand and a hundred thousand men, who drew sword, and Judah, four hundred and seventy thousand men, who drew sword.

text.NRSV: But he did not include Levi and Benjamin in the numbering, for the king’s command was abhorrent to Joab.
text.Rotherham: But, Levi and Benjamin, counted he not among them,—for detestable was the word of the king unto Joab.

text.NRSV: But God was displeased with this thing, and he struck Israel.
text.Rotherham: And it was displeasing in the eyes of God, concerning this thing,—therefore smote he Israel.

text.NRSV: David said to God, “I have sinned greatly in that I have done this thing. But now, I pray you, take away the guilt of your servant; for I have done very foolishly.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto God, I have sinned greatly, in that I have done this thing,—but, now, I beseech thee, take away the iniquity of thy servant, for I have shown myself very foolish.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Gad, David’s seer, saying,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Gad, the seer of David saying:

text.NRSV: “Go and say to David: Thus says the Lord: Three things I offer you; choose one of them, so that I may do it to you.”
text.Rotherham: Go—and thou shalt speak unto David, saying, Thus, saith Yahweh, Three things, do I offer thee,—choose thee one from among them, that I may do it unto thee.

text.NRSV: So Gad came to David and said to him, “Thus says the Lord: Take your choice:
text.Rotherham: So Gad came unto David,—and said to him, Thus, saith Yahweh,

text.NRSV: either three years of famine; or three months of devastation by your foes, while the sword of your enemies overtakes you; or three days of the sword of the Lord, pestilence on the land, and the angel of the Lord destroying throughout all the territory of Israel. Now decide what answer I shall return to the one who sent me.”
text.Rotherham: Choose thee:—Whether, for three years, [there be] famine, Or, for three months, that thou flee before thine adversaries, while the sword of thine enemies overtaketh thee, Or, for three days, the sword of Yahweh, and pestilence be in the land, with, the messenger of Yahweh, laying waste throughout all the boundary of Israel,—Now, therefore, see, what, answer, I shall return unto him that sent me.

text.NRSV: Then David said to Gad, “I am in great distress; let me fall into the hand of the Lord, for his mercy is very great; but let me not fall into human hands.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Gad, I am in a great strait,—Oh let me fall, I pray thee, into the hand of Yahweh, for abounding are his compassions, exceedingly, but, into the hand of man, let me not fall.

text.NRSV: So the Lord sent a pestilence on Israel; and seventy thousand persons fell in Israel.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh laid pestilence, upon Israel,—and there fell, of Israel, seventy thousand men.

text.NRSV: And God sent an angel to Jerusalem to destroy it; but when he was about to destroy it, the Lord looked and relented concerning the calamity; he said to the destroying angel, “Enough! Stay your hand.” The angel of the Lord was then standing by the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
text.Rotherham: And God sent a messenger to Jerusalem, to lay it waste, but, as he was laying it waste, Yahweh looked, and relented concerning the calamity, and said unto the messenger who was laying waste, Enough! now, stay thy hand. And, the messenger of Yahweh, was standing by the threshing-floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

text.NRSV: David looked up and saw the angel of the Lord standing between earth and heaven, and in his hand a drawn sword stretched out over Jerusalem. Then David and the elders, clothed in sackcloth, fell on their faces.
text.Rotherham: And, when David lifted up his eyes, and saw the messenger of Yahweh, standing, between the earth and the heavens, and his sword drawn, in his hand, stretched out over Jerusalem, then fell David and the elders, clothed in sackcloth, upon their faces.

text.NRSV: And David said to God, “Was it not I who gave the command to count the people? It is I who have sinned and done very wickedly. But these sheep, what have they done? Let your hand, I pray, O Lord my God, be against me and against my father’s house; but do not let your people be plagued!”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto God, Was it not, I, who gave word to number the people? Yea, I, it was who sinned and did the great wrong, what then had, these sheep, done? O Yahweh, my God, let thy hand, I beseech thee, be against me, and against the house of my father, but not against, thy people, that they should be plagued!

text.NRSV: Then the angel of the Lord commanded Gad to tell David that David should go up and erect an altar to the Lord on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
text.Rotherham: And, the messenger of Yahweh, commanded Gad, to say unto David,—that David should go up, to rear an altar unto Yahweh, in the threshing-floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

text.NRSV: So David went up at Gad’s word, which he had spoken in the name of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So David went up, according to the word of Gad, which he spake in the name of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Now Ornan turned and saw the angel; and while his four sons who were with him hid themselves, Ornan continued to thresh wheat.
text.Rotherham: And Ornan turned, and saw the messenger, but, his four sons with him, were hiding themselves,—now, Ornan, had been threshing wheat.

text.NRSV: As David came to Ornan, Ornan looked and saw David; he went out from the threshing floor, and did obeisance to David with his face to the ground.
text.Rotherham: Then came David unto Ornan,—and Ornan looked, and saw David, and went forth out of the threshing-floor, and bowed himself unto David, with his face to the ground.

text.NRSV: David said to Ornan, “Give me the site of the threshing floor that I may build on it an altar to the Lord—give it to me at its full price—so that the plague may be averted from the people.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Ornan, Grant me the place of the threshing-floor, that I may build thereon an altar, unto Yahweh,—for silver in full, shalt thou grant it me, that the plague may be stayed from off the people.

text.NRSV: Then Ornan said to David, “Take it; and let my lord the king do what seems good to him; see, I give the oxen for burnt offerings, and the threshing sledges for the wood, and the wheat for a grain offering. I give it all.”
text.Rotherham: And Ornan said unto David, Take it to thee, and let my lord the king do what is good in his own eyes,—see! I have given the oxen for ascending-sacrifices, and, the threshing implements for wood, and the wheat for a meal-offering,—the whole, have I given.

text.NRSV: But King David said to Ornan, “No; I will buy it for the full price. I will not take for the Lord what is yours, nor offer burnt offerings that cost me nothing.”
text.Rotherham: Then said King David to Ornan, Nay! but I will, surely buy, it for silver in full,—for I will not take that which is thine for Yahweh, nor offer an ascending-sacrifice that hath cost me nothing.

text.NRSV: So David paid Ornan six hundred shekels of gold by weight for the site.
text.Rotherham: So David gave to Ornan, for the place,—shekels of gold, by weight, six hundred.

text.NRSV: David built there an altar to the Lord and presented burnt offerings and offerings of well-being. He called upon the Lord, and he answered him with fire from heaven on the altar of burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: And David built there an altar unto Yahweh, and caused to go up ascending-sacrifices, and peace-offerings,—and called unto Yahweh, and he responded to him, by fire, out of the heavens, upon the altar of ascending-sacrifice.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord commanded the angel, and he put his sword back into its sheath.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh gave word to tile messenger, and he put back his sword into its sheath.

text.NRSV: At that time, when David saw that the Lord had answered him at the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite, he made his sacrifices there.
text.Rotherham: At that time, when David saw that Yahweh had answered him in the threshing-floor of Ornan the Jebusite, then he sacrificed there.

text.NRSV: For the tabernacle of the Lord, which Moses had made in the wilderness, and the altar of burnt offering were at that time in the high place at Gibeon;
text.Rotherham: But, the habitation of Yahweh, which Moses made in the desert, and the altar of ascending-sacrifice, were, at that time, in the high place at Gibeon;

text.NRSV: but David could not go before it to inquire of God, for he was afraid of the sword of the angel of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and David could not go before it, to seek God,—for he had been terrified, because of the sword of the messenger of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Then David said, “Here shall be the house of the Lord God and here the altar of burnt offering for Israel.”
text.Rotherham: Then said David, This, is the house of Yahweh God,—and, this, is the altar of ascending-sacrifice, for Israel.

text.NRSV: David gave orders to gather together the aliens who were in the land of Israel, and he set stonecutters to prepare dressed stones for building the house of God.
text.Rotherham: And David gave word, to gather together the sojourners, who were in the land of Israel,—and he set hewers to hew squared stones, for building the house of God;

text.NRSV: David also provided great stores of iron for nails for the doors of the gates and for clamps, as well as bronze in quantities beyond weighing,
text.Rotherham: and, iron in abundance, for nails for the doors of the gates, and for hooks, did David prepare,—and bronze in abundance, without weight;

text.NRSV: and cedar logs without number—for the Sidonians and Tyrians brought great quantities of cedar to David.
text.Rotherham: and cedar-trees, even without number,—for the Zidonians and the Tyreans brought cedar-trees in abundance, unto David.

text.NRSV: For David said, “My son Solomon is young and inexperienced, and the house that is to be built for the Lord must be exceedingly magnificent, famous and glorified throughout all lands; I will therefore make preparation for it.” So David provided abundantly before his death.
text.Rotherham: And David said, Solomon my son, is young and tender, and, the house to be built, must be great and lofty and famous and beautiful, for all lands,—oh let me then prepare for it. So David prepared abundantly, before his death.

text.NRSV: Then he called for his son Solomon and charged him to build a house for the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Then he called for Solomon his son,—and charged him, to build a house, for Yahweh, God of Israel.

text.NRSV: David said to Solomon, “My son, I had planned to build a house to the name of the Lord my God.
text.Rotherham: And David said to Solomon his son,—As for me, it was, near my heart, to build a house for the Name of Yahweh my God;

text.NRSV: But the word of the Lord came to me, saying, ‘You have shed much blood and have waged great wars; you shall not build a house to my name, because you have shed so much blood in my sight on the earth.
text.Rotherham: but the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying, Blood in abundance, hast thou shed, and, great wars, hast thou made,—thou shalt not build a house for my Name, because, much blood, hast thou shed upon the earth, before me.

text.NRSV: See, a son shall be born to you; he shall be a man of peace. I will give him peace from all his enemies on every side; for his name shall be Solomon, and I will give peace and quiet to Israel in his days.
text.Rotherham: Lo! a son, born to thee, He, shall be a man of rest, and I will give him rest from all his enemies, round about,—for, Solomon, shall be his Name, and, peace and quietness, will I bestow upon Israel, in his days;

text.NRSV: He shall build a house for my name. He shall be a son to me, and I will be a father to him, and I will establish his royal throne in Israel forever.’
text.Rotherham: He, shall build a house for my Name, and, he, shall be my son, and, I, will be his father,—therefore will I establish the throne of his kingdom over Israel, unto times age-abiding:

text.NRSV: Now, my son, the Lord be with you, so that you may succeed in building the house of the Lord your God, as he has spoken concerning you.
text.Rotherham: Now, my son, Yahweh be with thee,—so shalt thou be prosperous, and shalt build the house of Yahweh thy God, according as he hath spoken concerning thee.

text.NRSV: Only, may the Lord grant you discretion and understanding, so that when he gives you charge over Israel you may keep the law of the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Only Yahweh give thee discretion and understanding, and give thee charge over Israel,—so that thou take heed unto the law of Yahweh thy God.

text.NRSV: Then you will prosper if you are careful to observe the statutes and the ordinances that the Lord commanded Moses for Israel. Be strong and of good courage. Do not be afraid or dismayed.
text.Rotherham: Then, shalt thou prosper, if thou take heed to do the statutes and the regulations, which Yahweh commanded Moses, concerning Israel,—be strong and bold, thou mayst not be in fear, nor be dismayed.

text.NRSV: With great pains I have provided for the house of the Lord one hundred thousand talents of gold, one million talents of silver, and bronze and iron beyond weighing, for there is so much of it; timber and stone too I have provided. To these you must add more.
text.Rotherham: Lo! then, in my humiliation, have I prepared for the house of Yahweh, of gold a hundred thousand talents, and of silver a thousand thousand talents, and, as for bronze and iron, without weight, for, an abundance, hath it become,—timber also and stone, have I prepared, and, thereunto, thou canst add.

text.NRSV: You have an abundance of workers: stonecutters, masons, carpenters, and all kinds of artisans without number, skilled in working
text.Rotherham: And, with thee, in abundance, are workmen, hewers and carvers of stone and wood,—and every sort of wise man, in all manner of work:

text.NRSV: gold, silver, bronze, and iron. Now begin the work, and the Lord be with you.”
text.Rotherham: for gold and for silver and for bronze and for iron, without number. Arise and do, and Yahweh be with thee!

text.NRSV: David also commanded all the leaders of Israel to help his son Solomon, saying,
text.Rotherham: And David charged all the rulers of Israel, to help Solomon his son:—

text.NRSV: “Is not the Lord your God with you? Has he not given you peace on every side? For he has delivered the inhabitants of the land into my hand; and the land is subdued before the Lord and his people.
text.Rotherham: Is not, Yahweh your God, with you? and hath he not given you rest on every side? for he hath given into my hand, the inhabitants of the land, and subdued is the land before Yahweh, and before his people.

text.NRSV: Now set your mind and heart to seek the Lord your God. Go and build the sanctuary of the Lord God so that the ark of the covenant of the Lord and the holy vessels of God may be brought into a house built to the name of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Now, apply your heart and your soul, to seek unto Yahweh your God,—and then arise ye and build the sanctuary of Yahweh Elohim, that ye may bring the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, and the holy vessels of God, into the house which hath been built for the Name of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: When David was old and full of days, he made his son Solomon king over Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, David, was old, and satisfied with days,—so he made Solomon his son king, over Israel;

text.NRSV: David assembled all the leaders of Israel and the priests and the Levites.
text.Rotherham: and gathered together all the rulers of Israel, with the priests, and the Levites.

text.NRSV: The Levites, thirty years old and upward, were counted, and the total of their males was thirty-eight thousand.
text.Rotherham: Now the Levites were numbered, from thirty years old and upwards,—and their number by their polls, of mature men, was thirty-eight thousand.

text.NRSV: “Twenty-four thousand of these,” David said, “shall have charge of the work in the house of the Lord, six thousand shall be officers and judges,
text.Rotherham: Of these—to preside over the work of the house of Yahweh, were twenty-four thousand,—and officers and judges, six thousand;

text.NRSV: four thousand gatekeepers, and four thousand shall offer praises to the Lord with the instruments that I have made for praise.”
text.Rotherham: and four thousand, were doorkeepers,—and, four thousand, were offerers of praise unto Yahweh, with instruments, which [said David] I made for offering praise.

text.NRSV: And David organized them in divisions corresponding to the sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
text.Rotherham: And David divided them into courses,—pertaining to the sons of Levi, to Gershon, Kohath and Merari.

text.NRSV: For the Gershomites were Ladan and Shimei.
text.Rotherham: Of the Gershonites, Ladan and Shimei.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ladan: Jehiel the chief, Zetham, and Joel, three.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Ladan, the chief Jehiel, and Zetham and Joel, three.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shimei: Shelomoth, Haziel, and Haran, three. These were the heads of families of Ladan.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Shimei, Shelomoth and Haziel and Haran, three,—these, were the ancestral chiefs of Ladan.

text.NRSV: And the sons of Shimei: Jahath, Zina, Jeush, and Beriah. These four were the sons of Shimei.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Shimei, Jahath, Zina, and Jeush, and Beriah,—these, were the sons of Shimei, four.

text.NRSV: Jahath was the chief, and Zizah the second; but Jeush and Beriah did not have many sons, so they were enrolled as a single family.
text.Rotherham: And Jahath was chief, and Zizah the second,—but, Jeush and Beriah, had not many sons, so they became an ancestral house, by one reckoning.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, four.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Kohath, Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, four.

text.NRSV: The sons of Amram: Aaron and Moses. Aaron was set apart to consecrate the most holy things, he and his sons forever, to offer incense before the Lord, and to minister to him and pronounce blessings in his name forever.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Amram, Aaron and Moses,—and Aaron was separated, to hallow the holy of holies, he and his sons, unto times age-abiding, to make a perfume before Yahweh, to be in attendance upon him, and to bless in his name, unto times age-abiding;

text.NRSV: But as for Moses the man of God, his sons were to be reckoned among the tribe of Levi.
text.Rotherham: and, as for Moses, the man of God, his sons, were called unto the tribe of Levi.

text.NRSV: The sons of Moses: Gershom and Eliezer.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Moses, were Gershom, and Eliezer.

text.NRSV: The son of Gershom: Shebuel the chief.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Gershom, were Shebuel the chief;

text.NRSV: The son of Eliezer: Rehabiah the chief; Eliezer had no other sons, but the sons of Rehabiah were very numerous.
text.Rotherham: and the sons of Eliezer were Rehabiah the chief,—and Eliezer had no other sons, but, the sons of Rehabiah, were very many.

text.NRSV: The son of Izhar: Shelomith the chief.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Izhar, Shelomith the chief.

text.NRSV: The sons of Hebron: Jeriah the chief, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Hebron, Jeriah the chief, Amariah the second, Jehaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.

text.NRSV: The sons of Uzziel: Micah the chief and Isshiah the second.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Uzziel, Micah the chief, and Isshiah the second.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The sons of Mahli: Eleazar and Kish.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Merari, were Mahli and Mushi. The sons of Mahli, were Eleazar and Kish.

text.NRSV: Eleazar died having no sons, but only daughters; their kindred, the sons of Kish, married them.
text.Rotherham: But Eleazar died, and had no sons, but daughters only,—and so the sons of Kish their brethren took them.

text.NRSV: The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jeremoth, three.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Mushi, were Mahli and Eder and Jeremoth, three.

text.NRSV: These were the sons of Levi by their ancestral houses, the heads of families as they were enrolled according to the number of the names of the individuals from twenty years old and upward who were to do the work for the service of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: These, were the sons of Levi by their ancestral house, the ancestral chiefs of them who were counted in the number of their names, by their polls, doing the work, for the service of the house of Yahweh,—from twenty years old, and upwards.

text.NRSV: For David said, “The Lord, the God of Israel, has given rest to his people; and he dwells in Jerusalem forever.
text.Rotherham: For, said David, Yahweh the God of Israel hath given rest unto his people,—and hath taken up his habitation in Jerusalem, unto times age-abiding;

text.NRSV: And so the Levites no longer need to carry the tabernacle or any of the vessels for its service.”
text.Rotherham: moreover also, as for the Levites, they have no need to bear the habitation, nor any of its vessels in the laborious work thereof.

text.NRSV: For according to the last words of David, the sons of Levi were numbered from twenty years old and upward.
text.Rotherham: For, by the last words of David, the same, were the number of the sons of Levi,—from twenty years old, and upwards.

text.NRSV: For their duty was to assist the sons of Aaron in the service of the house of the Lord, having the care of the courts and the chambers, the cleansing of all that is holy, and any work for the service of the house of God;
text.Rotherham: For, their duty, was—to be in attendance upon the sons of Aaron, in the laborious work of the house of Yahweh, over the courts, and over the chambers, and over the purifying of everything holy,—and doing the laborious work, of the house of God;

text.NRSV: to assist also with the rows of bread, the choice flour for the grain offering, the wafers of unleavened bread, the baked offering, the offering mixed with oil, and all measures of quantity or size;
text.Rotherham: and for the bread set in array, and for the fine meal for the meal-offering, and for the unleavened cakes, even for that which is baked in a pan, for the moistened food,—even of every measure, and size;

text.NRSV: and to stand every morning, thanking and praising the Lord, and likewise at evening,
text.Rotherham: and to stand, morning by morning, to give thanks and offer praise unto Yahweh,—and, likewise, at even;

text.NRSV: and whenever burnt offerings are offered to the Lord on sabbaths, new moons, and appointed festivals, according to the number required of them, regularly before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and for all the offering of ascending-sacrifices unto Yahweh, on the sabbaths, and on the new moons, and on the set feasts,—by number, according to the manner prescribed for them continually, before Yahweh;—

text.NRSV: Thus they shall keep charge of the tent of meeting and the sanctuary, and shall attend the sons of Aaron, their kindred, for the service of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and they shall keep the charge of the tent of meeting, and the charge of the holy place, and the charge of the sons of Aaron, their brethren,—in the laborious work of the house of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The divisions of the descendants of Aaron were these. The sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: Now, the sons of Aaron, had their courses,—the sons of Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar;

text.NRSV: But Nadab and Abihu died before their father, and had no sons; so Eleazar and Ithamar became the priests.
text.Rotherham: but Nadab and Abihu, died, before their father, and sons, had they none,—but Eleazar and Ithamar became priests;

text.NRSV: Along with Zadok of the sons of Eleazar, and Ahimelech of the sons of Ithamar, David organized them according to the appointed duties in their service.
text.Rotherham: and David apportioned to them courses, both to Zadok of the sons of Eleazar, and to Ahimelech of the sons of Ithamar,—by their appointed place in their service.

text.NRSV: Since more chief men were found among the sons of Eleazar than among the sons of Ithamar, they organized them under sixteen heads of ancestral houses of the sons of Eleazar, and eight of the sons of Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: But the sons of Eleazar were found to be more numerous, by the chiefs of their able men, than were the sons of Ithamar, so they divided them,—the sons of Eleazar, had chiefs, of the ancestral house, sixteen, whereas, the sons of Ithamar, had of their ancestral house, eight.

text.NRSV: They organized them by lot, all alike, for there were officers of the sanctuary and officers of God among both the sons of Eleazar and the sons of Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: They divided them, therefore, by [casting] lots, these with those,—for there were princes of the sanctuary, and princes of God, from among the sons of Eleazar, and among the sons of Ithamar.

text.NRSV: The scribe Shemaiah son of Nethanel, a Levite, recorded them in the presence of the king, and the officers, and Zadok the priest, and Ahimelech son of Abiathar, and the heads of ancestral houses of the priests and of the Levites; one ancestral house being chosen for Eleazar and one chosen for Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: And Shemaiah son of Nethanel the scribe from among the Levites, wrote them down, before the king and the rulers and Zadok the priest, and Ahimelech son of Abiathar, and the ancestral chiefs, pertaining to the priests and to the Levites,—one ancestral house, was taken for Eleazar, and, was equally taken, for Ithamar.

text.NRSV: The first lot fell to Jehoiarib, the second to Jedaiah,
text.Rotherham: So then the first lot came forth for Jehoiarib, for Jedaiah, the second;

text.NRSV: the third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim,
text.Rotherham: for Harim, the third, for Seorim, the fourth;

text.NRSV: the fifth to Malchijah, the sixth to Mijamin,
text.Rotherham: for Malchijah, the fifth, for Mijamin, the sixth;

text.NRSV: the seventh to Hakkoz, the eighth to Abijah,
text.Rotherham: for Hakkoz, the seventh, for Abijah, the eighth;

text.NRSV: the ninth to Jeshua, the tenth to Shecaniah,
text.Rotherham: for Jeshua, the ninth, for Shecaniah, the tenth;

text.NRSV: the eleventh to Eliashib, the twelfth to Jakim,
text.Rotherham: for Eliashib, the eleventh, for Jakim, the twelfth;

text.NRSV: the thirteenth to Huppah, the fourteenth to Jeshebeab,
text.Rotherham: for Huppah, the thirteenth, for Jeshebeab, the fourteenth;

text.NRSV: the fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immer,
text.Rotherham: for Bilgah, the fifteenth, for Immer, the sixteenth;

text.NRSV: the seventeenth to Hezir, the eighteenth to Happizzez,
text.Rotherham: for Hezir, the seventeenth, for Happizzez, the eighteenth;

text.NRSV: the nineteenth to Pethahiah, the twentieth to Jehezkel,
text.Rotherham: for Pethahiah, the nineteenth, for Jehezkel, the twentieth;

text.NRSV: the twenty-first to Jachin, the twenty-second to Gamul,
text.Rotherham: for Jachin, the twenty-first, for Gamul, the twenty-second;

text.NRSV: the twenty-third to Delaiah, the twenty-fourth to Maaziah.
text.Rotherham: for Delaiah, the twenty-third, for Maaziah, the twenty-fourth.

text.NRSV: These had as their appointed duty in their service to enter the house of the Lord according to the procedure established for them by their ancestor Aaron, as the Lord God of Israel had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: These, were their appointed places for their service, for entering the house of Yahweh, according to the regulation of them, by the hand of Aaron their father,—just as Yahweh God of Israel, commanded him.

text.NRSV: And of the rest of the sons of Levi: of the sons of Amram, Shubael; of the sons of Shubael, Jehdeiah.
text.Rotherham: Now, as for the sons of Levi who remained, of the sons of Amram, Shubael, of the sons of Shubael, Jehdeiah.

text.NRSV: Of Rehabiah: of the sons of Rehabiah, Isshiah the chief.
text.Rotherham: Of Rehabiah, of the sons of Rehabiah, the chief Isshiah;

text.NRSV: Of the Izharites, Shelomoth; of the sons of Shelomoth, Jahath.
text.Rotherham: of the Izharites, Shelomoth,—of the sons of Shelomoth, Jahath;

text.NRSV: The sons of Hebron: Jeriah the chief, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, Jekameam the fourth.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons [of Hebron], Jeriah,—Amariah, the second, Jehaziel, the third, Jekameam, the fourth;

text.NRSV: The son of Uzziel, Micah; of the sons of Micah, Shamir.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Uzziel, Micah, of the sons of Micah, Shamir;

text.NRSV: The brother of Micah, Isshiah; of the sons of Isshiah, Zechariah.
text.Rotherham: the brother of Micah, Isshiah, of the sons of Isshiah, Zechariah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The son of Jaaziah: Beno.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Merari, Mahli and Mushi,—the sons of Jaaziah, Beno;

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: of Jaaziah, Beno, Shoham, Zaccur, and Ibri.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Merari, of Jaaziah, Beno, and Shoham and Zaccur and Ibri:

text.NRSV: Of Mahli: Eleazar, who had no sons.
text.Rotherham: of Mahli, Eleazar, who had no sons;

text.NRSV: Of Kish, the son of Kish: Jerahmeel.
text.Rotherham: of Kish—the sons of Kish, Jerameel;

text.NRSV: The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the sons of the Levites according to their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Mushi, Mahli and Eder and Jerimoth. These, were the sons of the Levites, belonging to their ancestral house.

text.NRSV: These also cast lots just as their kindred the sons of Aaron did in the presence of King David, Zadok, Ahimelech, and the heads of ancestral houses of the priests and of the Levites, the chief as well as the youngest brother, casting lots alike.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, they also, cast lots along with their brethren the sons of Aaron, before David the king and Zadok and Ahimelech, and the ancestral chiefs, pertaining to the priests and to the Levites, the ancestral chief along with his younger brethren.

text.NRSV: David and the officers of the army also set apart for the service the sons of Asaph, and of Heman, and of Jeduthun, who should prophesy with lyres, harps, and cymbals. The list of those who did the work and of their duties was:
text.Rotherham: And David and the captains of the host set apart for service, unto the sons of Asaph and Heman and Jeduthun, such as should prophesy, with lyres, with harps, and with cymbals,—and, the number of the workers, for their service, was:

text.NRSV: Of the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asarelah, sons of Asaph, under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the direction of the king.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Asaph, Zaccur and Joseph and Nethaniah and Asarelah, the sons of Asaph,—under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the direction of the king.

text.NRSV: Of Jeduthun, the sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, Zeri, Jeshaiah, Shimei, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, six, under the direction of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with the lyre in thanksgiving and praise to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Of Jeduthun, the sons of Jeduthun,—Gedaliah, and Zeri, and Jeshaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah—six, under the direction of their father Jeduthun, with the lyre, as he prophesied, in giving thanks and offering praise unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Of Heman, the sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, Mahazioth.
text.Rotherham: Of Heman, the sons of Heman—Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Jeremoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, Mahazioth.

text.NRSV: All these were the sons of Heman the king’s seer, according to the promise of God to exalt him; for God had given Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
text.Rotherham: All these, were sons of Heman, the seer of the king in the things of God, at the lifting of the horn. And God gave to Heman, fourteen sons and three daughters,

text.NRSV: They were all under the direction of their father for the music in the house of the Lord with cymbals, harps, and lyres for the service of the house of God. Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman were under the order of the king.
text.Rotherham: All these, were under the direction of their father, in the singing of the house of Yahweh, with cymbals, harps and lyres, for the service of the house of God,—Asaph and Jeduthun and Heman, under the direction of the king.

text.NRSV: The number of them along with their kindred, who were trained in singing to the Lord, all of whom were skillful, was two hundred eighty-eight.
text.Rotherham: And, the number of them—with their brethren, who were instructed in singing unto Yahweh,—even all the skilful, was two hundred and eighty-eight.

text.NRSV: And they cast lots for their duties, small and great, teacher and pupil alike.
text.Rotherham: So they cast lots for their charges, all alike, as the small so the great, the teacher with the learner.

text.NRSV: The first lot fell for Asaph to Joseph; the second to Gedaliah, to him and his kindred and his sons, twelve;
text.Rotherham: And the first lot came forth for Asaph, to Joseph,—Gedaliah, the second, he and his brethren and his sons, twelve.

text.NRSV: the third to Zaccur, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The third, Zaccur, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the fourth to Izri, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The fourth, to Izri, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the fifth to Nethaniah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The fifth, Nethaniah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the sixth to Bukkiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The sixth, Bukkiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the seventh to Jesarelah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The seventh, Jesharelah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the eighth to Jeshaiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The eighth, Jeshaiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the ninth to Mattaniah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The ninth, Mattaniah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the tenth to Shimei, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The tenth, Shimei, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the eleventh to Azarel, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The eleventh, Azarel, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the twelfth to Hashabiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The twelfth, to Hashabiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the thirteenth to Shubael, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the thirteenth, Shubael, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the fourteenth to Mattithiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the fourteenth, Mattithiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the fifteenth to Jeremoth, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the fifteenth, to Jeremoth, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the sixteenth to Hananiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the sixteenth, to Hananiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the seventeenth to Joshbekashah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the seventeenth, to Joshbekashah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the eighteenth to Hanani, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the eighteenth, to Hanani, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the nineteenth to Mallothi, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the nineteenth, to Mallothi, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the twentieth to Eliathah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twentieth, to Eliathah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-first to Hothir, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-first, to Hothir, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-second to Giddalti, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-second, to Giddalti, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-third to Mahazioth, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-third, to Mahazioth, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-fourth to Romamti-ezer, his sons and his kindred, twelve.
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-fourth, to Romamti-ezer, his sons and his brethren, twelve.

text.NRSV: As for the divisions of the gatekeepers: of the Korahites, Meshelemiah son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph.
text.Rotherham: To the courses of the door-keepers, pertained,—of the Korahites, Meshelemiah son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph;

text.NRSV: Meshelemiah had sons: Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,
text.Rotherham: and, Meshelemiah, had sons,—Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth;

text.NRSV: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Eliehoenai the seventh.
text.Rotherham: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Eliehoenai the seventh.

text.NRSV: Obed-edom had sons: Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, Sachar the fourth, Nethanel the fifth,
text.Rotherham: And, Obed-edom, had sons,—Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Josh the third, and Sacar the fourth, and Nethanel the fifth;

text.NRSV: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peullethai the eighth; for God blessed him.
text.Rotherham: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peullethai, the eighth,—for God had blessed him.

text.NRSV: Also to his son Shemaiah were sons born who exercised authority in their ancestral houses, for they were men of great ability.
text.Rotherham: And, to Shemaiah his son, were born sons, who bare rule to their ancestral house,—for, heroes of valour, were they.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shemaiah: Othni, Rephael, Obed, and Elzabad, whose brothers were able men, Elihu and Semachiah.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Shemaiah, were Othni and Rephael, and Obed, Elzabad, whose brethren were sons of valour,—Elihu, and Semachiah.

text.NRSV: All these were of the sons of Obed-edom with their sons and kindred, able men qualified for the service; sixty-two of Obed-edom.
text.Rotherham: All these, were of the sons of Obed-edom, they and their sons and their brethren, able men in strength for the service,—sixty-two, pertaining to Obed-edom.

text.NRSV: Meshelemiah had sons and kindred, able men, eighteen.
text.Rotherham: And, Meshelemiah, had sons, and brethren, sons of valour, eighteen.

text.NRSV: Hosah, of the sons of Merari, had sons: Shimri the chief (for though he was not the firstborn, his father made him chief),
text.Rotherham: And, Hosah of the sons of Merari, had sons,—Shimri the chief, for though he was not the firstborn, yet his father, made him, chief.

text.NRSV: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth; all the sons and kindred of Hosah were thirteen.
text.Rotherham: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth,—all the sons and brethren of Hosah, were thirteen.

text.NRSV: These divisions of the gatekeepers, through their chief men, had duties, just as their kindred did, ministering in the house of the Lord;
text.Rotherham: To these, belonged the courses of door-keepers, to the chiefs of the strong men, belonged charges, equally with their brethren,—to be in attendance in the house of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: and they cast lots by ancestral houses, small and great alike, for their gates.
text.Rotherham: Yea they cast lots, as the small so the great, by their ancestral house, for every several gate.

text.NRSV: The lot for the east fell to Shelemiah. Then they cast lots for his son Zechariah, a prudent counselor, and his lot came out for the north.
text.Rotherham: And the lot eastward fell to Shelemiah. And, for Zechariah his son, a discreet counsellor, they cast lots, and his lot came out, northward.

text.NRSV: Obed-edom’s came out for the south, and to his sons was allotted the storehouse.
text.Rotherham: To Obed-edom, southward,—and, to his sons, the storehouse.

text.NRSV: For Shuppim and Hosah it came out for the west, at the gate of Shallecheth on the ascending road. Guard corresponded to guard.
text.Rotherham: To Shuppim and to Hosah, westward, near the refuse-gate, in the causeway that goeth up,—one ward as well as another.

text.NRSV: On the east there were six Levites each day, on the north four each day, on the south four each day, as well as two and two at the storehouse;
text.Rotherham: Eastward—daily, were six,—northward—daily, four, southward—daily, four, and, for the stores, two and two.

text.NRSV: and for the colonnade on the west there were four at the road and two at the colonnade.
text.Rotherham: For the precinct—westward, four, at the causeway, two—by the precinct.

text.NRSV: These were the divisions of the gatekeepers among the Korahites and the sons of Merari.
text.Rotherham: These, were the courses of the door-keepers, of the sons of the Korahites, and of the sons of Merari.

text.NRSV: And of the Levites, Ahijah had charge of the treasuries of the house of God and the treasuries of the dedicated gifts.
text.Rotherham: And, the Levites, their brethren, were over the treasuries of the house of God, even to the treasuries of hallowed things.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ladan, the sons of the Gershonites belonging to Ladan, the heads of ancestral houses belonging to Ladan the Gershonite: Jehieli.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Ladan—the sons of the Gershonites belonging to Ladan—the ancestral chiefs belonging to Ladan the Gershonite, were Jehieli;

text.NRSV: The sons of Jehieli, Zetham and his brother Joel, had charge of the treasuries of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Jehieli, Zetham and Joel his brother who were over the treasuries of the house of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Of the Amramites, the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites:
text.Rotherham: Then came certain of the Amramites, of the Izharites, of the Hebronites, of the Uzzielites.

text.NRSV: Shebuel son of Gershom, son of Moses, was chief officer in charge of the treasuries.
text.Rotherham: And, Shebuel, son of Gershom, son of Moses, was chief ruler over the treasuries.

text.NRSV: His kindred by Eliezer: his son Rehabiah, and his son Jeshaiah, and his son Joram, and his son Zichri, and his son Shelomoth.
text.Rotherham: And, his brethren pertaining to Eliezer, were Rehabiah his son, and Jeshaiah his son, and Joram his son, and Zichri his son, and Shelomoth his son.

text.NRSV: This Shelomoth and his kindred were in charge of all the treasuries of the dedicated gifts that King David, and the heads of ancestral houses, and the officers of the thousands and the hundreds, and the commanders of the army, had dedicated.
text.Rotherham: The same Shelomoth and his brethren, were over all the treasuries of the hallowed things, which David the king and the ancestral chiefs, with the captains of thousands and hundreds and the captains of the army, had hallowed:

text.NRSV: From booty won in battles they dedicated gifts for the maintenance of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: out of the battles and out of the spoil, had they hallowed [them],—to strengthen [the provision] for the house of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Also all that Samuel the seer, and Saul son of Kish, and Abner son of Ner, and Joab son of Zeruiah had dedicated—all dedicated gifts were in the care of Shelomoth and his kindred.
text.Rotherham: And, all that Samuel the seer, and Saul son of Kish, and Abner son of Ner, and Joab son of Zeruiah, had hallowed, whosoever had hallowed anything, it was under the direction of Shelomoth, and his brethren.

text.NRSV: Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons were appointed to outside duties for Israel, as officers and judges.
text.Rotherham: Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons, were for the outward business over Israel,—as officers, and as judges.

text.NRSV: Of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his kindred, one thousand seven hundred able men, had the oversight of Israel west of the Jordan for all the work of the Lord and for the service of the king.
text.Rotherham: Of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his brethren, sons of valour, one thousand seven hundred, had the oversight of Israel, across the Jordan westward,—for all the business of Yahweh, and for the service of the king.

text.NRSV: Of the Hebronites, Jerijah was chief of the Hebronites of whatever genealogy or ancestral house. In the fortieth year of David’s reign search was made and men of great ability among them were found at Jazer in Gilead.
text.Rotherham: Of the Hebronites, Jerijah, was chief, [he was] of the Hebronites by his generations, by his fathers,—in the fortieth year of the reign of David, were they sought out, and there were found among them heroes of valour, in Jazer of Gilead;

text.NRSV: King David appointed him and his kindred, two thousand seven hundred able men, heads of ancestral houses, to have the oversight of the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of the Manassites, for everything pertaining to God and for the affairs of the king.
text.Rotherham: and, his brethren that were heroes of valour, were two thousand and seven hundred, ancestral chiefs,—so David the king gave them oversight, over the Reubenites and the Gadites and the half tribe of Manassites, in all the affairs of God, and the affairs of the king.

text.NRSV: This is the list of the people of Israel, the heads of ancestral houses, the commanders of the thousands and the hundreds, and their officers who served the king in all matters of the divisions that came on duty and went off duty month by month throughout the year, each division numbering twenty-four thousand:
text.Rotherham: Now, the sons of Israel—as to the number of them, the ancestral chiefs—and rulers of thousands and hundreds, and their officers who waited upon the king as to any matter of the courses, who came in and went out month by month, for all the months of the year, in each course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: Jashobeam son of Zabdiel was in charge of the first division, for the first month; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: Over the first course, for the first month, was Jashobeam, son of Zabdiel,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: He was a descendant of Perez, and was chief of all the commanders of the army for the first month.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Perez, was the chief for all the rulers of the hosts, for the first month.

text.NRSV: Dodai the Ahohite was in charge of the division of the second month; Mikloth was the chief officer of his division. In his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: And, over the course for the second month, was Dodai an Ahohite, and, of his course, was Mikloth also a chief ruler,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: The third commander, for the third month, was Benaiah son of the chief priest Jehoiada; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The ruler of the third host, for the third month, was Benaiah, son of Jehoiada the priest—a chief,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: This is the Benaiah who was a mighty warrior among the Thirty and in command of the Thirty; his son Ammizabad was in charge of his division.
text.Rotherham: The same Benaiah, was a hero of thirty, and over the thirty,—and, over his course, was Ammizabad his son.

text.NRSV: Asahel brother of Joab was fourth, for the fourth month, and his son Zebadiah after him; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The fourth, for the fourth month, was Asahel, brother of Joab, and Zebadiah his son, after him,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: The fifth commander, for the fifth month, was Shamhuth the Izrahite; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: Of the fifth, for the fifth month, the ruler, was Shamhuth the Izrahite,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: Sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira son of Ikkesh of Tekoa; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira son of Ikkesh, the Tekoite,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: Seventh, for the seventh month, was Helez the Pelonite, of the Ephraimites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The seventh, for the seventh month, was Helez the Pelonite, of the sons of Ephraim,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: Eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zerahites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zerahites,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: Ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer of Anathoth, a Benjaminite; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer the Anathothite, of the Benjamites,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: Tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai of Netophah, of the Zerahites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai the Netophathite, of the Zerahites,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: Eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah of Pirathon, of the Ephraimites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the sons of Ephraim,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: Twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.

text.NRSV: Over the tribes of Israel, for the Reubenites, Eliezer son of Zichri was chief officer; for the Simeonites, Shephatiah son of Maacah;
text.Rotherham: Furthermore, over the tribes of Israel, the chief ruler of the Reubenites, was Eliezer, son of Zichri. Of the Simeonites, Shephatiah, son of Maacah.

text.NRSV: for Levi, Hashabiah son of Kemuel; for Aaron, Zadok;
text.Rotherham: Of Levi, Hashabiah, son of Kemuel. Of Aaron, Zadok.

text.NRSV: for Judah, Elihu, one of David’s brothers; for Issachar, Omri son of Michael;
text.Rotherham: Of Judah, Elihu, one of the brethren of David. Of Issachar, Omri, son of Michael.

text.NRSV: for Zebulun, Ishmaiah son of Obadiah; for Naphtali, Jerimoth son of Azriel;
text.Rotherham: Of Zebulun, Ishmaiah, son of Obadiah. Of Naphtali, Jeremoth, son of Azriel.

text.NRSV: for the Ephraimites, Hoshea son of Azaziah; for the half-tribe of Manasseh, Joel son of Pedaiah;
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Azaziah. Of the half tribe of Manasseh, Joel, son of Pedaiah.

text.NRSV: for the half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo son of Zechariah; for Benjamin, Jaasiel son of Abner;
text.Rotherham: Of the half of Manasseh, in Gilead, Iddo, son of Zechariah. Of Benjamin, Jaasiel, son of Abner.

text.NRSV: for Dan, Azarel son of Jeroham. These were the leaders of the tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Of Dan, Azarel, son of Jeroham. These, were the rulers of the tribes of Israel.

text.NRSV: David did not count those below twenty years of age, for the Lord had promised to make Israel as numerous as the stars of heaven.
text.Rotherham: But David took not the number of them, from twenty years old, and under,—because Yahweh had said, he would multiply Israel like the stars of the heavens.

text.NRSV: Joab son of Zeruiah began to count them, but did not finish; yet wrath came upon Israel for this, and the number was not entered into the account of the Annals of King David.
text.Rotherham: Joab son of Zeruiah, began to number, but finished not, when there arose, on this account, indignation against Israel,—neither did the number come up into the account of the chronicles of King David.

text.NRSV: Over the king’s treasuries was Azmaveth son of Adiel. Over the treasuries in the country, in the cities, in the villages, and in the towers, was Jonathan son of Uzziah.
text.Rotherham: And, over the treasuries of the king, was Azmaveth, son of Adiel. And, over the treasuries in the fields, in the cities, in the villages and in the castles, was Jonathan, son of Uzziah;

text.NRSV: Over those who did the work of the field, tilling the soil, was Ezri son of Chelub.
text.Rotherham: And, over the workers of the field, for the tillage of the ground, was Ezri, son of Chelub.

text.NRSV: Over the vineyards was Shimei the Ramathite. Over the produce of the vineyards for the wine cellars was Zabdi the Shiphmite.
text.Rotherham: And, over the vineyards, was Shimei, the Ramathite. And, over that which was in the vineyards, for the treasuries of wine, was Zabdi, the Shiphmite.

text.NRSV: Over the olive and sycamore trees in the Shephelah was Baal-hanan the Gederite. Over the stores of oil was Joash.
text.Rotherham: And, over the olive-trees and the sycamore-trees that were in the lowland, was Baal-hanan the Gederite. And, over the treasuries of oil, was Joash.

text.NRSV: Over the herds that pastured in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite. Over the herds in the valleys was Shaphat son of Adlai.
text.Rotherham: And, over the herds that pastured in Sharon, was Shitrai, the Sharonite. And, over the herds in the vales, Shaphat, son of Adlai.

text.NRSV: Over the camels was Obil the Ishmaelite. Over the donkeys was Jehdeiah the Meronothite.
text.Rotherham: And, over the camels, was Obil, the Ishmaelite. And, over the asses, was Jehdeyahu the Meronothite.

text.NRSV: Over the flocks was Jaziz the Hagrite. All these were stewards of King David’s property.
text.Rotherham: And, over the flocks, was Jaziz, the Hagrite. All these, were rulers over the possessions that belonged to King David.

text.NRSV: Jonathan, David’s uncle, was a counselor, being a man of understanding and a scribe; Jehiel son of Hachmoni attended the king’s sons.
text.Rotherham: And, Jonathan the relative of David, was a counsellor, a man of understanding and a scribe, was he. And, Jehiel son of Hachmoni, was with the sons of the king.

text.NRSV: Ahithophel was the king’s counselor, and Hushai the Archite was the king’s friend.
text.Rotherham: And, Ahitophel, was counsellor to the king. And, Hushai the Archite, was the companion of the king.

text.NRSV: Ahithophel was succeeded by Jehoiada son of Benaiah, and Abiathar. Joab was commander of the king’s army.
text.Rotherham: And, after Ahitophel, was Jehoiada son of Benaiah—and Abiathar. And, the captain of the king’s army, was Joab.

text.NRSV: David assembled at Jerusalem all the officials of Israel, the officials of the tribes, the officers of the divisions that served the king, the commanders of the thousands, the commanders of the hundreds, the stewards of all the property and cattle of the king and his sons, together with the palace officials, the mighty warriors, and all the warriors.
text.Rotherham: And David called together all the rulers of Israel, the rulers of tribes and the rulers of the courses who waited upon the king,—and the rulers of thousands and the rulers of hundreds, and the rulers over all the possessions and substance that belonged to the king and to his sons, with the courtiers and the heroes, even every hero of valour, unto Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Then King David rose to his feet and said: “Hear me, my brothers and my people. I had it in my heart to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of the Lord, for the footstool of our God; and I made preparations for building.
text.Rotherham: Then David the king rose up on his feet, and said, Hear me, my brethren and my people,—As for me, it was near my heart, to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, and for the footstool of our God, and I made ready to build.

text.NRSV: But God said to me, ‘You shall not build a house for my name, for you are a warrior and have shed blood.’
text.Rotherham: But, God, said to me, Thou shalt not build a house for my Name,—for, a man of war, thou art, and, blood, hast thou shed.

text.NRSV: Yet the Lord God of Israel chose me from all my ancestral house to be king over Israel forever; for he chose Judah as leader, and in the house of Judah my father’s house, and among my father’s sons he took delight in making me king over all Israel.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit Yahweh, God of Israel, made choice of me, out of all of the house of my father, to become king over Israel unto times age-abiding, for, of Judah, had he made choice to be chief ruler, and, in the house of Judah, the house of my father,—and, among the sons of my father, with me, was he well-pleased, to make me king over all Israel;

text.NRSV: And of all my sons (for the Lord has given me many sons) he has chosen my son Solomon to sit upon the throne of the kingdom of the Lord over Israel.
text.Rotherham: and, from among all my sons,—for, many sons, hath Yahweh given me,—he hath made choice of Solomon my son, to sit upon the throne of the kingdom of Yahweh, over Israel.

text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘It is your son Solomon who shall build my house and my courts, for I have chosen him to be a son to me, and I will be a father to him.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto am, Solomon thy son—he, shall build my house, and my courts,—for I have made choice of him that he may be my son, and that, I, may be his father.

text.NRSV: I will establish his kingdom forever if he continues resolute in keeping my commandments and my ordinances, as he is today.’
text.Rotherham: So will I establish his kingdom, unto times age-abiding,—if he be strong, to do my commandments and my regulations, as at this day.

text.NRSV: Now therefore in the sight of all Israel, the assembly of the Lord, and in the hearing of our God, observe and search out all the commandments of the Lord your God; that you may possess this good land, and leave it for an inheritance to your children after you forever.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, in the eyes of all Israel—the convocation of Yahweh, and in the ears of our God, Observe and seek all the commandments of Yahweh your God,—to the end that ye may possess the good land,—and suffer your sons after you to inherit it, unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: “And you, my son Solomon, know the God of your father, and serve him with a single mind and a willing heart; for the Lord searches every mind, and understands every plan and thought. If you seek him, he will be found by you; but if you forsake him, he will abandon you forever.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, Solomon my son, Know thou the God of thy father, and serve him with a perfect heart and with a willing soul, for, all hearts, doth Yahweh search, and, every devised purpose, doth he understand,—If thou seek him, He will be found of thee, but, if thou forsake him, He will cast thee off for ever.

text.NRSV: Take heed now, for the Lord has chosen you to build a house as the sanctuary; be strong, and act.”
text.Rotherham: See! now, that, Yahweh, hath made choice of thee, to build a house for a sanctuary—be strong and do!

text.NRSV: Then David gave his son Solomon the plan of the vestibule of the temple, and of its houses, its treasuries, its upper rooms, and its inner chambers, and of the room for the mercy seat;
text.Rotherham: Then gave David unto Solomon his son, the plan of the porch, and the recesses thereof, and the treasuries thereof, and the upper rooms thereof, and the inner chambers thereof, and the recess for the propitiatory;

text.NRSV: and the plan of all that he had in mind: for the courts of the house of the Lord, all the surrounding chambers, the treasuries of the house of God, and the treasuries for dedicated gifts;
text.Rotherham: and the plan of all which had come by the spirit to be with him, for the courts of the house of Yahweh, and for all the rooms round about,—for the treasuries of the house of God, and for the treasuries of hallowed things;

text.NRSV: for the divisions of the priests and of the Levites, and all the work of the service in the house of the Lord; for all the vessels for the service in the house of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: and for the courses of the priests and the Levites, and for all the work of the service of the house of Yahweh,—and for all the utensils of the service of the house of Yahweh:

text.NRSV: the weight of gold for all golden vessels for each service, the weight of silver vessels for each service,
text.Rotherham: of gold, by weight, for the gold, for all manner of utensils for each several service,—for all manner of utensils of silver, by weight, for all manner of utensils for each several service;

text.NRSV: the weight of the golden lampstands and their lamps, the weight of gold for each lampstand and its lamps, the weight of silver for a lampstand and its lamps, according to the use of each lampstand in the service,
text.Rotherham: and a weight, for the lampstands of gold, and their lamps of gold, by the weight of each several lampstand, and the lamps thereof,—and for the lampstands of silver by weight, for each lampstand and the lamps thereof, according to the service of each several lampstand.

text.NRSV: the weight of gold for each table for the rows of bread, the silver for the silver tables,
text.Rotherham: And the gold [gave he] by weight for the tables for setting in array, for each several table,—and silver, for the tables of silver;

text.NRSV: and pure gold for the forks, the basins, and the cups; for the golden bowls and the weight of each; for the silver bowls and the weight of each;
text.Rotherham: and the fleshhooks and the bowls and the cups, pure gold,—and for the goblets of gold, by weight for each several goblet, and for the goblets of silver, by weight for each several goblet;

text.NRSV: for the altar of incense made of refined gold, and its weight; also his plan for the golden chariot of the cherubim that spread their wings and covered the ark of the covenant of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and, for the altar of incense, refined gold, by weight,—and, for the pattern of the chariot of the cherubim, gold, for them that were spreading out, and covering the ark of the covenant of Yahweh:—

text.NRSV: “All this, in writing at the Lord’s direction, he made clear to me—the plan of all the works.”
text.Rotherham: the whole in writing, From the hand of Yahweh upon me, to give understanding,—all the works of the pattern.

text.NRSV: David said further to his son Solomon, “Be strong and of good courage, and act. Do not be afraid or dismayed; for the Lord God, my God, is with you. He will not fail you or forsake you, until all the work for the service of the house of the Lord is finished.
text.Rotherham: Then said David, unto Solomon his son, Be strong and bold, and act, thou mayest not fear nor be dismayed,—for, Yahweh, God, my God, will be with thee, he will not let thee go, neither will he forsake thee, until thou hast finished all the work of the service of the house of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The divisions of the priests and the Levites are ready for all the service of the house of God; and with you in all the work will be every volunteer who has skill for any kind of service; also the officers and all the people will be wholly at your command.”
text.Rotherham: And there are the courses of the priests and the Levites, for all the service of the house of God,—and, with thee, in all manner of work, is every willing, skilful man for any service, also the rulers and all the people, for all thine affairs.

text.NRSV: King David said to the whole assembly, “My son Solomon, whom alone God has chosen, is young and inexperienced, and the work is great; for the temple will not be for mortals but for the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Then said David the king unto all the convocation, Solomon my son, the one, of whom God hath made choice, is young and tender,—and, the work, is great, seeing that, not for man, is the palace, but for Yahweh Elohim.

text.NRSV: So I have provided for the house of my God, so far as I was able, the gold for the things of gold, the silver for the things of silver, the bronze for the things of bronze, the iron for the things of iron, and wood for the things of wood, besides great quantities of onyx and stones for setting, antimony, colored stones, all sorts of precious stones, and marble in abundance.
text.Rotherham: And, with all my might, have I made preparation for the house of my God, the gold for the gold, and the silver for the silver, and the bronze for the bronze, the iron for the iron, and the wood for the wood,—and beryl stones and settings, stones coloured and particoloured, and all manner of precious stones and stones of white marble, in abundance.

text.NRSV: Moreover, in addition to all that I have provided for the holy house, I have a treasure of my own of gold and silver, and because of my devotion to the house of my God I give it to the house of my God:
text.Rotherham: And yet further, because of my delighting in the house of my God, having a treasure of mine own in gold and silver, I have given for the house of my God, above and beyond all that I have prepared for the holy house:

text.NRSV: three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir, and seven thousand talents of refined silver, for overlaying the walls of the house,
text.Rotherham: three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir,—and seven thousand talents of refined silver, for overlaying the walls of the recesses.

text.NRSV: and for all the work to be done by artisans, gold for the things of gold and silver for the things of silver. Who then will offer willingly, consecrating themselves today to the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: Of gold for the gold, and of silver for the silver, and for all manner of work in the hand of artificers,—Who then is ready to offer willingly, by filling his hand to-day, unto Yahweh?

text.NRSV: Then the heads of ancestral houses made their freewill offerings, as did also the leaders of the tribes, the commanders of the thousands and of the hundreds, and the officers over the king’s work.
text.Rotherham: Then offered they willingly—the ancestral rulers and the rulers of the tribes of Israel, and the rulers of thousands and hundreds, even to the rulers of the work of the king;

text.NRSV: They gave for the service of the house of God five thousand talents and ten thousand darics of gold, ten thousand talents of silver, eighteen thousand talents of bronze, and one hundred thousand talents of iron.
text.Rotherham: and gave for the service of the house of God, of gold, five thousand talents and ten thousand drams, and, of silver, ten thousand talents, and, of bronze, eighteen thousand talents,—and, of iron, one hundred thousand talents.

text.NRSV: Whoever had precious stones gave them to the treasury of the house of the Lord, into the care of Jehiel the Gershonite.
text.Rotherham: And, every one with whom were found precious stones, gave to the treasure of the house of Yahweh,—unto the hand of Jehiel the Gershonite.

text.NRSV: Then the people rejoiced because these had given willingly, for with a whole heart they had offered freely to the Lord; King David also rejoiced greatly.
text.Rotherham: So the people rejoiced because they willingly offered, for, with a perfect heart, offered they willingly unto Yahweh,—moreover also, David the king, rejoiced with great joy.

text.NRSV: Then David blessed the Lord in the presence of all the assembly; David said: “Blessed are you, O Lord, the God of our ancestor Israel, forever and ever.
text.Rotherham: Therefore did David bless Yahweh in the eyes of all the convocation,—and David said, Blessed art thou, O Yahweh, the God of Israel our father, from age even unto age.

text.NRSV: Yours, O Lord, are the greatness, the power, the glory, the victory, and the majesty; for all that is in the heavens and on the earth is yours; yours is the kingdom, O Lord, and you are exalted as head above all.
text.Rotherham: Thine, O Yahweh, are Greatness, and Might, and Beauty, and Victory, and Majesty, nay! all in the heavens and in the earth,—thine, O Yahweh, is the kingdom, who art exalted above all,—as chief;

text.NRSV: Riches and honor come from you, and you rule over all. In your hand are power and might; and it is in your hand to make great and to give strength to all.
text.Rotherham: and, riches and honour, are from before thee, and, thou, art ruling over all, and, in thy hand, are power and might,—and, in thy hand, it is, to give greatness and strength unto any.

text.NRSV: And now, our God, we give thanks to you and praise your glorious name.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O our God, we are giving thanks unto thee,—and offering praise, unto thy beautiful Name.

text.NRSV: “But who am I, and what is my people, that we should be able to make this freewill offering? For all things come from you, and of your own have we given you.
text.Rotherham: And yet, who am, I, and who are my people, that we should be able to offer willingly like this? for, from thee, is the whole, and, out of thine own hand, have we given unto thee;

text.NRSV: For we are strangers before you, and sojourners, as were all our ancestors; our days on the earth are like a shadow, and there is no hope.
text.Rotherham: for, sojourners, are we before thee, and strangers, like all our fathers,—like a shadow, are our days upon the earth, and there is no hope.

text.NRSV: O Lord our God, all this abundance that we have provided for building you a house for your holy name comes from your hand and is all your own.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh our God, all this abundance which we have prepared, to build thee a house for thy holy Name, out of thine own hand, it is, and, thine, is the whole.

text.NRSV: I know, my God, that you search the heart, and take pleasure in uprightness; in the uprightness of my heart I have freely offered all these things, and now I have seen your people, who are present here, offering freely and joyously to you.
text.Rotherham: But I know, O my God, that, thou, art proving the heart, and, with uprightness, wilt thou be pleased,—I, in the uprightness of my heart, have willingly offered all these things, now, therefore, thy people who are found here, have I seen with joy, offering willingly unto thee.

text.NRSV: O Lord, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, our ancestors, keep forever such purposes and thoughts in the hearts of your people, and direct their hearts toward you.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, our fathers, oh guard this age-abidingly, as the devised purpose of the heart of thy people,—and establish thou their heart, unto thyself.

text.NRSV: Grant to my son Solomon that with single mind he may keep your commandments, your decrees, and your statutes, performing all of them, and that he may build the temple for which I have made provision.”
text.Rotherham: Also, unto Solomon my son, give thou an undivided heart, to keep thy commandments, thy testimonies, and thy statutes,—to do the whole, and to build the palace for which I have prepared.

text.NRSV: Then David said to the whole assembly, “Bless the Lord your God.” And all the assembly blessed the Lord, the God of their ancestors, and bowed their heads and bent low before the Lord and the king.
text.Rotherham: Then said David, unto all the convocation, Bless, I beseech you, Yahweh your God. So all the convocation blessed Yahweh, the God of their fathers, and did bend their heads and bow themselves down unto Yahweh, and unto the king;

text.NRSV: On the next day they offered sacrifices and burnt offerings to the Lord, a thousand bulls, a thousand rams, and a thousand lambs, with their drink offerings, and sacrifices in abundance for all Israel.
text.Rotherham: and sacrificed unto Yahweh sacrifices, and caused to go up ascending-offerings unto Yahweh, on the morrow of that day, a thousand bullocks, a thousand rams, a thousand young sheep, and the drink-offerings thereof,—and sacrifices in abundance, for all Israel.

text.NRSV: They ate and drank before the Lord on that day with great joy. They made Solomon son of David king a second time; they anointed him as the Lord’s prince, and Zadok as priest.
text.Rotherham: And so they did eat and drink before Yahweh on that day, with great joy,—and they, the second time, made Solomon son of David king, and anointed him unto Yahweh to be chief ruler, and Zadok to be priest.

text.NRSV: Then Solomon sat on the throne of the Lord as king instead of his father David; he prospered, and all Israel obeyed him.
text.Rotherham: So Solomon took his seat upon the throne of Yahweh, as king, instead of David his father, and prospered,—and all Israel, hearkened unto him.

text.NRSV: All the leaders and the mighty warriors, and also all the sons of King David, pledged their allegiance to King Solomon.
text.Rotherham: And, all the rulers, and the heroes, yea moreover all the sons of King David, gave a hand, under Solomon the king.

text.NRSV: The Lord highly exalted Solomon in the sight of all Israel, and bestowed upon him such royal majesty as had not been on any king before him in Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh magnified Solomon, exceedingly, before the eyes of all Israel,—and gave unto him a royal majesty, that had not been on any king before him, over Israel.

text.NRSV: Thus David son of Jesse reigned over all Israel.
text.Rotherham: Thus, David son of Jesse, reigned over all Israel;

text.NRSV: The period that he reigned over Israel was forty years; he reigned seven years in Hebron, and thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: and, the days that he reigned over Israel, were forty years,—in Hebron reigned he seven years, and in Jerusalem, reigned he, thirty-three.

text.NRSV: He died in a good old age, full of days, riches, and honor; and his son Solomon succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And he died in a good old age, satisfied with days, riches and honour,—and Solomon his son reigned in his stead.

text.NRSV: Now the acts of King David, from first to last, are written in the Annals of the Prophet Samuel, and in the Annals of the Prophet Nathan, and in the Annals of the Prophet Gad,
text.Rotherham: Now, the acts of David the king, first and last, behold them! written in the records of Samuel the seer, and in the records of Nathan the prophet, and in the records of Gad the seer:

text.NRSV: with accounts of all his rule and his might and of the events that befell him and Israel and all the kingdoms of the earth.
text.Rotherham: with all his reign, and his might,—and the times which passed over him, and over Israel, and over all the kingdoms of the lands.
